3.2.6.9 Configuring Intermittent/Toggling Rules
After the intermittent/toggling rule is set, the alarms that last for a short period of
time or are frequently reported can be discarded or displayed on the Masked
Alarms page to reduce interference caused by repetitive alarms.
Prerequisites
You have the Set Intermittent/Toggling Rules permission.
Context

– Original alarm: The alarms that meet the conditions in the intermittent/
toggling rules are original alarms.
– Intermittent policy: When the interval between alarm generation and
alarm clearance is less than a specific period, the alarm is considered as
an intermittent alarm. The period is called intermittent period.
– Toggling policy: When the number of times that an alarm (with the same
alarm ID) is reported by the same alarm source in a specified period
reaches the trigger condition, the toggling handling is started. You can set
the action to generate a toggling alarm or avalanche alarm, or redefine
the original alarm severity. When the number of times that the alarm is
reported by the same alarm source in the specified period reaches the
termination condition, the toggling handling is terminated. After the
toggling ends, the generated toggling or avalanche alarm is
automatically cleared. If the original alarms are not cleared, the last
original alarm is reported.
● If an alarm is generated more than 30 minutes before it arrives at the system,
it does not match any intermittent/toggling rule. For example, if a device is
disconnected or the system restarts for more than 30 minutes, alarms
automatically or manually synchronized during this period do not match any
intermittent/toggling rule once the device is reconnected or the system
restarts.
● A maximum of 1,000 intermittent/toggling rules can be created.
● After a rule is created, with Alarm sources set to All alarm sources or Event
sources set to All event sources, any user with the permission can delete or
modify the rule.
● By default, rules are sorted by state (enabled or disabled) and enabled rules
are displayed preferentially. The rules in the same state are sorted by last
update time in descending order.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Alarms Management > Alarm Settings from the
main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Intermittent/Toggling Rules.
Step 3 On the Intermittent/Toggling Rules page, click Create.
Step 4 In the Basic Information area, set the rule name, description, and whether to
enable the rule.
Step 5 In the Conditions area, set the alarm severities, alarms, and alarm sources for the
rule to take effect. Set advanced conditions to filter the alarms for the rule to take
effect based on alarm parameters.
Step 6 In the Time Filter area, set the time and period for the rule to take effect as
required.
Step 7 In the Analysis Type area, select Intermittent analysis, Toggling analysis, or
both as required.

intermittent period is set to 30 and the policy action is set to Discard, the
alarm is discarded when the interval between the generation and clearance of
the alarm is less than 30 seconds.
– Period: If the interval between the alarm generation time and alarm
clearance time is less than the set period, the alarm meets the
intermittent policy.
– Action: The action for handling the alarm that meets the intermittent
policy can be set to Discard or Display on Masked Alarms.
NO TE
Before alarms are reported, alarm management checks whether the alarms meet any
intermittent policy within a specified period. If not, the reporting of the original alarms
is delayed. The delay duration depends on the configured period.
Example: In the rule, the intermittent period is set to 10 seconds. If the interval
between the generation and clearance of an alarm is longer than 10 seconds, the
alarm does not match the intermittent policy. In this case, the original alarms are
reported with a delay of 10 seconds.
● Toggling Policy: Sets the toggling trigger condition, termination condition,
and handling policy. The differences between toggling alarms and avalanche
alarms are as follows:
– Toggling alarm: A new alarm is generated. The alarm is identified as a
toggling alarm. The alarm information is the same as that of an original
alarm. You can set Toggling Identifier in the Advanced settings area on
the filter panel to filter the alarms.
– Avalanche alarm: ALM-132 Avalanche alarm is generated.
NO TE
The alarm toggling rule is triggered only when the number of reporting times of an
alarm reaches the specified number in the specified period. After the toggling ends,
the toggling alarm is automatically cleared and the last original alarm is reported with
a delay. This delay corresponds to the interval between the time when the alarm is
generated and the time when the toggling termination condition is met.
Example: In the rule, Period and Generated alarms in Trigger condition are set to 60
seconds and 2 respectively, Period and Generated alarms in Termination condition
are set to 60 seconds and 0 respectively, and Handling policy is set to Generate a
toggling alarm and discard original alarms generated after toggling.
– If the same alarm is reported twice in the first 60 seconds, the first two original
alarms are reported normally, and the third alarm enters the toggling period and a
toggling alarm is generated.
– If the alarm is not reported within the next 60 seconds, the toggling ends and the
toggling alarm is automatically cleared. If the third original alarm is not cleared, it
is reported with a delay of 60 seconds.
Step 9 In the Other area, set the priority of the rule.
Step 10 Click OK.
----End
Related Tasks
● Deleting a rule: You can select a redundant rule from the rule list and click

from the rule list and click Disable. To use a disabled rule, select the rule and
click Enable.
● Migrating rules: You can click Export on the rule page of the source system to
export rules. Then, click Import in the target system to import the rule file
exported from the source system to the target system.
● Backing up, collecting statistics on, and reviewing rules, and modifying rules
in batches: You can click Export on the rule page to export rules and back up,
review, and collect statistics on the rules. If you need to modify rules in
batches, you can modify the rules in batches in the exported rule file. Then,
click Import on the rule page to import the modified rule file.
Task Example
Scenario
A minor alarm M lasts for a short period of time or is reported frequently. The
cause may be that an NE or service switches between the normal state and the
abnormal state repeatedly. To reduce the interference of such alarms, you can set
an intermittent/toggling rule to handle the alarms using the following policies:
● Intermittent policy: If alarm M is reported and cleared within 30 seconds, this
alarm is discarded.
● Toggling policy: If the number of times that alarm M is generated within 60
seconds is greater than 2, the toggling handling is triggered. If the number of
times that alarm M is generated within 60 seconds is less than or equal to 0,
the toggling handling is terminated.
Procedure
1. On the Intermittent/Toggling Rules page, click Create.
2. In the Basic Information area, enter the rule name.
3. In the Conditions area, select Designated alarms, and click Add Alarm. In
the dialog box that is displayed, select alarm M, which indicates that the
intermittent/toggling rule is performed on alarm M, and click OK after alarm
M is displayed in the Selected area.
4. In the Analysis Type area, select Intermittent analysis and Toggling
analysis.
5. In the Intermittent Policy and Toggling Policy areas, configure the policies.
– Intermittent Policy: Set Period (s)(T) to 30 and Action to Discard.
– Toggling Policy: Set Period to 60 and Generated alarms to 2 for Trigger
condition, Period to 60 and Generated alarms to 0 for Termination
condition, and Handling policy to Generate a toggling alarm and
discard original alarms generated after toggling.
6. Click OK.
Result
If alarm M is cleared within 30 seconds after being reported, it is discarded. If the
M alarm is reported frequently, the original alarms generated after the toggling

If alarms or events are reported repeatedly within a specified period, you can
configure rules to aggregate them into one, thereby reducing interference. When
monitoring or viewing alarms, you can select Aggregated alarms to filter out
original alarms that have been aggregated and focus only on the alarms you need
to handle.
Prerequisites
You have the Set Aggregation Rules permission.
Context
● A rule is valid only to the alarms or events reported after the rule is enabled.
● In an aggregation rule, when the number of times that the same alarm or
event (with the same alarm or event ID) is reported by NEs in a specified
period meets the trigger condition, an aggregation is triggered, a new
aggregated alarm is generated, and the alarms that meet all the conditions of
the alarm aggregation rule are the original alarms. When an aggregated
alarm is generated based on the rule, the correlative alarms are marked as
original alarms. To view all original alarms, you can select Original alarms
for Associated alarm ID when setting the filter criteria for current alarms.
● If an aggregated alarm generated based on the alarm aggregation rule is
cleared, its correlative original alarms are also cleared after the aggregation
task is complete. If all original alarms are cleared, their correlative aggregated
alarms are also periodically cleared after the aggregation task is complete. To
handle an aggregated alarm, handle the faults corresponding to the original
alarms one by one.
● A maximum of 1,000 custom aggregation rules can be created.
● After a rule is created, with Alarm sources set to All alarm sources or Event
sources set to All event sources, any user with the permission can delete or
modify the rule.
● By default, rules are sorted by state (enabled or disabled) and enabled rules
are displayed preferentially. The rules in the same state are sorted by last
update time in descending order.
● If the interval between the time when an alarm is generated and the time
when the alarm arrives at the system is longer than 5 minutes, the alarm
does not match the aggregation rule even if the alarm meets the conditions.
● In an event aggregation rule, only the Generate an aggregated alarm and
Generate an aggregated alarm and discard all original events options are
provided for Aggregation action. The information about the aggregated
alarm generated based on the event aggregation rule is the same as that of
an original event. The original events of the aggregated alarm cannot be
viewed.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Alarms Management > Alarm Settings from the

Step 3 Create an aggregation rule.
1. On the Aggregation Rules page, click the tab, click Create, and choose the
type of the rule to be created.
2. In the Basic Information area, set the rule name, description, and whether to
enable the rule.
3. In the Conditions area, set the alarm or event name, alarm or event source,
and advanced conditions. If alarms or events reported by the selected alarm
or event source meet the configured advanced conditions, aggregation
analysis is performed.
4. In the Aggregation Parameters and Aggregation Action areas, set the
aggregation parameters and aggregation action. For details about the
parameters, see Table 3-50.
5. Click OK.
----End
Related Tasks
● Deleting a rule: You can select a redundant rule from the rule list and click
Delete to reduce the rule maintenance workload.
● Enabling/disabling a rule: You can select a rule that is not used temporarily
from the rule list and click Disable. To use a disabled rule, select the rule and
click Enable.
● Migrating rules: You can click Export on the rule page of the source system to
export rules. Then, click Import in the target system to import the rule file
exported from the source system to the target system.
● Backing up, collecting statistics on, and reviewing rules, and modifying rules
in batches: You can click Export on the rule page to export rules and back up,
review, and collect statistics on the rules. If you need to modify rules in
batches, you can modify the rules in batches in the exported rule file. Then,
click Import on the rule page to import the modified rule file.

The following uses the parameters for creating an alarm rule as an example, and
they are similar to those for creating an event rule.

3.2.6.11 Configuring Correlation Rules
If an alarm causes other alarms, you can use the correlation rule to define the
correlation between alarms. When O&M maintenance personnel monitor or view
alarms, they can set a correlation rule to filter out correlative alarms and focus
only on root alarms.
Prerequisites
You have the following operation permissions: Set Correlation Rules.
Context
● A rule is valid only to the alarms reported after the rule is enabled.
● After the rule is enabled, the NetEco needs to analyze alarm relationships,
which affects the time for reporting alarms to the NetEco.

modified or deleted.
● If the interval between the time when an alarm is generated and the time
when the alarm arrives at the NMS is longer than 30 minutes, the alarm does
not match the correlation rule even if the alarm meets the conditions.
● By default, the rules are sorted by the enabled and disabled states, and the
rules in the same state are sorted by update time in descending order.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Alarms Management > Alarm Settings from the
main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Correlation Rules.
Step 3 On the Correlation Rules page, click the Default tab. On the tab page that is
displayed, check whether a rule meeting requirements exists.
● If yes, go to Step 4.
● If no, go to Step 5.
Step 4 On the Default tab page, enable or disable rules as required.
Step 5 Create a custom correlation rule.
1. On the Correlation Rules page, click Custom and then click Create.
2. In the Basic Information area, set the rule name, description, and whether to
enable the rule.
3. In the Alarms area, select root alarms and correlative alarms and set actions.
– Root alarms: Alarm that causes multiple correlative alarms.
– Correlative alarms: Alarm that is generated due to the root alarm.
4. In the Conditions area, click Create to create a correlation rule between the
root alarm and the correlative alarms.
5. In the Other area, set the generation interval between the root alarms and
their correlative alarms and the priority of the rule.
A correlation is true only if the generation interval between the root alarm
and the correlative alarm is shorter than or equal to the specified period.
6. Click OK.
----End
Related Tasks
● Deleting a rule: You can select a redundant rule from the rule list and click
Delete to reduce the rule maintenance workload.
● Enabling/disabling a rule: You can select a rule that is not used temporarily
from the rule list and click Disable. To use a disabled rule, select the rule and
click Enable.
● Migrating rules: You can click Export on the rule page of the source system to
export rules. Then, click Import in the target system to import the rule file

in batches: You can click Export on the rule page to export rules and back up,
review, and collect statistics on the rules. If you need to modify rules in
batches, you can modify the rules in batches in the exported rule file. Then,
click Import on the rule page to import the modified rule file.
Task Example
Scenario
When the connection of an NE is faulty, the NE reports alarm M periodically. At
the same time, a large number of connection interruption alarms N are generated
by this NE or other NEs because of the connection interruption. The type of alarm
N is the same as that of alarm M. In this case, you only need to handle alarm M
instead of alarm N. By setting a correlation rule, you can set the alarm N
generated later than the alarm M to a correlative alarm.
Procedure
1. On the Correlation Rules page, click Custom and then click Create.
2. In the Basic Information area, enter the rule name.
3. In the Alarms area, set Root alarms to alarm M and Correlative alarms to
alarm N.
4. In the Conditions area, click Create. Set the following condition: The
occurrence time of alarm N is later than the occurrence time of alarm M.
Click OK.
5. Click OK.
Result
When the occurrence time of alarm N is later than that of alarm M, alarm M is
the root alarm of alarm N. In this case, choose Current Alarms from the main
menu and open the Filter tab page. In the Advanced settings area, click
Associated Alarm ID, and then set Operator to is in and Value to Normal
alarms and Root alarms. After the query is started, the correlative alarm N is not
displayed in the current alarm list. You only need to handle the root alarm M.

3.2.6.12 Configuring Auto Acknowledgement Rules
When the number of current alarms reaches the threshold, the system processes
the full current alarm cache and moves current alarms to the historical alarm list.
To prevent the important alarms in the full current alarm list from being
processed, the system automatically acknowledges the current alarms in the
cleared status based on an acknowledgement rule specified by users and converts
them to historical alarms.
Prerequisites
You have the Set Auto Acknowledgement Rules permission.
Context
● Auto acknowledgement rules take effect only for unacknowledged and
cleared alarms. Alarms cleared before immediate acknowledgement is
enabled are not affected.
● A maximum of 1,000 auto acknowledgement rules are supported in the
system. Four preset rules exist in the system and a maximum of 996 rules can
be created.
● If an alarm complies with auto acknowledgement rules in both the delayed
and immediate modes, the system acknowledges the alarm in immediate
mode.
● By default, rules are sorted by state (enabled or disabled) and enabled rules
are displayed preferentially. The rules in the same state are sorted by last
update time in descending order.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Alarms Management > Alarm Settings from the
main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Auto Acknowledgement Rules.
Step 3 In the auto acknowledgement rule list, the first four rules are preset for
automatically acknowledging alarms by severity. Check whether the rules already

● If no, go to Step 5.
Step 4 Enable an auto acknowledgement rule by alarm severity. For example, when the
auto acknowledgement rule for major alarms is enabled, the cleared but
unacknowledged major alarms will be acknowledged automatically.
In the auto acknowledgement rule list, the first four rules are preset. Only users
who can manage all resources can enable or disable these rules.
1. Select the auto acknowledgement rule required based on the severity and
click Enable.
2. In the Mode column, view the acknowledgement mode of the rule.
– Immediate: indicates that alarms will be automatically acknowledged
immediately after being cleared.
– Delayed: indicates that alarms will be automatically acknowledged based
on the Duration setting in a rule or the settings in Change Delay after
being cleared. When days is selected for Duration in the rule, the system
automatically acknowledges related alarms based on Execute at in
Change Delay above the rule list. If Duration is not set in the rule, the
system automatically acknowledges related alarms based on the settings
in Change Delay.
Step 5 Create a custom auto acknowledgement rule.
1. On the Auto Acknowledgement Rules page, click Create.
2. In the Basic Information area, set the rule name, description, and whether to
enable the rule.
3. In the Conditions area, set the alarm severities, alarms, and alarm sources for
the rule to take effect. Set advanced conditions to filter the alarms for the
rule to take effect based on alarm parameters.
NO TE
All alarm sources is available only for users whose managed objects are All Objects.
4. In the Other area, set the acknowledgement mode.
5. Click OK.
Step 6 On the Auto Acknowledgement Rules page, click Change Delay and set the
parameters. If the acknowledgement mode is set to Delay and Duration is not set
in the rule, the system automatically acknowledges alarms based on the settings
in Change Delay. If the acknowledgement mode is set to Delay and days is
selected for Duration in the rule, the system automatically acknowledges related
alarms based on Execute at in Change Delay.
1. Click Change Delay and set Execute at and Duration.
2. Click Apply.
----End
3.2.6.13 Configuring Identification Rules
After an identification rule is set, the system automatically sets alarms that match

status of alarms generated during commissioning to Maintenance so that O&M
personnel do not need to spend time on these alarms.
Prerequisites
You have the Set Identification Rules permission.
Context
● A rule is valid only to the alarms reported after the rule is enabled.
● Before an identification rule takes effect, Validity is Valid and Maintenance
Status is NORMAL by default. NORMAL corresponds to the alarm state
Normal.
● A maximum of 1,000 identification rules can be created.
● By default, rules are sorted by state (enabled or disabled) and enabled rules
are displayed preferentially. The rules in the same state are sorted by last
update time in descending order.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Alarms Management > Alarm Settings from the
main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Identification Rules.
Step 3 On the Identification Rules page, click Create.
Step 4 In the Basic Information area, set the rule name, description, and whether to
enable the rule.
Step 5 In the Conditions area, set the alarm severities, alarms, and alarm sources for the
rule to take effect. Set advanced conditions to filter the alarms for the rule to take
effect based on alarm parameters.
NO TE
All alarm sources is available only for users who can manage all resources.
Step 6 In the Time Filter area, set the time and period for the rule to take effect as
required.
Step 7 In the Action area, select the status of alarms you want to identify.
Step 8 Click OK.
----End

Delete to reduce the rule maintenance workload.
● Enabling/disabling a rule: You can select a rule that is not used temporarily
from the rule list and click Disable. To use a disabled rule, select the rule and

3.2.6.15 Configuring Unhandled Alarm Severity Rules
If some alarms that require special attention are not handled for a long time (that
is, the alarms are not cleared or acknowledged for a long time), you need to raise
the alarm severity. If some alarms that do not require special attention are not
handled for a long time, you can lower the alarm severity. You can create
unhandled alarm severity rules to change the severity of alarms that are not
handled within a specified period.
Context
● After a rule is enabled, it takes effect for the alarms reported before and after
the rule is enabled.
● After the alarm severity is changed, the alarm severity displayed in the alarm
list is the new one. However, when you select an alarm for other rules, the
alarm severity displayed is still the original one.
● A maximum of 2,000 unhandled alarm severity rules can be created.
● By default, rules are sorted by state (enabled or disabled) and enabled rules
are displayed preferentially. The rules in the same state are sorted by last

Step 1 Choose Device Management > Alarms Management > Alarm Settings from the
main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Unhandled Alarm Severity Rules.
Step 3 On the Unhandled Alarm Severity Rules page, click Create.
Step 4 In the Basic Information area, set the rule name, description, and whether to
enable the rule.
Step 5 In the Conditions area, set the alarm name, alarm ID, group name, and alarm
status.
Step 6 In the Handling Policy area, set Trigger condition and Action.
Step 7 In the Other area, set the priority of the rule.
Step 8 Click OK.
----End
Related Tasks
● Deleting a rule: You can select a redundant rule from the rule list and click
Delete to reduce the rule maintenance workload.
● Enabling/disabling a rule: You can select a rule that is not used temporarily
from the rule list and click Disable. To use a disabled rule, select the rule and
click Enable.
3.2.6.16 Configuring Notification Rules
You cannot browse alarms or events on the alarm management page when you
are out of office, for example, during off-work hours or business trips. You can set
notification rules to receive notifications about concerned alarms or events by
SMS message or email. In this way, you can handle alarms or events in a timely
manner.
Prerequisites
● You have completed the notification configuration. The navigation path is as
follows: Choose System > System Settings > Notifications from the main
menu.
● You have the Set Notification Rules permission.
Context
● Notifications by email and SMS message are sent through third-party servers
and devices. Therefore, the sending capabilities (such as the delay in receiving
notifications by email) vary with the third-party servers, devices, or networks.
For details about the notification sending capabilities, see "Notification
Process" in Online Help.

are displayed preferentially. The rules in the same state are sorted by last
update time in descending order.
● A maximum of 1,000 notification rules can be created.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Alarms Management > Alarm Settings from the
main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Notification Rules.
Step 3 On the Notification Rules page, click Create and choose Alarm Notification
Rules or Event Notification Rules.
Step 4 In the Basic Information area, set the rule name, description, and whether to
enable the rule.
Step 5 In the Conditions area, set the severities, alarm status, alarms or events, alarm or
event sources, and advanced conditions (which are used to filter alarms or events
based on the alarm or event parameters).
NO TE
All alarm sources or All event sources is available only for the users who can manage all
resources.
Step 6 In the Time Filter area, set the time and period for the rule to take effect as
required.
Step 7 Set the notification method and content, delay, and recipient time zone. When the
alarms meeting the rule are reported, the system sends notifications to recipients
of specified groups by email or SMS message. Table 3-53 describes the
parameters for creating a notification rule.
NO TE
To send notifications to relevant personnel, you need to enter their personal data, such as
mobile numbers and email addresses. You are obligated to take considerable measures, in
compliance with the laws of the countries or regions concerned and the user privacy
policies of your company, to ensure that users' personal data is fully protected.
Step 8 Click OK.
----End

● Deleting a rule: You can select a redundant rule from the rule list and click
Delete to reduce the rule maintenance workload.
● Enabling/disabling a rule: You can select a rule that is not used temporarily
from the rule list and click Disable. To use a disabled rule, select the rule and
click Enable.
● Migrating rules: You can click Export on the rule page of the source system to

3.2.6.17 Configuring Notification Templates
When the default alarm notification templates do not meet your requirements,
you can create an alarm notification template and use it when configuring
notification rules. The system fills the alarm information into an email or SMS
message based on the alarm notification template, and sends the notification to
related users.
Prerequisites
You have the Set Notification Rules permission.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Alarms Management > Alarm Settings from the
main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Alarm Notification Templates.
Step 3 On the Alarm Notification Templates page, click Create.

the notification method, and set the important information of the notification,
such as, severity, name, and occurrence time.
● If you select New alarms + new cleared alarms for Subtitle, a notification
email can contain multiple alarms or events. The maximum size of an email is
2 MB. If the size of an email exceeds the maximum size, the email is split into
multiple emails. When you create a notification rule, an email can contain a
maximum of 200 alarms or events. If Delay is set in the rule, the number of
alarms in an email is not limited to 200.
● If you deselect New alarms + new cleared alarms for Subtitle, only one
alarm or event is sent in a notification email.
NO TE
The contents in black in the template are fixed fields displayed in emails or SMS messages.
You can modify them as required. The characters in blue show alarm information and vary
depending on alarms. You can add them by selecting the labels below rather than manually
editing them.
The meaning of Occurred varies in the following scenarios:
● Generally, when an alarm is reported, this parameter refers to the reporting time,
corresponding to the First Occurred field on the Current Alarms page.
● When a merged alarm is reported, this parameter refers to the reporting time of the
merged alarm, corresponding to the Last Occurred field on the Current Alarms page.
● When an alarm clearance notification is sent, this parameter refers to the time when the
alarm is generated, corresponding to the Last Occurred field on the Alarm Logs page.
Step 6 Click OK.
----End
3.2.6.18 Managing Handling Experience
After handling an alarm, record the handling experience to the handling
experience database for future reference or guidance.
Prerequisites
You have the Record Experience permission.
Context
● The handling experience on the Alarm Settings > Experience page is the
same as that in the alarm details.
● A maximum of 10,000 experience records can be created.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Alarms Management > Alarm Settings from the
main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Experience.
Step 3 On the Experience page, click Create.
Step 4 Select the alarm whose handling experience needs to be created. If the page

creating handling experience.
Step 5 Enter experience.
Step 6 Click OK.
----End
Related Tasks
You can import or export alarm handling experience for statistics, review, and
migration. On the Experience page, click Export or Import to export or import
experience records.
Parameter Description
Table 3-54 Parameters for creating an experience record
Parameter Description
Alarm name Name of an alarm.
Alarm ID ID of the alarm.
Group name Alarm group ID, which is assigned by
NEs or devices that report alarms.
3.2.6.19 Setting the Current Alarm Threshold Warning
When the number of current alarms reaches the maximum capacity, the system
moves some current alarms to the historical-alarm list based on the full-cache
processing rule. To prevent important alarms from being converted into historical
alarms, you can set a threshold warning for current alarms. When the number of
current alarms reaches a specified threshold, an alarm is reported to prompt you
to handle the current alarms.
Prerequisites
Only users attached to the Administrators role can set the current alarm
threshold warning.
Context
To prevent excessive current alarms from affecting system performance, alarm
management provides the processing rules of the full current alarm cache.
● Start full current alarm cache processing: When the number of current alarms
in the database reaches the upper limit, the system starts full current alarm
cache processing according to the following rules and converts some alarms
into historical alarms:
a. Alarms are converted into historical alarms in the following sequence:

normal state, and alarms that do not match any merging rules and are in
the normal state.
b. Alarms in the same maintenance state and merging state are converted
into historical alarms in the following sequence: cleared and
acknowledged alarms, cleared and unacknowledged alarms, and
acknowledged and uncleared ADMC alarms.
c. Unacknowledged and uncleared alarms are converted into historical
alarms in sequence based on the preceding rules.
d. For the alarms that meet the preceding conditions, the earlier alarms are
converted into historical alarms first.
e. In a distributed system, when the NE alarms are reported to different
nodes and the processing of the full current alarm cache is triggered, the
alarms are converted into historical alarms in descending order of the
node alarm quantity. The more alarms are reported to a node, the higher
the priority of the node is. For example, if an NE is connected to node A
and node B, and the alarm quantity on node A is more than node B, the
processing of the full current alarm cache is performed only on node A.
● Stop full current alarm cache processing:
– If the number of alarms to be processed at a time exceeds 30,000, the
system processes 30,000 alarms. After the processing is complete, the
system stops the full current alarm cache processing.
– If the number of alarms to be processed at a time is less than or equal to
30,000, the full current alarm cache processing is stopped when the
alarm quantity falls to 90% of the capacity upper limit.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Alarms Management > Alarm Settings from the
main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Current Alarm Threshold Warning.
Step 3 Select whether to send an alarm when the number of current alarms reaches the
threshold and set the thresholds for generating and clearing alarms.
● Generation threshold: When the ratio of the number of current alarms to
the maximum capacity is greater than or equal to the specified threshold, the
alarm "ALM-157 The Number of Current Alarms Reaches the Threshold" is
generated.
● Clearance threshold: When the ratio of the number of current alarms to the
maximum capacity is less than the specified threshold, the alarm "ALM-157
The Number of Current Alarms Reaches the Threshold" is cleared. The
clearance threshold cannot be higher than the generation threshold.
Step 4 Click Apply.
----End

This section describes how to customize dry contact alarms. You can customize a
dry contact alarm, bind it with an NE, and record the dry contact alarms reported
by the NE in the NetEco alarm list to centrally manage these alarms.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Set Dry Contact Rules.
Context
Dry contact is a type of passive switch that has two states: open and closed. If a
device is connected to the site energy or environment device controller using a dry
contact, you can create a dry contact alarm on the NetEco and bind the alarm
with the device. After that, the controller can report alarms indicating the dry
contact status to the NetEco.
NO TE
Only NEs whose versions are PowerCube are supported.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Alarm Management > Alarm Settings.
Step 2 In the displayed Alarm Settings window, choose Dry Contact Alarm in the left
pane.
Step 3 On the Dry Contact Alarm page, click Create.
Step 4 In the displayed Create Alarm Definition dialog box, set the parameters for a dry
contact alarm.
Table 3-55 lists the parameters.
Table 3-55 Parameters for a dry contact alarm
Parameter Description
Alarm Name Name of a dry contact alarm.
ID ID of a dry contact alarm for automatic
identification, which uniquely identifies a dry
contact alarm.
NOTE
The alarm ID ranges from 65200 to 65300. Therefore,
you can customize a maximum of 100 alarms.
Severity Critical A critical alarm indicates that a service-affecting
fault occurs and an immediate corrective action is
required.
Major A major alarm indicates that a service-affecting
fault occurs. If the fault is not rectified

Parameter Description
Minor A minor alarm indicates a fault that currently
does not affect service occurs, but the fault needs
to be rectified to prevent a more serious fault.
Warning A warning alarm indicates that a potential or
impending service-affecting fault is detected
before any impact is found.
Type Communicatio Alarms for faults in the signaling system.
ns alarm

alarm such as network cable disconnection and network
equipment fault.

error alarm certain processing, such as database exception
and abnormal NetEco service stop.
Quality of Alarms for QoS-related faults, such as high CPU
service alarm and memory usage.
Environmental Alarms for faults related to the environment in
alarm the equipment room, such as the power fault and
excessively-high CPU temperature.
Integrity One type of security alarm, which indicates that
violation information may be modified, inserted, or deleted
illegally.
Operational One type of security alarm, which indicates that
violation services cannot run properly due to service
unavailability, faults, or incorrect service
invocation.
Physical One type of security alarm, which indicates that
violation physical resources are damaged by suspicious
attacks.

service or the security service or mechanism suffers hacker
mechanism attacks.
violation

be avoided or are forbidden.

3.2.6.21 Redefine Alarm
To quickly search for and handle key device alarms to which you pay special
attention, you can redefine these alarms.
Prerequisites

● A Device Type Redefinition alarm rule applies to all device instances of the
device type. If a Device Instance Redefinition alarm rule is also configured,
the alarms of the device instance are reported based on the Device Instance
Redefinition rule.
● After a Device Instance Redefinition alarm is configured on the NetEco, the
name and severity of the redefined alarm will be synchronized to the devices.
Procedure
1. Choose Device Management > Alarm Management > Alarm Settings.
2. In the navigation pane, choose Redefine Alarm.

3.2.6.22 Threshold Alarm
This section describes the management of thresholds. When managing
components, you can customize threshold alarms for some key counters.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Set Threshold Rules.
Context
● You can set performance counter thresholds for certain components of NEs.
● The thresholds can be set based on the alarm severity, including Critical,
Major, Minor, and Warning.
● After the threshold rule is set, the rule is enabled by default.
● After the file is exported, you can edit rules locally. Multiple rules correspond
to one alarm name. The rule upper or lower thresholds are displayed in one
rule. If one rule corresponds to one alarm name, you are advised to copy and
insert the current signal data. The rule upper or lower thresholds are

Step 1 Choose Device Management > Alarm Management > Alarm Settings.
Step 2 In the navigation tree on the left, click the threshold alarm.
Step 3 In the device tree and select a target device, choose Real-Time Signals, Statistics
Signals or Custom Signals.

3.3.1.2 Synchronizing Device Configuration Data
When device configuration data changes, you can use the synchronization
function of the NetEco Smart Energy to synchronize the data on the NetEco Smart
Energy NMS to the NetEco Smart Energy.
Context
After a device is created on the NetEco, the preset parameters are automatically
delivered from the NetEco to the device if the network connection is normal. In
this case, the configuration data on the NetEco is the same as that of the device.
The configuration data of the device, however, may be directly modified the device
during sequent maintenance, which results in the inconsistency of configuration
data between the device and the NetEco. To ensure data consistency, the NetEco
provides both automatic synchronization and manual synchronization. Table 3-59
lists the two synchronization modes.
Table 3-59 Synchronization modes and mechanisms of the configuration data of a
device
Synchronization Synchronization Advantage and
Mode Mechanism Disadvantage
Auto Periodic The NetEco initiates data The synchronization time
matic automatic of device synchronization is fixed, which ensures
synch synchronizati once every five days. data synchronization to
roniz on some extent, but the
ation synchronization period is
long.

Synchronization Synchronization Advantage and
Mode Mechanism Disadvantage
Triggered If the configuration data of The NetEco may fail to
automatic a device is modified or a receive the synchronization
synchronizati component is added or notification due to
on deleted, the device notifies network problems and
the NetEco of the cannot initiate data
modification. The NetEco synchronization in time.
initiates data
synchronization upon
receiving the notification.
Manual Data synchronization is Data can be synchronized
synchronization initiated in real time. in time.
The following part describes how to manually synchronize the configuration data
of a device.
Prerequisites
● You have the operation rights for Planning Configuration.
● The device has been properly connected.
Procedure
1. Choose Device Management > Configuration > Planning Configuration.
2. In the Planning Configuration window, click .
3. Select the devices,click OK, synchronize configuration data.
If the synchronization fails, contact technical support.
3.3.1.3 Planning Configuration Interface Reference
This describes the parameters in the planning configuration window.
3.3.1.3.1 Basic Parameters of a Controller
This section describes the basic parameters that need to be set for a controller
created on the NetEco client.

Parameter Description
ESN ESN of the controller. After a controller is
created successfully, the system automatically
connects to the corresponding site based on the
ESN. If the connection succeeds, the controller
is displayed as in the navigation tree. If the
connection fails, the controller is displayed as
in the navigation tree.
SSL Status Indicates whether controllers and the NetEco
are connected using SSL.
NOTE
A non-SSL connection has security risks. You are
advised to use a secure SSL connection.
Scenario Power supply system type of a controller.
Password For details, see 3.3.1.3.4 Changing an
Authentication Password of a Device.

domain created on the NetEco client.

mandatory.

3.3.1.3.3 Basic Parameters of the Devices
This section describes the basic parameters that need to be set for devices created
on the NetEco client.
They include but are not limited to the following parameters. Configure the
parameters as required. The parameter marked with an asterisk (*) is mandatory.
Table 3-65 Device parameter description
Attribute Parameter Description
Managem Parent Management domain of the device.
ent Info Object
Type Device type.
Name Name of the device.
Media NodeId of the med (Mediation) layer.
Gateway
Protocol Protocol type of the device.
Type

Attribute Parameter Description
IP Address It must be an IPv4 address, for example, X.X.X.X. X
is a number ranging from 0 to 255. In addition,
0.0.0.0 is not allowed.
● When the device protocol is MODBUS_TCP, set
the IP address of the device.
● When the device protocol is MODBUS_RTU, set
the IP address of the data collector to which the
device connects.
● When the device protocol is SNMP(Simple
Network Management Protocol), set the IP
address of the device.
● When the device protocol is CInterface, set the
IP address of the device.
Port The value is an integer ranging from 0 to 65535.
● When the device protocol is MODBUS_TCP, set
the corresponding port of the device. The port is
502 by default.
● When the device protocol is MODBUS_RTU, set
the port number of collector to which the device
connect.
● When the device protocol is SNMP, set the
corresponding port of the device. The port is 161
by default.
● When the device protocol is CInterface, set the
corresponding port of the device.
Device ● When the device protocol is MODBUS_TCP or
Address MODBUS_RTU, set the address of the device.
● When the device protocol is SNMP, the device
address parameter does not exist.
● When the device protocol is CInterface, the
device address parameter does not exist.
NOTE
The value combination of the IP Address, Port, and
Device Address must be unique to each device.

Attribute Parameter Description
Out Time Timeout duration for connection test (set to 3s by
default).
NOTE
● When the NetEco delivers a heartbeat message to the
device, the device considers that the device does not
respond if the duration exceeds the value of this
parameter.
● This parameter affects the detection speed when the
device is disconnected. The heartbeat is invalid only
when the time specified in Out Time is reached. In
this case, the device may be disconnected.
● This parameter affects the packet response success
rate of the device. Assume that the device takes 2
seconds to respond to the NetEco and Out Time is set
to less than 2 seconds, the device does not receive any
response.
Security Name of a user.
Name
NOTE
Specify this parameter when configuring devices that
support the SNMP V3 protocol.
Context Context name of the managed object.
Name
Authenticati Write down the authentication protocol setting on
on Protocol the device. The default selection is SHA.
Support SHA and MD5 agreement.
NOTE
Specify this parameter when configuring devices that
support the SNMP V3 protocol.
NOTICE
MD5 protocol is not secure. It is recommended that you
set protocol to SHA.
Authenticati Write down the authentication password setting on
on Password the device.
NOTE
● Specify this parameter when configuring devices that
support the SNMP V3 protocol.
● You are advised to set Authentication Password and
Privacy Password to different values.
Privacy Write down the privacy protocol setting on the
Protocol device. The default selection is AES128.
Support AES128 and CBC_DES agreement.
NOTE
Specify this parameter when configuring devices that
support the SNMP V3 protocol.
NOTICE
DES protocol is not secure. It is recommended that you set
protocol to AES.

Attribute Parameter Description
Privacy privacy password setting on the device.
Password
● Change the Privacy Password : In the Custom
Parameters area, click Modify next to
Authentication Password.
NOTE
Change user passwords periodically (for example,
every three or six months) to improve system security
and prevent security risks such as brute force cracking.
● Resetting the authentication password: After the
password of a device is changed or reset locally,
the device may fail to connect to the NetEco. In
this case, you need to reset the password on the
NetEco to ensure that the device can connect to
the NetEco properly.
In the Custom Parameters area, click Reset
next to the authentication password.
The secondary authentication password entered
during password resetting is the login password
of the system.
NOTE
● Specify this parameter when configuring devices that
support the SNMP V3 protocol.
● You are advised to set Authentication Password and
Privacy Password to different values.
● The default user name for the NetEco smart energy to
communicate with the ECC800 is emscomm and the
authentication password is Modifyme_123.
● During the access of a BIN35 device, the NetEco
changes the default authentication password to a
random password, which can be changed.
● After the password is reset, if the device connects to
the NetEco properly, the authentication passwords of
the device and NetEco are changed to the default
passwords. Otherwise, only the NetEco password is
reset.
Context Context engine ID of the managed object.
Engine ID
Read Write down the read community of the device.
Community
Modifying community per three months is advised.
NOTE
● Specify this parameter when configuring devices that
support the SNMP V1/V2 protocol.
● The NetEco Smart Energy uses SNMP V1/V2 to
communicate with devices directly connected to SNMP.
The default community is public.

Attribute Parameter Description
Write Write down the write community of the device.
Community
Modifying community per three months is advised.
NOTE
● Specify this parameter when configuring devices that
support the SNMP V1/V2 protocol.
● The NetEco Smart Energy uses SNMP V1/V2 to
communicate with devices directly connected to SNMP.
The default community is public.
Connection Connection mode of a device. You can set this
Mode parameter to Non-SSL Connection or SSL
Connection.
If you modify Connection Mode on the NetEco,
you must modify Connection Mode on the web
page of the device at the same time.
NOTE
● Connection Mode for the NetEco and device must be
the same.
● By default, Connection Mode is set to SSL
Connection for the NetEco and device.
Authenticati Reauthentication password for the NetEco and
on Password device.
● Change the Privacy Password : In the Custom
Parameters area, click Modify next to
Authentication Password.
● Resetting the authentication password:In the
Custom Parameters area, click Reset next to
the authentication password.
The secondary authentication password entered
during password resetting is the login password
of the system.
NOTE
● Change user passwords periodically (for example,
every three or six months) to improve system security
and prevent security risks such as brute force cracking.
● After the password is reset, if the device connects to
the NetEco properly, the authentication passwords of
the device and NetEco are changed to the default
passwords. Otherwise, only the NetEco password is
reset.
Camera The camera code specified in the intelligent video
Code surveillance system, has 15 digits. For details, see
Device Installation and Commissioning Guide.
NOTE
The camera code is the value of Device ID whose last
three digits are replaced with 101. For example, if the
value of Device ID is 878719000000000, the value of
Camera Code is 878719000000101.

Attribute Parameter Description
Protocol Number of Indicates that the device is disconnected for N
Info Heartbeats consecutive heartbeat failures. The default value is
3.
NOTE
This parameter affects the speed at which the NetEco
detects device disconnection.
Heartbeat Interval for the NetEco to send heartbeat detection
Period (s) messages to the device to determine whether the
device is connected.
NOTE
The default value varies according to protocols.
Sending Interval at which the device sends each packet. The
Interval (ms) default value is 600 ms.
NOTE
This parameter affects the packet processing efficiency.
Based on the response efficiency of the device, the device
can process a packet within the interval specified by
Sending Interval (ms), which is the highest efficiency.
Receiving Timeout interval for the NetEco to receive packets
Interval (ms) from the device. The default value is 1000 ms.
Collection Mode in which the NetEco collects device signals.
Mode Only the MODBUS_RTU device has the collection
mode configuration item.
Polling: Requests are sent to devices in sequence
based on the collection sequence number. After a
round of signal collection is complete, the second
round of signal collection is performed.
Routine: Signals are collected based on the value
of Period set on the Device Management >
Configuration > Signal Management page.
3.3.1.3.4 Changing an Authentication Password of a Device
This section describes how to set the password parameters for changing the device
authentication password on the NetEco client.
Prerequisites
You have created the device.
Context
The new passwords must meet the following complexity requirements. Resetting
password per three months is recommended.

– At least one lowercase letter.
– At least one uppercase letter.
– At least one of the following special characters: ` ~ ! @ # $ % ^ & * ( ) - _
= + \ | [ { } ] ; : ' " , < . > / ? and space.
● The password cannot be the user name or user name in reverse order.
Password Parameter Description
Change the login password of the controller:
● If disconnection occurs because the password of the NE controller is different
from the password on the NetEco, enter the password of the NE controller on
the NetEco again and do not deliver the password to NEs so that the NE
controller can be properly connected to the NetEco.
● When the NE controller is properly connected to the , you can change the
password of the NE controller on system and deliver the password to NEs.
After the password is changed, communication between NEs and the system
is authenticated using the new password.
NO TE
The default authentication passwords of the site controller are as follows:
● In versions earlier than SMU V500R001C10, the default user name is ectrlcomm and
the authentication password is ectrlcomm.
● For SMU V500R001C10 and later versions, the default user name is emscomm and the
authentication password is Modifyme_123.
● For SMU V500R001C11, the default user name is ectrlcomm and the authentication
password is Changeme_123.
● The default user name for theNetEco smart energy to communicate with the ECC800 is
emscomm and the authentication password is Modifyme_123.
3.3.2 Device integration
After the NetEco is installed, you need to access devices and perform routine
maintenance on the devices that have been connected to the NetEco.
3.3.2.1 Device Access
The device connection panorama shows the device connection tasks for different
protocols. Perform the tasks corresponding to the device protocol.
Prerequisites
NetEco interconnection parameters have been configured on the device side. For
details, see "NetEco Management" in SMU Site Monitoring Unit User Manual.
Context
The device connection panorama provides E2E processes and tasks in various O&M

3.3.2.2 Integration Management
O&M personnel view the devices connected to the NetEco.
Prerequisites
● You have permissions on View Configuration and Edit Configuration.
● The controller ESN has been obtained.
– The ESN has been connected by setting IP parameters on the device.
For details about how to obtain the ESN, see the user manual of the
corresponding controller, for example, SMU02B User Manual.
Contact technical support engineers to obtain the user manual.
– The ESN has been connected through the channel management function.
For details, see the in-band channel configuration.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Configuration > Device Integration..

Method 1: Connect a device by automatically discovering it.
On the Auto Discovery page, click Access in the Operation column of the target
ESN.
NO TE
● If is displayed, the adapter has been installed or does not need to be
installed.
● If is displayed, the adapter corresponding to the ESN is not installed. In
this case, you need to install the adapter.
Search for the required adapter name in the iMaster NetEco SitePower
V600R026C10XXX Version Mapping Table based on the information displayed in the
Adapter Version column on the Auto Discovery page. For details about how to obtain
the version mapping table and adapter software installation package, see "Installation
Preparations > Obtaining the Adapter Software Installation Packages" in the Software
Installation and Commissioning Guide.
● The adapter of the inverter has been preset in the system.
Method 2: Connect a device by using its ESN.
On the Auto Discovery page, click Access with ESN to create a site and controller.
Step 4 In the dialog box that is displayed, select the corresponding node.
● To connect an inverter:
a. Select the PV plant to which the device is to be connected, and click OK.
b. In the dialog box that is displayed, click Apply.
c. Set the string capacity.
i. Select the device whose capacity is to be configured and click Set
String Capacity.
ii. In the dialog box that is displayed, enter the PV capacity and click
OK.
iii. Confirm the configured string capacity.
d. On the Set Electricity Prices tab page, set Feed-in Tariff and Purchase
Price.
e. On the Set Other Info tab page, set the address and time zone of the PV
plant.
NO TE
When setting the time zone of a plant, ensure that the time zone of the plant is
the same as that of the device. If their time zones are different, the management
system will deliver the time zone of the plant to the device and replace the time
zone of the device with that of the plant.
f. Click Save.
● To connect a site device:
a. Select the parent subnet or root node to which the new subnet belongs,
enter information such as the site name and controller name, and click

NO TE
▪
In the Device Integration dialog box, if a site has been created, select the site
and enter information such as the controller name.
▪
The SSL Connection for automatic site deployment is available only in the
out-of-band access mode. During in-band site deployment, SSL Connection is
unavailable and the in-band connection attribute is determined by the
channels.
b. In the Confirm dialog box, if you click Yes, the Site Management page is
displayed, showing the access status. If you click No, the system returns
to the Auto Discovery page.
----End

Managing In-Band Channels
On the In-band Channel Management tab page, you can perform the following
operations.
Operation Procedure
Selecting a Select the channel connection type of the BSC and NodeB/
channel eNodeB based on the device type.
connection type
Selecting a ● SSL (certificate authentication): Select this connection
connector type mode when the NetEco is preinstalled.
● SSL (PSK authentication):
– Select this connection mode when the NetEco is not
preinstalled and the customer does not have PKI.
In this connection mode, you need to set PSK ID and
PSK Key Material. The values of these parameters
must be the same as those of PSK ID and PSK
password on the SSL Connection Settings page of
MAE. You can click Modifying the selected channel
PSK key material to change the key password.
– Select this connection mode when the pre-installed
environment is used and the certificate is damaged.
● Normal: Select this connection mode when the device
does not support the SSL connection mode.
NOTE
● When Channel Type is set to BSC, only SSL (certificate
authentication) and Normal are supported.
● When Channel Type is set to NodeB/eNodeB, only SSL
(certificate authentication), SSL (PSK authentication), and
Normal are supported.

Operation Procedure
Modifying 1. Select the channel parameters to be modified and edit
channel data them.
2. Click Apply.
NOTE
When modifying the channel information, you can edit only Channel
Name and Channel IP.
Deleting 1. Delete channel data.
channel data In the channel list, select the channel data to be deleted
and click Delete.
2. In the dialog box that is displayed, click Yes.
Importing 1. Click Import.
channel data
2. In the Import dialog box, click Download Template.
3. Click and select the file to be imported.
4. Click Upload.
If the import is successful, the newly imported channel
data is displayed in channel list.
Exporting 1. Export channel data.
channel data
● Export some channel data.
Select the desired channel data, click Export, and
choose Selected.
● Export all channel data.
Click Export and choose Export All.
2. In the Download dialog box, click Save to save the data in
the specified directory.
Viewing Click Show Associated Site in the row where the channel to
associated sites be viewed is located. In the dialog box that is displayed, view
the sites that communicate with NetEco through the channel.

● You have obtained the adapter software installation packages.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Configuration > Device Integration.

Step 4 In the Upload adapter package dialog box that is displayed, click .
Step 5 Select the adapter file to be uploaded and click Upload.
NO TE
Ensure that each .zip file has two corresponding signature files (in .crl and .cms formats).
Step 6 Check the status in the Digital Signature column of the uploaded adapter file.
● Valid: The adapter package is obtained from an official channel.
● Invalid: The adapter package is not obtained from the official channel. Obtain
the adapter package from the official channel and upload it again.
Step 7 Select the uploaded adapter package and click Install.
Step 8 In the displayed Information dialog box, click Yes.
NO TE
The information about the installed adapter package is displayed in the list.
----End

3.3.2.5 PV Inverter Device Access
You can bind devices or unbind users connected to the system to quickly connect
to or disconnect from a plant.
Prerequisites
● You have permission to manage device access.
● The new device has been connected to the management system.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Configuration > Device Integration.
Step 2 On the displayed page, manage device access. For details, see Table 3-67.
Table 3-67 Operations related to new device management
Operation Procedure
Binding to an 1. Select one or more devices and click Bind to Existing
existing PV plant Plant. In the displayed dialog box, select a PV plant and
click OK.
2. On the Add Devices tab page, click Apply and click
Confirm.
3. On the Set String Capacity tab page, select the bound
devices and click Set String Capacity, In the displayed
dialog box, set the string capacity and click OK.
4. Click Save and then click Confirm.
Binding to a new 1. Select one or more devices and click Bind to New Plant.
PV plant
2. In the displayed Add Plant dialog box, enter related
information as prompted and click Save to complete the
creation and device binding of the new plant.

----End
3.3.2.6 Plant Management
You can centrally manage multiple plants and view important information about a
single plant. This meets different management requirements.
3.3.2.6.1 Creating a Plant
After a device is connected to the management system, you can create a plant in
the management system.
Prerequisites
● You have commissioned the device and set Huawei NMS parameters on the
SmartLogger client. For details, see the SmartLogger User Manual.
● You have obtained the SN of the access device.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Configuration > Device Integration.
Step 2 On the Plant Management page, click Add Plant.
Step 3 In the displayed dialog box, enter the basic information about the plant and click
Next.
Step 4 Connect a device.
1. Click Add.
2. In the dialog box that is displayed, select the device to be connected and click
OK.
3. Confirm the selected device information and click Next.
Step 5 Set the string capacity.
1. Select an inverter and click Set String Capacity.
2. In the displayed dialog box, enter the PV capacity values and click OK.
3. Confirm the configured string capacity and click Next.
Step 6 On the Set Electricity Prices page, set Feed-in Tariff and Purchase Price, and
click Next.
Step 7 On the Set Other Info page, configure the address, location, and time zone of the
plant.
NO TE
When setting the plant time zone, ensure that the plant time zone is the same as the
device time zone. Otherwise, the management system delivers the plant time zone to the
devices to overwrite the time zone configured on devices after the plant is created.
Step 8 Click Save.

On the Plant Management page, you can manage multiple plants in a
centralized manner, such as sharing EMI (Environmental Monitoring Instrument),
sharing plants, and modifying plant information.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the management system as an installer.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Configuration > Device Integration.
Step 2 On the Plant Management page, you can perform the operations listed in Table
1 as required.
Table 3-68 Plant management operations
Operati Procedure
on
Sharing 1. In the plant list, select one or more plants that require EMI
EMI sharing and click Shared EMI.
2. In the displayed dialog box, select the EMI to be shared, and click
Save.
NOTE
Each plant can receive a maximum of one EMI.
Cancelin 1. Select a plant that has received a shared EMI and click Shared
g EMI EMI.
sharing
2. In the displayed dialog box, click Reset, and then click Save.
3. In the displayed dialog box, click Confirm.
Modifyin
1. Click in the Operation column.
g plant
informat 2. In the displayed dialog box, enter the basic information about the
ion plant and click Apply.
3. Click Save.
Deleting
Select a plant in the plant list and click Delete or in the
a plant
Operation column.
NOTE
If there are a large number of devices in the plant, it takes about 10 to 20
minutes to delete the plant.
----End
3.3.2.7 Device License Management
This section describes how to check device license status on the Device License

apply for and update the licenses in a timely manner to ensure that the I-V curve
and smart tracking function can be used properly.
Procedure
1. Choose Device Management > Configuration > Device Integration.
2. Table 3-69 describes the operations related to device license management.
Table 3-69 Operations related to device license management
Task Name Procedure
Checking license On the License Information tab page, enter the
information Device name, Plant name, and Device SN, or select
License status, and click Search.
Applying for device 1. On the License Application tab page, click Export
licenses All or select the devices for which you want to
apply for licenses, and click Export Selected.
2. After exporting the application form, send it to
echnical support engineers to apply for a license
file.
Loading device 1. Click Upload License to upload license files.
licenses
2. After the license files are uploaded successfully,
click Load All to load the licenses for all devices.
Alternatively, select the devices for which licenses
need to be loaded and click Load Selected to load
the licenses for the selected devices.
During the loading, click Stop Loading to stop all
loading tasks. If you select the loading tasks to be
stopped and then click Stop Loading, the selected
tasks will be stopped.
Revoking device ● Select one or more devices whose licenses are to be
licenses revoked and click Revoke License.
● For devices whose licenses have been revoked, click
Export All Revocation Codes or Export Selected
Revocation Codes to export the license revocation
codes of all or selected devices.
After obtaining the revocation codes, contact echnical
support engineers to apply for new license files using
the ESNs and revocation codes of the current licenses.
3.3.3 Device Software Management
This section describes how to manage and upgrade the device software on the
NetEco.

Device software management means that the NetEco manages and remotely
upgrades the device software.
Table 3-70 describes the basic concepts related to software management in the
NetEco system.
Table 3-70 Basic concepts related to software management
Concept Description
Software To meet the growing requirement for network management, some
version new functions are added and some existing functions are
enhanced during the lifecycle of the software. These technical
changes are incorporated in the newly released software version.
device A version file is the file used for software upgrade.
Software
Verificatio The verification file is a digital signature. It is used to verify the
n file software package of the device to be upgraded.
Software Software upgrade is a process during which a new software version
upgrade is installed to replace an earlier version. Through software
upgrade, technical changes are incorporated into the current
version. Software upgrade may involve the adjustment of certain
system configuration data and device hardware. Software upgrade
involved in the NetEco system includes device upgrade.For details
about the device software upgrade process, seeFigure 3-15.
History You can view the device information that have been successfully
Upgrade upgraded.
Tasks

3.3.3.2 Viewing Device Version Information
This section describes how to view information about software versions on the
NetEco.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Software Management.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Configuration > Device Software Management.
Step 2 In the left pane of the displayed Software Management window, click Device

and refreshes the version information in the version information list.
Step 3 On the Device Version page, view the device version information.
You can view the version information about all devices or search version
information about a specified device by device name. You can also filter the
devices by version to view all devices for a specified version.
NO TE
For versions earlier than V100R022C00SPC010, DPSPV300R022C00, and
V100R022C00SPC100, a hyphen (-) is displayed in the Package Name column for the
directly connected devices.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
You can perform the following operations on the devices displayed in the device
version list:
If You Need To... Then...
Refresh the current page On the Device Version page, click
Refresh.
The device versions on this page are
refreshed.

If You Need To... Then...
Upgrade a device 1. On the Device Version page, select
one or more devices and click
Device Upgrade.
2. In the displayed Device Upgrade
Wizard dialog box, select the target
version and active strategy.
NOTE
If a selected device is improperly
connected to the NetEco, the device
will not be displayed in the Selected
Objects list and the device software
cannot be upgraded.
3. You can select the device to be
upgraded on the displayed page
and click Upgrade Check.
According to the check result, check
whether any devices need to be
upgraded. If yes, select the device
to be upgraded and click Confirm.
NOTE
● You can determine whether to
perform pre-upgrade check. If you
do not perform pre-upgrade check,
click Confirm to perform
subsequent operations.
● To ensure the correctness of device
upgrade, you are advised to perform
pre-upgrade check.
4. In the displayed confirmation dialog
box, click Yes.
Export device versions On the Device Version page, click
Version Export.
● Export Selected: export selected
records in the list.
● Export All: export all records in the
list.

If You Need To... Then...
Synchronize device versions On the Device Version page,
● Sync Selected: select one or
multiple devices and click
Synchronize Version > Sync
Selected to synchronize the device
version information.
● Sync All: click Synchronize Version
> Sync All to synchronize all device
version information.
After the synchronization, the NetEco
then obtains the latest version
information and refreshes the version
information on the version information
list.
3.3.3.3 Uploading Device Software Packages
This section describes how to upload the device software packages from the
NetEco client to the NetEco server.
Prerequisites
● You have the operation rights for Software Management.
● You have obtained the software package and verification file to be upgraded
from Huawei enterprise technical support website.
The download method is as follows:
– For enterprise users:
i. Log in to Huawei enterprise technical support website https://
support.huawei.com/enterprise.
ii. Browse or search for the device type name, obtain the desired
software package, and click cms in the row that contains the
software package to download the P7S signature file.
– For carriers:
i. Log in to Huawei technical support website https://
support.huawei.com.
ii. Browse or search for the device type name, obtain the desired
software package, and click cms in the row that contains the
software package to download the P7S signature file.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Configuration > Device Software Management.
Step 2 In the left pane of the displayed Software Management tab page, click Device

Step 4 In the displayed Upload and Verify Software Package dialog box, click .
Step 5 Select the software packages and verification file to be upload and click Upload
NO TE
● The NetEco verifies the software packages to be uploaded. If the verification or
uploading fails, troubleshoot the failure as prompted. Then upload the device software
package again.
● The size of the device software package to be uploaded must be less than 300 MB, and
the size of the verification file must be less than 1 MB.
Step 6 Close the Upload and Verify Software Package dialog box.
Information about the software packages that are successfully uploaded is
displayed on the device software package list of the Device Software page.
----End
Follow-up Procedure

3.3.3.4 Creating Upgrade Templates
The upgrade template supports automatic identification of devices to be
upgraded. Multiple paths are automatically upgraded in sequence. You can quickly
upgrade devices based on the upgrade template.
Prerequisites
● You have the operation rights for Software Management.
● You have uploaded required software packages to the NetEco server. For
detailed operations, see 3.3.3.3 Uploading Device Software Packages.

Step 1 Choose Device Management > Configuration > Device Software Management.
Step 2 In the left pane of the displayed Device Software Management tab page, click
Upgrade Templates.
Step 3 On the Upgrade Templates page, click Create.
Step 4 In the Create upgrade template page, set related parameters and click OK.
NO TE
Basic parameters on the Create upgrade template page:
● Template name: specifies the name of the upgrade template, which is user-defined.
● Source version: indicates the version to be upgraded. Automatically synchronize existing
versions in Device Version.
● Upgrade path: The number of upgrade paths cannot exceed 10. The system
automatically synchronizes the existing versions in the Device Software. If multiple
versions are selected, the system upgrades the software in sequence.
● Scope: The scope to be upgraded. You can customize the upgrade scope.
Step 5 In the displayed dialog box, click OK.
Step 6 After the upgrade template is created, click the number in the Upgradeable
column.
NO TE
The number in the Upgradable column identifies the same number of devices as the source
version.
Step 7 In the Create an upgrade task based on the upgrade template dialog box, set
related parameters and click OK.
Step 8 Sure to upgrade? In the dialog box, click Yes.
Step 9 In the displayed dialog box,click OK.
NO TE
Click OK. On the Device Software Management > Upgrade Task view the upgrade
progress. Multiple upgrade tasks are created based on the upgrade path defined in the
upgrade template. After the previous upgrade task is successfully upgraded, the next
upgrade task is automatically performed.
----End

This section describes how to remotely upgrade device software on the NetEco.
Prerequisites
● You have the operation rights for Software Management.
● You have uploaded required software packages to the NetEco server. For
detailed operations, see 3.3.3.3 Uploading Device Software Packages.
● The NetEco communicates with devices properly.
Context
This section describes only universal operations performed on the NetEco.
Procedures for upgrading the devices are different. For details about the upgrade
processes, see the upgrade guides of the corresponding devices.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Configuration > Device Software Management.
Step 2 In the left pane of the displayed Software Management tab page, click Upgrade
Task.
Step 3 On the Upgrade Task tab page, click Upgrade.
Step 4 You can select the device to be upgraded and the target version on the displayed
page and click Pre-Check. According to the check result, check whether any
devices need to be upgraded.
NO TE
● A maximum of 2000 devices can be upgraded at a time.
● You can determine whether to perform pre-upgrade check. If you do not perform pre-
upgrade check, perform subsequent operations.(To ensure the correctness of device
upgrade, you are advised to perform pre-upgrade check.)
Step 5 On the displayed dialog box, view details about the upgrade task. After confirming
the information, set the execution mode and active strategy of the upgrade task
according to Table 3-71 and Table 3-72. Then, click Confirm.
Table 3-71 Upgrade task execution mode
Execution Mode Description Operation
Immediate The NetEco executes Select Immediate.
the upgrade task
immediately.
Delay Execute The NetEco executes Select Delay Execute and set the
the upgrade task execution time of the upgrade
according to the time task.
you have set.

Delay Activate The activation time Select Delay Activate from the
can be set. Active Strategy drop-down list
box.

needs to be manually Active Strategy drop-down list
activated. box.
Auto Activate The upgrade task is Select Auto Activate from the
automatically Active Strategy drop-down list
activated after the box.
download is complete.
Step 6 In the displayed confirmation dialog box, click Yes.
To upgrade device software, you need to download software and activate
software. The progress and status of the upgrade task are displayed in the
upgrade task list. When the progress reaches 100%, the upgrade task is complete.
Step 7 Check information about the target version by referring to 3.3.3.2 Viewing Device
Version Information.
● If the version you have upgraded to is the same as the target version, the
upgrade succeeds.
● If the version you have upgraded to is different with the target version, the
upgrade fails. If this occurs, contact technical support engineers.
NO TE
You can view, delete, and export the successful upgrade task on the History Upgrade Task
page. For details, see 1.6-Viewing Historical Upgrade Tasks.
If the upgrade fails and prompts Exceeded the maximum number of device SO libraries,
please contact the device side to delete redundant devices in the SO library or modify the
maximum limit of devices in the SO library.
----End

management system.

● You have the Device Upgrade permission.
● You have obtained the software package of the target version.
– For details about how to obtain the device software package, see the
upgrade guide of the corresponding device.
– To obtain the app software package, perform the following steps:
i. Log in to https://support.huawei.com/enterprise/en/index.html.
ii. Navigate to or search for SmartPVMS.
iii. Find and download the app software and digital signature file.
– After the downloading is complete, use the digital certificate and
verification tool available at Huawei technical support website to verify
the digital signature of the software package.
i. Log in to https://support.huawei.com/enterprise/en/index.html.

Step 1 Choose Device Management > Configuration > Device Software Management.
