3.3.3.7.2 Device Upgrade
This topic describes how to remotely upgrade a device through the management
system.
Prerequisites
● You have the Device Upgrade permission.
● The software package of the target version has been uploaded. For details
about the procedure, see 3.3.3.7.1 Software Package Management.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Configuration > Device Software Management.
Step 2 On the PVMS Upgrade Management page, click the Device Upgrade tab.
Step 3 On the Device Upgrade page, click Add to create an upgrade task.
Step 4 In the dialog box that is displayed, enter the task information and click ok.
Step 5 In the displayed dialog box, click I Know.
Step 6 In the upgrade task list, click to view the upgrade details.

Automatic update rules can be configured. When a user accesses a device that
meets the automatic update rules, the system automatically updates the device to
avoid some functions being disabled due to outdated version.
Prerequisites
● You have the Device Upgrade permission.
● The software package of the target version has been uploaded. For details
about the procedure, see 3.3.3.7.1 Software Package Management.
Context
● Forced update rules take effect for devices newly bound to a plant. After a
communication device that meets the update rule is bound to the plant, an
update task will be automatically created. The bug-fixing update rules take
effect for devices that have been bound to the plant. After a device triggers a
rule, an update task will be automatically created.
● If a forced update task fails, the system automatically retries every four hours
for a maximum of three times. If a bug-fixing update task fails, the system
automatically retries every one hour for a maximum of times specified in the
update rule.
● After an automatic update task is created, you need to log in to the
management system as an installer or log in to the App as an owner to
confirm the update.
● If an automatic update task fails, you can manually create an update task if
needed. For details about the procedure, see 3.3.3.7.2 Device Upgrade.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Configuration > Device Software Management.
Step 2 On the PVMS Upgrade Management page, click the Auto Update Rule tab.
Step 3 On the Auto Update Rule page, set automatic update rules. For details about the
procedure, see Table 3-75.
Table 3-75 Automatic update rule management
Operation Procedure
Creating a rule 1. Click Add.
2. In the displayed dialog box, select an update type, set
related information, and click OK.
Deleting a rule Select a rule and click Delete.
NOTE
Deleting a rule has no impact on an automatic update task that has
been created.

----End

● The service life alarm threshold indicates the time before the service life expires.
For example, if the service life of a device will expire in October and the service life
alarm threshold is set to 6 months, the device starts to report the alarm indicating
that the service life is about to expire every day from April.

You can log in to the NE system through the NetEco site console to configure

NO TE

and click Log In.
● You can log in to the third-party device client through the NetEco.

click OK.
b. In the device port list, click Link to access the third-party device client.

numbers to other sites in batches.

3.4 Performance
Performance management allows network management and maintenance
personnel to check and monitor the operating status of devices in a period of time
in the past, obtain device operating tendency, and take corresponding measures.
3.4.1 Viewing Historical Data
This section describes how to query historical data for users to learn the running
status of sites in a specified time period. Users can query historical data by device
type or template type. In addition, users can choose to display query results in line
charts, level diagrams, or tables.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights to View Historical Data and Export Historical
Data.
Context
Historical data refers to the measurement values of performance counters during

1. Choose Device Management > Performance > Historical Data.
2. Select the indicators to be viewed.
– Method 1: Select indicators in the template management module or by
creating a custom template.
i. Choose Template Management > Adding a Template on the left.
ii. In the Adding a Template dialog box, set Template Name and
Select Indicator.
iii. Click OK.
NO TE
After a template is added, all users can view the template. When deleting a
template, you can delete only the template created by yourself. The user
with the highest permission can delete all templates.
iv. After a template is created, select the template in the search box on
the left.
v. Click the device type and click Query in the lower part.
– Method 2: Check the device type.
i. In the left pane, select the device to be queried.
ii. Select counters and click Query in the lower part.
3. On the top of the query result page, select devices and counters by device
type.
4. Click the chart and select the query time in the upper left corner of the page.
5. View the historical data query result.
Follow-up Procedure
On the Historical Data page, you can perform the following operations.
Table 3-82 Historical data

● : Figures are displayed in up-

supported.

3.4.2 Sync Historical Data
When the data in a certain period is incomplete or lost, you can retrieve historical
data through supplementary collection.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Create Synchronized Tasks and View
Synchronized Tasks.
Procedure

Step 3 Select the time range for supplementary collection.
Step 4 Click Create Tasks to recollect historical data.
In the displayed dialog box, click OK. The synchronization progress of the device is
updated in the right pane. Check whether the supplementary collection is
successful.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
You can perform the following operations on the Sync Historical Data page.
Operation Procedure
Querying supplementary collection You can view the status of
tasks supplementary collection tasks and
retry or cancel a task.
1. Select the device under the root
node on the left.
2. Specify the task status and the time
range for supplementary collection.
NOTE
If you select Query All Tasks under
Time Range, the system queries all
historical supplementary collection
tasks.
3. Click Query Tasks.
Retrying historical data collection You can re-collect the data that is in
the Cancel or Failed state.
In the supplementary collection task
list, select the Cancel or Failed state
and click Retry.
NOTE
The causes of the supplementary collection
failure are as follows: The device is
unavailable, an unknown error occurs,
waiting for the response packet from the
device times out, a manual synchronization
task exists, or an invalid task exists.
Canceling the supplementary Cancel the supplementary collection
collection of historical data operation in the historical data
collection process.
In the supplementary collection task
list, select the task whose progress
does not reach 100% and click Cancel.

parameters. Table 3-83 describes the function scenarios. After the counters are
set, the collection task is delivered to the connected device along with the
template.

3.5 Report
NetEco provides the functions of Configuring the Report System, Report
Management, and Report Task, to manage the Reports.

This section describes how to query the real-time information about site devices
by using the report management function.
3.5.1.1 PAV Management
Power Availability Related Statistics includes Querying the Power Availability
Analysis Report and Querying Site Power Outage Report.
3.5.1.1.1 Querying the Power Supply Availability Analysis Report
The Power Availability Analysis Report collects the energy efficiency available
ratios and availability of network energy.
Prerequisites
You have gained the access to Report Management.
Procedure
1. Choose Device Management > Report > Report Management.
2. On the displayed Report Management page, choose PAV Management in
the navigation pane.
3. Click Power Availability Analysis Report in the navigation pane.
– The report supports power availability statistics in regional reports, site
reports, and group reports.
NO TE
Group report statistics: Branch group statistics are supported. To use this function,
you need to enable the LLVD alarm or DC current algorithm. For details, see
Strategy Configuration in 3.5.4 Report System Configuration.
● LLVD alarm statistics (PAV calculation): 24 hours – LLVD durations/24 x 100%.
● DC current statistics (PAV calculation): 24 hours – Duration of the DC output
power distribution current or branch group current that (≤ 0)/24 x 100%.
● If the branch group name or configuration is changed, the name of the
branch group in the subsequent summary data and reports is updated to the
latest name.
● Changes (adding or deleting branches) to the branch group configuration do
not affect the historical summary data. The reports that are automatically
generated later and those that are manually regenerated are all based on the
latest branch group configuration.
– On the report page, perform operations based on the following scenarios.

3.5.1.2 Site Energy Efficiency Management
The hybrid power station running report displays the report data of batteries,
mains, diesel generators, and solar power supply, and allows users to export and
send reports by email.
3.5.1.2.1 Querying the Energy Efficiency and Consumption Report
The Energy Efficiency and Consumption Report page collects statistics on the
efficiency of network energy.

In the Energy Efficiency and Consumption Report page, Solar Power (kWh)
indicates the total input energy yield of all site instances. The report is displayed in
a bar chart and a table, with the collection periods of Day, Week, Month, Year.
The full-data report can be exported.

3.5.1.2.3 Querying the Site Fuel Consumption Report
The Site Fuel Consumption Report collects the accumulated fuel consumption
and identifies the sites with abnormal fuel levels.
Prerequisites
You have gained the access to Report Management.
Context
The Site Fuel Consumption Report includes the Site Report,, Regional Chain
Report, and Site Chain Report. The Site Report collects the accumulated site fuel
consumption and average fuel consumption per hour, whereas the Regional
Chain Report collects statistics on the chain comparison between the fuel
consumption data of sites in the current period and that of the previous period
within a region. The Site Chain Report collects statistics on the chain comparison
between the fuel consumption data of sites in the current period and that of the
previous period.
Table 3-85 Site fuel consumption report – site report

(L)

(L)

to delete conditions. A maximum of four conditions can be

3.5.1.2.5 Querying the Bit/Watt Synergy Report
By querying the Bit/Watt synergy report, you can learn about the site end-to-end
energy efficiency, traffic volume, and energy consumption details.
Prerequisites
● You have gained the access to Report Management.
● The wireless NMS has been connected.
● The energy saving statistics function has been enabled on the wireless NMS,
and energy consumption data-related counters have been subscribed to. For
details, see iMaster MAE Product Documentation of GUIs and Parameters for
Base Station Energy Conservation Management.
Context
In the Bit/Watt Synergy Report page, Solar Power (kWh) indicates the total
input energy yield of all site instances. The report is displayed in a bar chart and a
table, with the collection periods of Day, Week, Month, Year. The full-data report
can be exported.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Report > Report Management.
Step 2 On the displayed Report Management page, choose Site Energy Efficiency
Management in the navigation pane.
Step 3 Click Bit/Watt Synergy Report. The report page is displayed.

3.5.1.2.6 Querying Carbon Emission Reports
This section describes how to query carbon emission reports. Using the reports,
you can learn about the energy consumption and carbon emission of a site, which
helps you carry out carbon emission reduction.
Prerequisites

● Site Chain Report: View and understand the difference between the energy
consumption and carbon emission of the current period and the previous
period.
● The Energy Input Per Traffic Input and Network Carbon Intensity data is
displayed only when the NetEco is connected to the wireless NMS. For details
about how to connect them.
● Table 3-89 describes the data in a carbon emission report.
Table 3-89 Carbon emission report
Parameter Description
Total Energy Total energy input = Green energy input + Non-green
Input (kWh) energy input
NOTE
Green energy includes solar energy, wind energy, and other green 48
V inputs. Non-green energy sources include the mains, genset
(D.G.), and other non-green 48 V inputs. You can view the data in
the Energy Efficiency and Consumption Report.
Network Carbon Network carbon = Genset input energy x Genset CEF (1.150)
(kg) + Mains input energy x Mains CEF (0.475) + Other non-
green 48 V input energy x Other non-green 48 V CEF
(0.475)
NOTE
● The carbon emission is calculated based on the actual input
energy.
● For details about the genset, mains, and other non-green 48 V
input energy, see the Energy Efficiency and Consumption
Report.
● The CEF of other non-green 48 V inputs is consistent with the
mains CEF.
● You can choose Device Management > Device View > View,
select a site, and set the Carbon Factor (or CEF) in the Total
CO Emitted area on the right.
2
Carbon Factor Carbon factor = Network carbon/Total energy input
(kg/kWh)
Energy Input Per Energy input per traffic input = Total energy consumption/
Traffic Input Total traffic volume
(kWh/kbit)
NOTE
Total traffic volume: traffic consumed by transmission device data.
You can choose Device Management > Performance > Historical
Data to view the value.
Network Carbon Network carbon intensity = Network carbon/Total traffic
Intensity (kg/ volume
kbit)

Parameter Description
SEE (%) ● SEE refers to the site energy efficiency, indicating the
ratio of the energy consumption of the main equipment
to the power supply.
● If Telecom Equipment Energy Consumption is available
at a site: SEE = Telecom Equipment Energy
Consumption/Total Energy Input. If Telecom
Equipment Energy Consumption is unavailable: SEE =
(DC Load Energy Consumption – Temperature
Equipment Energy Consumption)/Total Energy Input
NOTE
● You can view SEE data by week, month, or year.
● You can view Telecom Equipment Energy Consumption, DC
Load Energy Consumption, and Temperature Equipment
Energy Consumption in the Energy Efficiency and
Consumption Report.
(NEE) (%) ● NEE indicates the ratio of the energy consumption of
main devices in a region to the power supply.
● If Telecom Equipment Energy Consumption is available
at a region: NEE = Telecom Equipment Energy
Consumption/Total Energy Input. If Telecom
Equipment Energy Consumption is unavailable: NEE =
(DC Load Energy Consumption – Temperature
Equipment Energy Consumption)/Total Energy Input
NO TE
The reports are displayed in a table, with the collection periods of Day, Week, Month, Year. Full
report data can be exported.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Report > Report Management.
Step 2 On the Report Management page, choose Site Energy Efficiency Management
in the navigation tree.
Step 3 Click Carbon Emissions Report.
On the report page, perform operations based on the following scenarios.
Applicatio Operation Method

Context
NEs supporting the SIM card usage statistic report are as follows
● Power1000 V200R003C00 and later versions

Parameter Description
SIM IMSI International Mobile Subscriber Identity of the SIM.
SIM Card Phone Phone number of the controller SIM card.

traffic needs to be set.
– Period: Set the package by two dimensions: Month
and Year.
– Traffic Start Time: time when the site starts to use
wireless traffic.
– Wireless Traffic Packages Period: actual package
traffic of the site.
– Packages Traffic Remind Line (MB): Set a threshold
for the site to use wireless traffic. When the usage
reaches the threshold, an event of remaining wireless
traffic reminder is generated in Device Management
> Alarm Management > Current Alarms.

3.5.1.3.9 Querying the Load Running Report
This report collects statistics on the DC load power and current. Therefore, you can
learn whether the load is powered off and whether the peak power or current is
too high, which helps you learn about the load stability.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Report Management.
Context
Table 3-96 Load running report
Parameter Description
Max DC Load Maximum daily total DC load power of all devices at a site
Power (kW)
Avg DC Load Average DC load power of all devices at a site
Power (kW)

Power (kW)
BLVD Duration Total BLVD duration of all batteries at a site
(h)
Max DC Load Maximum daily total DC load current of all devices at a site
Current (A)
Avg DC Load Average DC load current of all devices at a site
Current (A)
Min DC Load Minimum DC load current of all devices at a site
Current (A)
Daily Total shutdown duration of the device due to link
Communicatio disconnection during the running period.
n Failure
Duration (h)

NO TE
● Report statistics principles:
● If DC output power distribution devices are available at a site, collect statistics on
the power, current, and energy consumption of the DC output power distribution
devices.
● If only iDMU devices exist at a site, collect only the power, current, and energy
consumption of the iDMU devices.
● Daily Communication Failure Duration can be collected only by day, week, or
month. Other parameters can be collected by day, week, month, or year.
● In the selection box on the right of the report page, you can select one to three
parameters to be displayed.
Procedure

3.5.1.3.11 Querying Power Supply Equipment Run Log
Collects statistics on the running data of power supply devices at a site.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Report Management.
Context
The NetEco obtains device run log files from the site on a scheduled basis every
day and stores the log files in the database. When a user queries the report on
NetEco, the NetEco obtains data from the database and generates the
corresponding report.
Table 3-98 Power Supply Equipment Run Log report
Display Parameter Description
Mode
By time Time Time when device running status changes.
Device Name Name of a device.
State Device running status.
Details Reason why a device starts or stops. This column
displays only the reason why the genset starts or
stops.
By device Device Name Name of a device.
Start Time Time when a device starts to run.
Stop Time Time when a device stops running.
Total Total runtime of a device, that is, Stop Time–Start
Runtime Time.

not displayed.

the corresponding device.

3.5.1.4 Customized Reports
The report management system provides the resource report, performance report,
energy report, and capacity report.

You can create reports based on service requirements to quickly query required
report data.
Prerequisites
● The NetEco IP address has been added to the trusted site list of the browser.
● You have gained the access to Report Management.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Report > Report Management.
Step 2 In the displayed Report Management page, click Custom.
Step 3 In the Customized Report page, set basic information of the report and select
objects and counter source.
NO TE
● The Predefined reports collect statistics on the counters in the predefined reports.
These counters are not displayed by default. To view these counters, contact technical
support engineers.
● The Historical Data refers to the data of historical performance indexes.
● The Configuration Data statistics are the data of configuration counters.
● If you select Historical Data or Configuration Data from the Counter source drop-
down list box and select Device from the View drop-down list box, you can click
Setting custom metrics to create customized indicators to ensure the report data
display effect. In the Selected Indicator area, select the created customized indicators.
Step 4 Choose Predefine Report and Historical Data, click Next.
NO TE
Choose Configuration Data, click Finish and Finish and create task.
Step 5 You can create charts for predefined reports and performance data counter source
reports.
1. Click +.
2. Select a type for the chart to be added, and click OK.
NO TE
– In the chart area, click to modify the object and items.
– In the chart area, click to delete the chart.
Step 6 Click Finish.
Click Finish and create task. The report is created and the page automatically
jump to the Report Task page. For details about creating a report task, see 3.5.3.1
Creating a Report Task .

You can perform the following operations on a customized report.
Option Description

● For reports for which no report tasks are created, click

● For reports for which report tasks are created, delete
the report tasks before you delete the reports.

details, see3.5.1.4.2 Querying a Report.

3.5.1.4.4 Saving Report Results
Enables you to save required information in a certain format when you are
querying the report data. This facilitates querying and browsing.

● You have gained the access to Report Management.
● The required report has been created.
● The email server has been configured.
Context
The report result can be saved as a PDF, EXCEL, or CSV file.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Report > Report Management.

a. In the preset report area, select the report to be saved.
b. Set the query parameters.
● Customized report
a. In the Customized Report area, select the report to be saved and click
the report name. The Please select query time dialog box is displayed.
b. Set a query time.
Step 3 In the Customized Report area, select the name of the report which you want to
save.
NO TE
By default, report information is queried by the time and periods when reports are created.
Users can re-select the time and view the information about reports of a different time.
Step 4 Click Query to enter the result querying page.
In the result querying page, you can perform the following operations on the
queried result.
Operation Operation Method
Exporting report Perform the following operations (Chrome is used as an
results example):
1. Click Export and select an export format from the drop-
down list box.
2. Click View Export Tasks to download the export result to
the local PC.
NOTE
CSV supports export by table only, not chart. If you do not select
table when creating reports, it will not display on the drop-down list
when clicking Export.

Operation Operation Method
Sending an e- 1. Click Email.
mail
2. In the dialog box that is displayed, enter the recipient,
subject, body, and report format.
NOTE
If email recipients have been defined, click Recipients and select
receipients from the displayed Select User Group dialog box. If
no recipient is defined, click Edit User Group to create recipients.
For details, see 8.1.3.3.2 Creating a Notified User Group.
The policy for sending an email attachment package is as
follows:
– Size of the attachment package ≤ 10 MB: Send it normally.
– Size of the attachment package > 10 MB: Compress it into
multiple volumes and send each volume (≤ 9 MB) separately.
3. Click OK.
NOTE
● PDF and Excel support table and chart.
● To send remote notifications, you need to enter personal data,
such as phone numbers and email addresses. Therefore, you are
obligated to take considerable measures, in compliance with the
laws of the countries concerned and the user privacy policies of
your company, to ensure that the personal data of users is fully
protected.
● Personal data such as phone numbers and email addresses is
anonymized on the NetEco GUI and encrypted during batch data
transmission to ensure data security.
----End

3.5.3 Report Tasks
This section describes how to manage report task, including creating, deleting,
modifying, and querying report tasks.
3.5.3.1 Creating a Report Task
You can create a report task and set execution frequency for the report task to
generate a report automatically and send the report by email.
Prerequisites
● Report templates that have been created are available.
● You are authorized to perform operations on the Report Task page.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Report > Report Task.
Step 2 On the Report Task page, click Create and set parameters.
Table 3-101 Report Task parameters
Parameter Description
Task Name Name of the task to be created.
Select report Select the report of the task to be collected. If the required
report is not in the list, click Custom to create a report.
Execution Set the execution frequency of the report task to automatically
Frequency generate reports.
NOTE
If you select Hourly, you are advised to set the execution time to 30 to
60 minutes to prevent data loss due to ongoing report data calculation.
Conditions ● Custom: Report task statistics are set based on different
dimensions.
● Template: This function is not available.

Parameter Description
Report format Format of the report to be sent. Currently, the PDF, Excel, and
CSV formats are supported. The CSV file format applies only to
the export of tables, but not figures.
NOTE
● When Select report is set to a custom report and Report format is
set to Excel, you can customize an Excel template. The system
provides four template styles.
● After Custom report module upload is set to on the
Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management >
Business function configuration page of the PowerEcho, the
Customize Template function is available. For details, see 3.5.3.2
Example: Creating a Report Task Based on a Custom Template.
Send Email Set the recipient, subject, body, and report format. For details
about how to define a recipient, see 8.1.3.2.2 Commissioning
Email Server Notifications.
Dump Report Set the file path and report format. After the setting is
complete, the report file is saved to /opt/neteco/nbi/
neteco/dld/report/+File path for the northbound system to
use.
For example, if File path is AAA, the report file is saved
to /opt/neteco/nbi/neteco/dld/report/AAA.
----End

3.5.3.2 Example: Creating a Report Task Based on a Custom Template
This section describes how to use a custom template.
Context
A customized report records the daily input energy (kWh) and output energy
(kWh) of the PDU. You also need to collect statistics on the total input energy,
total output energy, monthly energy loss of a single object, and total energy loss
of all objects at the end of a month.
Prerequisites
● You have the operation rights for Report Task.
● You have enabled the custom report template function.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Report > Report Task.
Step 2 Click Create in the upper right corner of the page to create a report task.

Parameter Settings
Task Name PDU energy consumption statistics
Select report PDU energy
Execution At 15:45 on the first day of each month
Frequency
Report format Excel
Step 4 Click Customize Template. In the dialog box that is displayed, select Template 3.
Step 5 Click Download. Download the report task template to the PC.
Step 6 Open the report task template downloaded to the PC, customize indicators and
calculation formulas on the Report sheet, and save the settings.
Read the Description sheet in the report task template to learn the modification
requirements of the template file.

2. Enter the cell content based on Table 3-103.

3.5.4 Report System Configuration
Through report system configuration, users can configure the threshold of the
report storage area to prevent insufficient storage space of the report storage
area. In addition, users can configure the parameters of the abnormal report data
and PAV algorithm.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Report > Report System Config.
Step 2 On the report system configuration page, you can perform the following

Function Description
Storage Set the upper limit of the report storage capacity and click Save.
area
NOTE
The system checks the report storage capacity at 00:00 every day. If the
used capacity exceeds 80% of the upper limit, an alarm is generated. If the
used capacity reaches the upper limit, the system cannot generate new
reports. In this case, you need to adjust the upper limit of the report
storage capacity or delete the generated report files.
Strategy
Configurati ● In the Abnormal Data Display Setting area, click or
on
to show or hide abnormal data. For details about the
definition of abnormal report data, see Table 3-104.
● In the PAV Algorithm Switch area, the system calculates the
PAV data at the site and branch group levels in the Power
Availability Analysis Report based on the selected data
source. The details are as follows:
– LLVD Alarms: The PAV data is calculated based on the
power-on and power-off time of the LLVD alarm.
PAV calculation formula: (24 hours – LLVD durations in a
day)/24 x 100%
When a site-level power-off alarm is used to calculate the
DC output current in the power availability analysis report,
the DC output current is calculated as follows:
▪
SMU/Blade controller scenario: If the number of LLVD
breakers is 0, the DC output power distribution current is
used for calculation.
▪
SCC800 controller scenario: If any route of the AC meter
current channel is mapped to the digital power-current,
the total DC load current is used for calculation.
Otherwise, DC PAV calculation is not supported.
– DC Current: The PAV data is calculated based on the DC
output power distribution current or branch group current.
PAV calculation formula: (24 hours – Duration when the
current is less than 0 or is empty)/24 x 100%
When a site-level current alarm is used to calculate the DC
output current in the power availability analysis report, the
DC output current is calculated as follows:
SCC800 controller scenario: If any route of the AC meter
current channel is mapped to the digital power-current, the
total DC load current is used for calculation. Otherwise, DC
PAV calculation is not supported.

Function Description
Period start
time ● Select the start time of the month and click or
to determine whether to offset the monthly summary data to
the next month. After the setting is complete, you can view the
monthly summary data whose start time is any day.
NOTE
Only the administrator user can modify the period start time.

Step 2 In the KPI Benchmark Setting window, select a report name and a column name.
Step 3 Click in the Operation column of the site to be modified, select a value for
Inspection Algorithm, and set BaseLine Value.
NO TE
If there are many sites, click Batch Set at the upper right corner of the page, select a value for
Site Group and Inspection Algorithm, set BaseLine Value, and click Save.
Step 4 Click in the Operation column to complete the baseline setting. The new
baseline threshold is displayed on the report page.

and smart tracking function can be used properly.

Inverter Device Management.

5
Energy Efficiency Management
NetEco provides energy consumption management, energy saving management,
and intelligent power consumption management. It monitors the energy efficiency
information of the management domain and devices in real time, helping users
use proper power consumption.
5.1 Power Consumption Management
This section describes features related to power consumption.
5.1.1 Intelligent Load Management
Intelligent Load Management provides Branch Definition, Precise Metering,
and Backup Slicing to achieve efficient management, flexible power distribution,
precise power consumption metering, and differentiated power backup for various
services, and thereby lowering manual O&M costs.
5.1.1.1 Branch Definition
This function enables you to define branch group IDs and branch groups, and
customize the capacities to view and manage the load information of load
branches corresponding to different branch groups.
Prerequisites
You have the View Intelligent Load Management and Operate Intelligent Load
Management permissions. For details, see User Authorization.
Context
● The load branch, branch group ID, and branch group are described as follows:
– Load branch: Connects to each load in the power system. Intelligent
circuit breakers or non-intelligent circuit breakers can be deployed to

NO TE
● For devices connected to intelligent circuit breakers, you can set Branch
Group ID and Branch Group for one or more load branches.
● For devices connected to non-intelligent circuit breakers, you can set Branch
Group for one or more load branches.
– Branch Group ID: Indicates a unique ID of a load branch group. To
improve O&M efficiency, you can group different load branches. After one
branch group ID is allocated to multiple load branches, the grouping is
complete.
– Branch Group: Indicates the name of a load branch group. You can
rename the load branches corresponding to the branch group ID as
required. After allocating multiple load branches to one branch group,
you can configure the load branches in the branch group in a batch to
improve O&M efficiency.
– One branch group ID can be configured with multiple load branches, but
with only one branch group.
● Secondary authorization for intelligent load management is disabled by
default. To enable secondary authorization, see 8.2.7 Configuring Secondary
Authorization Operations.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Power Consumption Management >
Intelligent Load Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Branch Definition.
NO TE
By default, all load branches that support the branch definition function are displayed, and
the search criteria can be customized.

Task Name Operation
Configuring a 1. Click Config Branch Group.
branch group
2. Add a branch group.
– Optional: Single configuration
a. Click Create, enter a branch group name, and click
.
– Batch configuration
a. Choose More > Upload.
b. In the dialog box that is displayed, click and
select a file.
c. (Optional) Click to download the branch group
template and edit it on the local PC.

– Click to edit a branch group.
– Click to delete a branch group.

branch group is displayed when you set the site.
– If you select The checked branch group label is

applies the selected branch group to the new site by default.

----End
5.1.1.2 Power Use Authorization
This function enables you to control remote connection and disconnection
permissions through power use authorization, reducing economic losses.
Prerequisites
● You have the Power Use Authorization permission. For details, see User
Authorization.
● You have activated the Software Define Breaker(per branch) license (ID:
LWM5STFY). For details, see 8.2.9.5 Allocating a License.
Context
● Secondary authorization for intelligent power consumption management is
disabled by default. For details, see 8.2.7 Configuring Secondary
Authorization Operations.
● After authorization is disabled, the power-on and power-off operations are
not supported, and data collection of power consumption metering and
power consumption inspection is not supported.
● If Power on/off Status on the Remote Power On/Off menu is Power on, the
Power on/off Status of the corresponding branch will change to Power off

Step 1 Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Power Consumption Management >
Intelligent Load Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, click Power Use Authorization.
NO TE
By default, all load branches that support the power use authorization function are
displayed. You can customize the query criteria.
Task Operation
Enabling
authorization 1. Click to expand the load branches.
– Authorizing one site: Click in
the Authorized Power-On/Off column.
– Authorizing sites in a batch: Select load branches
and click Batch Authorization.
2. Confirm user permissions.
– admin user: Click Yes in the dialog box that is
displayed.
– Common user:
▪
If secondary authorization is not enabled, enter
the password of the current user in the dialog
box that is displayed and click OK.
▪
If secondary authorization is enabled, enter the
username and password for the user who has
the Secondary Authorization and Intelligent
Load Management permissions in the dialog
box that is displayed. Click OK.

Task Operation
Disabling
authorization 1. Click to expand load branches, and click
in the Authorized Power-On/Off
column.
2. Confirm user permissions.
– admin user: Click Yes in the dialog box that is
displayed.
– Common user:
▪
If secondary authorization is not enabled, enter
the password of the current user in the dialog
box that is displayed and click OK.
▪
If secondary authorization is enabled, in the
dialog box that is displayed, enter the
username and password of the account with
the Secondary Authorization Authentication
and Intelligent Load Management
permissions, and click OK.
Exporting report Click Export, select an export format, and export the
results load branch data to the local PC.
----End
5.1.1.3 Backup Slicing
This function supports template-based quick configuration of load branch
disconnection parameters and branch group information.
Prerequisites
● You have the View Intelligent Load Management and Operate Intelligent
Load Management permissions. For details, see User Authorization.
● You have activated the Intelligent load mgmt feature package (per site)
licenses (ID: LWM5SECFP). For details, see 8.2.9.5 Allocating a License. If the
licenses are excessively consumed, you must purchase for the excessively
consumed licenses before you activate a license for a newly connected load
branch.
Context
● The differentiated power backup function defines the disconnection capability
based on the load voltage, current, time, and capacity. After an AC power
failure occurs, the NMS determines the disconnection priority through circuit
breakers. For details, see Figure 5-1.

● The backup resource reserve function dynamically adjusts the capacity of
secondary loads through intelligent prediction based on the backup time and
priority principles to ensure preferential power backup for primary loads. For
details, see Figure 5-2.
Figure 5-2 Backup resource reserve
● After the backup resource reservation function is disabled, the NetEco
performs differentiated backup based on the configured backup power
connection and disconnection parameters by default.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Power Consumption Management >
Intelligent Load Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Backup Slicing.

Power Task Operation
Backup
Mode
Differen Manually set ● Single-site setting
tiated backup

5.1.1.4 Precise Metering
This function enables you to collect and view statistics on the power consumption
of load branches corresponding to different branch groups.
Prerequisites
● You have the View Intelligent Load Management permission. For details,
see User Authorization.
● Authorized Power-On/Off for load branches is set to Authorized. For details,
see 5.1.1.2 Power Use Authorization.
● You have activated the RTU for DC Branch Measurement(per branch)
license (ID: LWM5ASO00). For details, see 8.2.9.5 Allocating a License.
● You have configured the branch group. For details, see 5.1.1.1 Branch
Definition.
Context
● A branch cluster is a combination of multiple branch groups.
● After a branch cluster is set, the system can collect statistics on the power
consumption of multiple branch groups by branch cluster.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Power Consumption Management >
Intelligent Load Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, click Precise Metering.
Step 3 Set the search criteria and click Query.
● Site Details tab page: displays power consumption data of sites.
● Branch Details tab page: displays power consumption data of load branches.
NO TE
You can click Export and select an export format to export the data by site and by load
branch to the local PC.
----End

each load branch is normal.
● Authorized Power-On/Off for load branches is set to Authorized. For details,
see Power Use Authorization.
● You have activated the RTU for DC Branch Measurement(per branch)
license (ID: LWM5ASO00). For details, see 8.2.9.5 Allocating a License.
Context
● The power consumption inspection function collects data and calculates the
power of each load branch at 06:00 every day. If the device is connected after
data is collected, you can view related data after data is collected at 06:00 on
the next day.
● If the Total Energy Consumption and Energy Consumption per Hour
indicators are unavailable for a load branch, information about this load
branch will not be displayed on the power inspection list.

5.1.1.6 Remote Control
This function enables you to remotely connect or disconnect devices to avoid
losses caused by delayed disconnection and reduce the cost of onsite operations.
Prerequisites
● You have the Power On and Power Off Management permission. For details,
see User Authorization.
● Authorized Power-On/Off for load branches is set to Authorized.
Unauthorized load branches cannot be powered on or off. For details, see

Context
● If Power Supply Mode is set to Automatic Mode, the NetEco performs
power disconnection control based on the configured load parameters by
default. If Power Supply Mode is set to Manual mode or Maintain Mode,
you need to remotely and manually control power connection/disconnection.
● Power Supply Mode can be switched between Manual Mode and
Automatic Mode, and between Automatic Mode and Maintain Mode, but
cannot be switched between Maintain Mode and Manual Mode.
● If Power Supply Mode is set to Automatic Mode and the load branch
supports Manual Mode, this parameter changes to Manual Mode after you
perform power connection/disconnection operations. If the load branch does
not support Manual Mode, this parameter changes to Maintain Mode.
● Secondary authorization for intelligent power consumption management is
disabled by default. For details about how to enable secondary authorization,
see 8.2.7 Configuring Secondary Authorization Operations.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Power Consumption Management >
Intelligent Load Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, click Remote Power On/Off.

see User Authorization.
● AC meters with AC channels have been connected.

Intelligent Load Management.

Step 3 Set the search criteria and click Query.

5.1.2 Site Energy Analysis
This section describes how to analyze the site energy for you to learn the status of
site energy statistics and energy consumption.
5.1.2.1 Viewing Site Energy
This section describes how to view site energy in the NetEco, including site
efficiency and consumption statistics, thereby learning site energy status in time.
Prerequisites
● You are authorized to use the Energy Analysis function.
● Activated Intelligent O&M Feature package (per site) license (ID:
LWM5IOMFP). For details, see 8.2.9.5 Allocating a License.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Power Consumption Management >
Energy Analysis.
Step 2 Select the site in the left pane to view Site Efficiency in the right pane.
● You can set Period to Month or Year to view Site Efficiency.
● Value of site energy efficiency SEE and energy consumption statistics are
displayed in the Site Efficiency area.

NO TE
The NE reports the energy consumption data. The NetEco displays SEE after calculation.
The calculation formulas are as follows:
SEE (%) = Energy consumption of telecom equipment/Total input of site energy x 100%
Total input of site energy = Mains power supply + DG power supply + PV power supply +
Power supply of other unknown energy sources. If the energy consumption of telecom
equipment cannot be collected, the SEE is calculated approximately by dividing the total
DC output energy consumption of the power supply by the total input of site energy. The
SEE calculated using this method is larger than the actual value if there is a DC air
conditioner.
----End
5.1.2.2 Analyzing Site Energy
This section describes how to analyze the site energy for you to obtain analysis
data and optimization suggestions about temperature control and PSU energy
consumption.
Prerequisites
● You are authorized to use the Energy Analysis function.
● Activated Intelligent O&M Feature package (per site) license (ID:
LWM5IOMFP). For details, see 8.2.9.5 Allocating a License.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Power Consumption Management >
Energy Analysis.
Step 2 Select the site in the left pane and view analysis data of energy consumption in
the Details area in the right pane.
● View analysis data of energy consumption for temperature control.
Click Analysis for Temperature Control in the left pane to view analysis data
of energy consumption for temperature control in the line chart in the right
pane.
To view data of a specific day or month, you can click the point corresponding
to the day or month in the line chart, and then place the pointer on the point
in the yyyy-mm-dd Details or yyyy-mm Details line chart to view data of
this day or month.
● View analysis data of PSU energy consumption.
Click Analysis for Power Module in the left pane to view analysis data of
PSU energy consumption in the line chart in the right pane.
To view data of a specific day or month, you can click the point corresponding
to the day or month in the line chart, and then place the pointer on the point
in the yyyy-mm-dd Details or yyyy-mm Details line chart to view data of
this day or month.

By viewing site optimization suggestions, you can view the site power usage
effectiveness (PUE) and state of health (SOH) of batteries. The system provides
the corresponding optimization suggestions based on the site PUE and battery
SOH to help users improve the investment efficiency and facilitate continuous
optimization.
Prerequisites
● The site is properly connected to the NetEco.
● You are authorized to use the Energy Analysis function.
● Activated Intelligent O&M Feature package (per site) license (ID:
LWM5IOMFP). For details, see 8.2.9.5 Allocating a License.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Power Consumption Management >
Energy Analysis.
Step 2 Select the root node, a subnet or a site in the left pane, click Optimize.
Step 3 View the optimization propose of SEE.
● The proportion of common rectifier modules on the entire network is
displayed on the left of the Site Energy Efficiency Optimization Suggestion
tab page.
NO TE
Rectifier modules whose rated conversion efficiency is lower than 98% are common
rectifier modules.
● Optimization suggestions for all sites on the entire network are displayed on
the right of the Site Energy Efficiency Optimization Suggestion tab page.
– You can filter optimization suggestions by Site Name and Site Efficiency.
– You can export the site PUE optimization suggestions in CSV or Excel
format.
Step 4 View the optimization propose of battery SOH.
● The proportion of sites whose SOH is Subhealthy and Abnormal is displayed
on the left of the Battery SOH Optimization Suggestion tab page.
● Optimization suggestions for the SOH of batteries on the entire network are
displayed on the right of the Battery SOH Optimization Suggestion tab
page.
– You can filter optimization suggestions by Site Name and Battery
Health Status.
NO TE
You can set Battery Health Status to Subhealthy or Abnormal.
– You can export the battery SOH optimization suggestions in CSV or Excel
format.

5.2 Energy Saving Management
This section describes the features related to energy saving management.
5.2.1 A/C Energy Saving Management
By monitoring the temperature of each device in real time and properly adjusting
the temperature of the air conditioners, the energy consumption of the air
conditioners is reduced, thereby reducing the energy consumption of the entire
site.
Prerequisites
● You have operation rights of viewing and configuring air conditioner energy
saving management.
● Synergistic Cooling with RAN(per site) is activated for a site that supports
peak shaving, For details, see 8.2.9.5 Allocating a License.
● The wireless has been configured. For details, see 8.1.4 BTS Synergy
Configuration.
● The BBU power consumption counter subscription function has been enabled
on MAE.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Energy Saving Management > A/C
Energy Saving Management.
Step 2 Associate wireless base stations.
● Batch association: Click Import Associations in Batches. In the displayed
dialog box, click Download Template, fill in the association relationships, and
upload the template. After the upload is successful, click OK.
● Single association: Click in the Operation column of the target data. In
the displayed dialog box, select the wireless OSS and then select the
corresponding wireless base station. Click or to move
the selected wireless base station or all wireless base stations to the
Associated Wireless Base Station area, and click OK.
NO TE
You can click a value in the BBU Count column to view.
Step 3 Configure energy saving parameters.
NO TE
Energy saving parameters can be configured if the site communication is normal.
● Batch configuration: Click Config Energy Saving Param in Batches. In the

algorithm, and click OK.
● Single configuration: Click in the Operation column of the target data,
configure energy saving parameters, enabling or disabling the temperature
control algorithm, and click OK.
NO TE
Click in the Operation column of the target data to view Temperature Trend
and Energy Consumption Trend of the base stations associated with the specified site
within a specified period. You can also click Export to export the data to the local PC.
Step 4 Click or , Enabling or Disabling the energy saving algorithm
of the Air Conditioner.
NO TE
Click Export to export the site data to the local PC.
----End
5.3 Intelligent Power Consumption
This section describes features related to intelligent power consumption.
5.3.1 Intelligent Peak Shaving
The intelligent peak shaving function limits the output power of the power system
based on the AC input mains capacity or circuit breaker capacity to ensure that
the input power of the power system does not exceed the AC capacity. This
prevents load breakdown caused by heavy load and improves the load availability.
When the output power of the power system is insufficient to supply power to
loads, the lithium battery automatically supplies power. In this way, the AC input
does not need to be changed.
Prerequisites
● You have the operation rights for Intelligent Peak Shaving.
● Intelligent Peak Shaving of AC Input (per site) is activated for a site that
supports peak shaving, For details, see 8.2.9.5 Allocating a License.
● When simplified and other third-party power systems are connected in
parallel, peak shaving cannot be started or shut down properly.

CA UTION
The power supply to the site may be affected if you set the following parameters
on the NetEco: DG startup/shutdown, air conditioner startup/shutdown, LLVD/
BLVD voltage, AC circuit breaker capacity, mains capacity, load power-on/off
control, battery power-on/off control, HVDC/PSU/SSU/MIMO/24 V module
startup/shutdown, DG capacity, and PSU power limit.
This section describes the parameters related to the intelligent peak shaving and
peak shaving curves.
Table 5-2 Parameters related to intelligent peak shaving
Parameter Description
Expandable Load Capacity - Week/ Minimum remaining capacity after
Month/Year peak shaving in the past seven days,
30 days, or 365 days.
Battery Capacity for Peak Shaving - Maximum battery capacity required for
Week/Month/Year peak shaving in the past seven days,
30 days, or 365 days.
Table 5-3 Parameters related to peak shaving curves
Parameter Description
AC Power With Peak Statistical data of the mains power signal when the
Shaving Function peak shaving function is enabled.
AC Power Without Peak Statistical data of the mains power signal when the
Shaving Function peak shaving function is not enabled.

Parameter Description
Mains Power Limit Mains Power Limit = Min (AC capacity kW: rated AC
phase voltage V x rated AC circuit breaker current A/
1000 x Number of phases) x AC derating coefficient.
Ensure that the input power of the power system
does not exceed the mains power limit. In peak
shaving mode, if the input power exceeds the mains
power limit for a long time, the power supply of the
power system may be risky and the circuit breaker
may trip.
NOTE
● When the mains AC system is dual–live wire or single-
phase, the number of phases is 1.
● When the mains AC system is three-phase four-wire or
three-phase three-wire, the number of phases is 3.
● Sampling time of the mains power limit: when the peak
shaving function is enabled, at 03:00 every day, or when
the configuration signal is changed.
Total Load Power Statistics on DC output power distribution
Charge/Discharge Power Statistics on the charge and discharge power of a
lithium battery group
Expandable Load Expandable load capacity = Remaining battery
Capacity capacity for peak shaving/Duration for canceling
peak shaving. A larger value of Expandable Load
Capacity indicates a larger load supported by the
batteries for peak shaving.
Baseline AC utilization baseline, which is configurable.
NOTE
On the Reference Curve for Adding Loads tab page, click
Baseline Threshold Setting. After the setting, the
reference line is displayed in the curve chart for
comparison.
AC Utilization AC utilization = Accumulated mains power
consumption in the last seven days/Total hours/
Limited value of the average mains power in the last
seven days. A larger value of AC Utilization indicates
a lower mains usage.

5.3.2 Staggering Electricity Usage Management
In areas where the price difference between power consumption peaks and valleys
(if any) is huge, the staggering feature can scientifically schedule the power
supply of the mains and backup batteries. In the valley period, the power grid
energy is used to supply power to devices and fully charge batteries. When the
price is high, the battery energy is preferentially used to supply power to devices
to minimize electricity fees. This section describes the complete enabling and
O&M process of the staggering feature on the NetEco.
5.3.2.1 Understanding Staggering Electricity Usage
You can view the overview statistics to learn about the running status of
staggering electricity usage.
Prerequisite
You have the operation rights for Staggering Electricity Usage and Intelligent
Maintenance.

need to enable the electricity saving setting. For details, see the prerequisites in
5.3.2.5 Viewing the Peak Staggering Revenue Report.
Context
To help users fully understand the overall effect and running status of staggering
electricity usage, the overview function collects key data such as revenue data,
alarm information, and staggering planning data, and provides intuitive service
insights through multi-dimensional statistics analysis and visualized display.
Procedure
1. Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Intelligent Power Consumption >
Staggering Electricity Usage Management.
2. Choose Overview from the navigation pane.
Task Description
Revenue data/ Collects statistics on the peak staggering electricity
Charged and usage and revenue trend based on the selected site.
discharged Collects statistics by day, month, and year.
energy
NOTE
If Saved Fees Setting is disabled, the charged and discharged
energy statistics are displayed.
Lithium battery Collects statistics on lithium battery strings, current
alarm statistics alarms, and new alarms by site, and displays the
statistics by alarm severity.
Top N sites with Displays top N sites in descending order of the
abnormal difference between the theoretical revenue and actual
revenue revenue.
yesterday
Peak staggering Displays the total number of sites where peak

This section describes the data to be prepared and information to be obtained

5.3.2.2.1 Electricity Price Table
For a new site, you need to fill in the electricity price template in advance based
on the local electricity price. The template will be imported to the system later.
Download the Electricity_Price_Template.xlsm. For details about how to set the
electricity price template, see Table 5-5.
For an existing site, the system can directly synchronize the electricity price
information from other sites. For details, see 5.3.2.3.4 Manually Importing an
Electricity Price File.

5.3.2.3.2 Accessing the Staggering Feature
This section describes the staggering function and WebUI layout, helping you
quickly get familiar with the WebUI.
Prerequisites
● You have the operation rights for Staggering Electricity Usage and
Intelligent O&M.
● Device access requirements:
– Power Staggering: The lithium batteries, mains, power supply, and SMU
have been connected to the NetEco by site.
– Li-Self Staggering: The IoT and SCC800 of the third-party power systems
have been connected to the NetEco by site.
Procedure
Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Intelligent Power Consumption >
Staggering Electricity Usage Management. The Staggering Electricity Usage
page is displayed.
Figure 5-4 and Table 5-8 describe the layout of the main page.

NO TE
● The Saved Fees and Electricity Fees Trend areas visible only to users with the
Administrator permission.
● Users with the Administrator permission can set Saved Fees and Electricity Fees Trend
in the Saved Fees Setting area by choosing System Configuration > Site Business
Settings.
Figure 5-4 WebUI layout
Table 5-8 WebUI functions
N Area Description
o.
1 Search bar Allows you to query the list of staggering sites by
filtering sites and periods.
2 Staggered Displays the number of sites for which staggering is
planning statistics enabled and that for which staggering is disabled. You
chart can click Staggering enabled and Staggering
disabled to filter sites.
3 Saved fee statistics Displays the total saved electricity fees within the
chart search period. You can click Staggering enabled and
Staggering disabled to filter sites and view how
much money is saved after power staggering and Li-
self staggering are enabled.
4 Staggered battery Displays the total capacity of the batteries with power
statistics chart staggering and self-staggering enabled. You can click
Power Staggering and Self Staggering to view the
battery capacity.

N Area Description
o.
5 Electricity fees Displays the trends of daily electricity fees saved by
trend chart power staggering and Li-self staggering. You can view
the hourly mains energy yield of each day in the last
month to check whether the staggering function is
working properly.
6 Function Allows users to import an electricity price template,
operations set sites in batches, view operation details, send
notifications by email, and set exception rules.
7 Staggering Displays detailed staggering data of a site. You can
electricity usage view the staggered battery list. You can enable or
list disable staggering, set staggering parameters, and
view real-time and historical staggering line graphs in
the Operation column.
NOTE
● Real-time staggering details: Real-time data is collected in
real time.
● Historical staggering details: The historical signal data is
collected at a period of 5 minutes.

5.3.2.3.4 Manually Importing an Electricity Price File
This section describes how to set an electricity price template. You can set the
peak and valley electricity prices in the electricity price template. After the
template is imported to the system, the mains and battery power supply can be
automatically scheduled.
Prerequisites
For a new site area, prepare the electricity price table in advance.
Procedure
1. Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Intelligent Power Consumption >
Staggering Electricity Usage Management. The Staggering Electricity
Usage page is displayed.
2. In the upper right corner of the Staggering Electricity Usage page, click
Electricity Price Template. The Electricity Price Template dialog box is
displayed. For details about the electricity price template, see Figure 5-5.
Figure 5-5 Electricity price template
3. Set an electricity price table and deliver it to sites.
– If an electricity price file is available for the KPIs reported by a site, you
can directly synchronize the data to other sites in batches.
i. Click Synchronize, select desired sites, and click OK. You can view
the obtained site electricity price template in the Electricity Price

ii. Click in the Operation column that contains the electricity price
template.
iii. Select desired sites and click OK.
– View the operation result.
▪
A dialog box is displayed, indicating that the operation is successful.
▪
A dialog box is displayed indicating that the operation is failed, click
Task Details.
If the message The charging rate in the imported template is
greater than the charging current limit coefficient of the lithium
battery group at the site is displayed, modify the charge current
limit coefficient by referring to the following instructions.
1) Choose Device Management > Device View > View.
2) In the navigation pane, choose a lithium battery string under the
site.
3) Click the Configuration tab page.
4) In the Charge Parameters area, view the charge current limit
coefficient.
– If no electricity price file is available for the KPIs reported by sites, you
can edit the blank electricity price template and deliver the template to
sites in batches.
i. Click Add and click Download Template to download a blank
electricity price template.
ii. Fill in the electricity price template.
iii. Upload the electricity price template that has been filled in, and click
OK.
iv. Click in the Operation column that contains the electricity price
template.
v. Select desired sites and click OK.
– View the operation result.
▪
A dialog box is displayed, indicating that the operation is successful.
▪
A dialog box is displayed indicating that the operation is failed, click
Task Details.
If the message The charging rate in the imported template is
greater than the charging current limit coefficient of the lithium
battery group at the site is displayed, modify the charge current
limit coefficient by referring to the following instructions.
1) Choose Device Management > Device View > View.
2) In the navigation pane, choose a lithium battery string under the
site.
3) Click the Configuration tab page.
4) In the Charge Parameters area, view the charge current limit

The NetEco connects to the electricity price website to obtain the daily dynamic
electricity price. The system formulates an electricity price policy based on the
obtained electricity price and delivers the policy to the site to implement
automatic scheduling of mains and battery power supply.
Prerequisites
Dynamic electricity price applies only to the power staggering scenario.
Procedure
1. Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Intelligent Power Consumption >
Staggering Electricity Usage Management. The Staggering Electricity
Usage page is displayed.
2. In the upper right corner of the Staggering Electricity Usage page, click
More and choose Dynamic Electricity Prices.
Figure 5-6 Apply Dynamic Electricity Prices
3. In the Apply Dynamic Electricity Prices dialog box, select the site to be
applied and click OK.
NO TE
If an electricity price template has been configured for the selected site, the original
electricity price template will be overwritten after the dynamic electricity price is
applied.

5.3.2.3.7 Setting a Staggering Policy
This section describes how to set staggering parameters. You can set proper
charge and discharge policies to achieve peak staggering.

Peak staggering applies to these three scenarios: lithium batteries only, lead-acid
batteries only, and hybrid use of lithium and lead-acid batteries. In the hybrid use
scenario, staggering applies only to lithium batteries.
Procedure
Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Intelligent Power Consumption >
Staggering Electricity Usage Management. The Staggering Electricity Usage
page is displayed. On the Staggering Electricity Usage page, you can perform the
following operations.
Task Procedure
Setting staggering parameters one by
1. Click in the Operation column
one
of the row that contains a desired
site.
2. On the Staggering Parameters
Settings page, set parameters. For
details about how to set parameters
for different staggering types, see
Table 5-9 and Table 5-10.
3. Click OK.
Setting staggering parameters in 1. In the upper right corner of the
batches Staggering Electricity Usage page,
click Batch Set.
2. On the Power Staggering or Li-
Self Staggering tab page, select a
site and set staggering parameters.
For details about how to set
parameters for different staggering
types, see Table 5-9 and Table
5-10.
3. Click OK.

5.3.2.4 Monitoring
This section describes how to monitor the running status of the feature and how
to locate and handle common exceptions.

Procedure
1. Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Intelligent Power Consumption >
Staggering Electricity Usage Management. The Staggering Electricity
Usage page is displayed.
2. Check whether the electricity fees trend chart in the upper part of the
Staggering Electricity Usage page is properly displayed when the feature is
enabled and whether the daily, weekly, and monthly saved electricity fees
meet customer's expectation. These results help determine whether the
staggering function is running properly.
NO TE
Only the admin user can view the electricity fees trend chart.
Figure 5-10 Electricity fees trend
3. In the site list on the Staggering Electricity Usage page, select a site for
which staggering is enabled and click to view the historical staggering data
chart. In the chart, click Export to view details about the staggering data
report.
4. View and analyze KPIs in real-time and historical data charts to check
whether the enabled feature meets the customer's expectation.
NO TE
● Query the electricity fees trend chart by filtering dates in the historical data chart.
● In the historical data chart, click Export to export the staggering data to the local
PC as Excel file.
– View the historical power staggering data chart and compare the saved
electricity fees by day, week, month, and year to learn about the benefits
of the staggering feature.

NO TE
The reserved SOC and predicted power curves are not displayed in the PVPU+third-
party power supply scenario.

This section describes the function and WebUI layout of the peak staggering
benefit report, helping you quickly get familiar with the WebUI.
Context
The peak staggering benefit report helps users clearly track the benefit
performance in different time dimensions. This report displays the benefit details
of the site at the time granularity in a day, month, or year. You can set the benefit
proportion and benefit exception warning threshold (an exception is considered if
the benefit is equal to or lower than the threshold). The report provides data
support for benefit analysis and risk monitoring.
Prerequisite
● You have the operation rights for Staggering Electricity Usage and
Intelligent Maintenance.
● Lithium batteries have been connected.
● You have enabled Saved Fees Setting. The procedure is as follows:
a. Choose System > System Settings > System Configuration.
b. In the navigation pane, choose Site Business Settings.
c. On the Saved Fees Setting tab, set Display Saved Fees to Yes.
d. Click Apply.
Procedure
1. Choose Energy Efficiency Management > Intelligent Power Consumption >
Staggering Electricity Usage Management.
2. In the navigation pane, choose Staggering Revenue Report. The Staggering
Revenue Report page is displayed.
Table 5-17 WebUI functions
Function Description
Viewing the Collects statistics on the charged and discharged
staggering energy, sites with abnormal revenue, and staggering
revenue report electricity usage list, and displays the statistics from
different dimensions, helping users learn about the
detailed staggering revenue information.
● Charged and discharged energy statistics chart:
compares the theoretical and actual charged and
discharged energy.
● Abnormal revenue site statistics chart: compares the
theoretical revenue with the actual revenue.
● Staggering revenue report: displays detailed data of
staggering information at sites. You can view the
staggering revenue report. For details about the

Function Description
Setting the Set the revenue proportion based on site requirements.
revenue The default value is 100%.
proportion
Setting revenue If the actual revenue/theoretical revenue of a site is
threshold equal to or less than the revenue warning threshold,
configuration the revenue of the site is abnormal.
Exporting data You can export data of one or more sites.
Table 5-18 Parameters in the staggering revenue report
Parameter Description
Theoretical Charged Calculated charged energy based on the lithium
Energy battery parameters.
Theoretical Calculated discharged energy based on the lithium
Discharged Energy battery parameters.
Actual Charged Actual charged energy of the device.
Energy
Actual Discharged Actual discharged energy of the device.
Energy
Theoretical Saved Calculated theoretical saved electricity fee based on
Electricity Fee the theoretical charge/discharge capacity and
electricity price template.
Actual Saved Actual electricity fee saved by the device.
Electricity Fee
Theoretical Revenue Theoretical electricity fee saving x Revenue ratio
Actual Revenue Actual electricity fee saving x Revenue ratio
Fulfillment Rate Actual revenue/Theoretical revenue x 100%
Revenue Warning If the fulfillment rate is equal to or lower than the
revenue warning threshold, an exception is
displayed. Otherwise, no exception is displayed.
NOTE
● If the revenue warning threshold is not configured, N/A
is displayed.
● If the theoretical revenue is 0, N/A is displayed.

6
Maintenance Management
This section describes O&M operations.
6.1 Security Protection
The NetEco provides functions such as access control management, camera
management, and device ilock to ensure physical safety of devices and
environment and facilitate users to detect and handle exceptions in a timely
manner.
6.1.1 Access Control Management
The access control management function allows you to configure and manage
access controllers, access users, and access groups. The NetEco supports multiple
authentication modes (such as card, password, fingerprint, and combinations of
the preceding modes) and monitors access events to control and audit access
permissions and improve the security management capability for each
management domain.
6.1.1.1 Managing Access Control Devices
After an access control device is created, you can set its authentication mode and
door parameters and deliver permissions to effectively manage the access control
device.
6.1.1.1.1 Managing Door Lock Information
After a door lock is created, you can set the authentication mode, initialize the
access control system, deliver permissions, remotely open the door, and change
the padlock binding relationship for the door lock to effectively manage the door
lock.
Prerequisites

Lock.
● The padlock mediation package has been installed.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Security Protection > Access Control
Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Door Lock Information Management. Then,
perform the following operations based on the scenario.

6.1.1.1.2 Managing a Bluetooth Padlock
You can manage electronic key information, electronic key software packages, and
secret keys.
Prerequisites
● The root key has been set. For details, see 6.1.1.2.1 Setting the Bluetooth
Lock.
● You have the operation rights for Bluetooth Padlock Management.

You can set the device time, send SMS messages, and approve workflows.

6.1.1.2.2 Setting SMS Messages
The SMS function is used to sent unlock passwords in the motor lock scenario or
send offline dynamic codes in the scenario of a padlock with a key.
Prerequisites
Before using the SMS message sending function, you need to configure the SMS
server. For details, see 8.1.3.2.1 Commissioning SMS Server Notifications.
Procedure
1. Choose Maintenance Management > Security Protection > Access Control
Management.
2. In the navigation pane on the left, choose Initialize > SMS Settings.
3. In the SMS Settings area, set Automatic Sending and Sending Time, and
click Apply.

● You have the operation rights for Global Settings.

6.1.1.3 Managing Access Groups
You can create an access group and assign operation permissions to the access
control device based on the authorization plan. In addition, you can modify or
delete an access group and deliver the access group data to the access control
device.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Access Control Management and View Access

Access users created on the ECC800-Pro can be synchronized to the NetEco
automatically, and an access group can be created automatically after the
synchronization.
● Access users created on the ECC800-Pro that are authorized, after being
synchronized to the NetEco, can automatically create an access group named
after Access Controller Location/Collector Name/User Name_UserID.
● Access users created on the ECC800-Pro that are not authorized, after being
synchronized to the NetEco, belong to Untreated group automatically.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Security Protection > Access Control
Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane on the left, choose Access Group and perform the
following operations as required.

6.1.1.5 Managing Access Users
You can create an access user based on the permission plan and add the user to
the access group to complete user authorization. In addition, you can modify and
delete an access user.
Prerequisites
● You have the operation rights for User Management.
● The fingerprint controller has been bound to the door access controller.
● If the fingerprint controller needs to be connected in USB mode, ensure that
the USB card reader and USB fingerprint reader have been inserted into the
USB ports on the NetEco client. In addition, ensure that you have installed the
device driver of the fingerprint reader and card issuer. For details, see 9.16
How Do I Install the Device Driver of the Fingerprint Reader and Card
Issuer?.
● If the fingerprint controller needs to be connected in TCP mode, ensure that
the fingerprint controller has been connected to the access controller and
ECC800-Pro.
● You have created the access groups. For details, see 6.1.1.3 Managing Access
Groups.
Context
● If users need to enter their personal data such as phone numbers and email

the user privacy policies of your company.
● This function needs to collect privacy information such as the position, gender,
employee ID, phone number, department, email address, identity certificate,
validity period of the identity certificate, validity period of the security
certificate, card number, fingerprint, labor insurance period, and avatar.
Privacy data is virtualized on the NetEco, and is encrypted during batch
transmission in the NetEco to ensure the security of personal data.
● To support the recording of user's fingerprint information, the bound access
controller must be in single-card mode.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Security Protection > Access Control
Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane on the left, choose User Management. Perform the
following operations as required.

n
Creating You can create an ● Creating a single access user

● After an access user is created successfully,
Card Number is anonymized based on the
following rules:
– If the card number is not set, no value
is displayed.
– If the card number contains fewer than
4 characters, all the characters are
displayed as *.
– If the card number contains 4 to 6
characters, the first three characters are
displayed as * and other characters are
displayed as digits.
– If the card number contains 7 to 10
characters, the last four characters are
displayed as digits and other characters
are displayed as *.

users in the NetEco. For system users, only
the user name and user group information
are displayed in the list.

method can only perform unlocking using

the NetEco app.

fingerprint accessed by swiping a card or fingerprint.
collection
1. Click .
2. In the displayed Card Number & Fingerprint Collection
dialog box, set Port and perform the following operations
as required:
– Collecting the card number: Click Collect next to Card
No, and swipe the card on the corresponding USB card
reader to record the card number information.
– Collecting fingerprints: When a USB fingerprint reader
is properly connected, enter Password, and click
Collect to record users' fingerprint information. You
need to record the same fingerprint three times within
30 seconds.
NOTE
– Password: The password of the fingerprint reader is
8888888888 by default.
– To view the serial port number on Windows 10, perform the
following steps:
1. On the system desktop of the PC, right-click the This PC
icon and choose Properties from the pop-up menu.
2. On the System page, click Device Manager.
3. In the Device Manager dialog box, choose Ports
(COM&LPT) to check the serial port number connected to
the device.
3. Record the user fingerprint information and click Save.
After user fingerprint information is successfully recorded,
you can check the number of recorded fingerprints in the
Fingerprint Count column of the Access Users tab page.
Card number You can set the card number in the following ways:
● Manually enter the card number.
● After you swipe your card on the USB card reader, the card
number is displayed automatically.
Password Set this parameter when the access control system is
accessed using a password.
NOTE
Password entered by a user is available only when the access control
device is an access controller.
