You can query the access records and event records of a specified access control
device within a specified time range, and export the query results to a PC for
storage.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Access Control Management.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Security Protection > Access Control
Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane on the left, choose Access Event .
Step 3 By default, the access records or event records of all access control devices within
24 hours on the current day are displayed. You can also set the search criteria and
perform the search operation.
● Access Records: users' door opening/closing records on the device side
● Event Records: all event records related to devices, including door opening/
closing records and door open alarm records
NO TE
● To export access records or event records, click Export.
● If the event records on the NetEco are inconsistent with those on the device, you can
click Synchronize Log Information to synchronize the log information on the device to
the NetEco.
● For card swiping on an access controller, the card number, user name, and user ID are
recorded for a valid card, and only the card number is recorded for an invalid card.
● For card swiping on a motor lock, the user name and user ID are recorded for a valid
card, and only the card number is recorded for an invalid card.
----End
6.1.2 Camera Management
Camera management includes camera management, group management, and
parameter configuration.

You can create and manage cameras and video surveillance subsystem devices.

6.1.2.1.2 Video Surveillance Subsystem
You can create, modify, and delete video surveillance subsystems. After a video
surveillance subsystem is created and connected, the camera information in the
video surveillance subsystem is automatically synchronized to the NetEco.
Prerequisites
● You have the operation rights for Camera Management.
● You have uploaded and install related adapter packages on the Adapter
Management page. For details, see 3.3.2.4 Adapter Management.
● Cameras have been added to the video surveillance subsystem.
● You have obtained the IP address, port number, user name, and password for
logging in to the client of the video surveillance subsystem.
● The NetEco needs to have browse and query permissions. Please configure the
video system users with browse and query permissions.
● You are advised to use a new user created in the video surveillance subsystem
to connect to the cameras. Before the connection, ensure that the initial
password of the user has been changed and the user has successfully logged
in to the video surveillance subsystem.
● You are advised to set the number of login clients by the user on the video
surveillance subsystem to a value greater than 5. Otherwise, when the user
has logged in to the video surveillance subsystem, the user cannot connect to

Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Security Protection > Camera
Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Video Resources > Video Surveillance
Subsystem, and perform the following operations as required.

V100R002C50 or a later version.

6.1.2.2 Video Playback
You can view real-time videos of devices and play back videos of video
surveillance subsystem devices to monitor sites.

You can record suspicious images and alarm-triggered images, take snapshots, and
view real-time videos to monitor sites for visualized site management.
Prerequisites
● You have the operation rights for Camera Management.
● Video streaming media service has been set to ON and
DPLiveStreamService has been restarted.

Only cameras managed by the video subsystem support the recording playback
function. You can view video that is manually recorded and video that is
automatically recorded by the client of the video surveillance subsystem when an
associated alarm is generated.

● A recorded video is available.
● Before playing videos from cameras managed by the video surveillance
subsystem, you need to set video playback parameters.

6.1.2.3 Video Linkage Management
After you set a linkage relationship between alarms and video, when an alarm is
generated based on the video linkage rule, the NetEco automatically invokes the
associated camera to view live video or play back recorded video. You can receive
video linkage messages through Live Video Notification. You can also view live
video or play back recorded video on the Current Alarms, Historical Alarms, or
Interaction Log page.
6.1.2.3.1 Managing Video Linkage Rules
Set the linkage relationship between alarms and videos. After a video linkage rule
is created and enabled, the NetEco automatically invokes the associated camera to
play the live video or recorded video when an alarm in the video linkage rule is
generated.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Linkage Configuration.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Security Protection > Camera
Management.
Step 2 In the navigation tree, choose Video linkage > Linkage Configuration.
Step 3 On the Linkage Configuration page, perform related operations as required.

condition.

condition details column.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
If a video linkage rule is enabled, you can perform the following operations based
on the site requirements after the corresponding alarm is generated:
● On the Linkage Log tab page, view video linkage logs.
● On the Current Alarms or Historical Alarms page, click Recording playback
or Live video in the Video Messages column to view live video.

video through the Interaction Log function after the alarm specified in the video
linkage rule is generated.

● A video linkage rule has been created and enabled. For details, see 6.1.2.3.1
Managing Video Linkage Rules.
Procedure

Management.
Step 2 In the navigation tree, choose Video linkage > Interaction Log.
Step 3 On the Interaction Log tab page, set search criteria and click Query.
To view live video or play back recorded video, click Recording playback or Live
video in the Associated Action column.

You can enable or disable the video linkage message dialog box. After the video
linkage message dialog box is enabled, the Video Linkage Temporary Message
icon is displayed on all pages except the Alarm Management page and the video
linkage message is received.
Prerequisites
● You have the operation rights for Live Video Notification.
● A video linkage rule has been created and enabled. For details, see 6.1.2.3.1
Managing Video Linkage Rules.
Context
Live Video Notification applies only to the current login user.
Procedure

Management.
Step 2 In the navigation tree, choose Video linkage > Live Video Notification.
Step 3 On the Live Video Notification tab page, activate or disable the video linkage
message dialog box and click Apply.
----End

You can set the linkage relationship between alarms, image capturing, and video
recording. When an alarm is generated for a device, the NetEco automatically
invokes the camera associated with the alarm to capture images or record videos,
and sends images to the recipients set in the associated alarm by email.

Subsystem.
● Communication is normal between cameras and the NetEco.

6.1.3 Site Device iLock
Device iLock includes PV iLock, battery iLock, and rectifier iLock. Enable or disable

6.1.3.1 PV iLock
Set the solar iLock enabling status. If the PV module communication fails for a
specified period of time, anti-theft lock will be triggered. The PV module cannot
be directly used after being stolen, reducing the theft rate.
Prerequisites
You have the operation permissions for Site Device iLock, Enable Lock and
Disable Lock.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Security Protection > Device iLock.
Step 2 In the navigation pane on the left, click PV iLock.
Step 3 Enable or disable the PV iLock.
● Batch operation: Click Set iLock Parameters. In the displayed Set iLock
Parameters dialog box, set parameters and click OK.
● Single device operation: Click in the Operation column of the target data.
In the displayed Set iLock Parameters dialog box, set parameters and click
OK.
NO TE
Choose System > System Management > License Management from the main
menu. After the license is deactivated on the Allocate License page, Enable iLock is
set to No.
----End
6.1.3.2 Battery iLock
After you have set Enable iLock to Enable, if a lithium battery string fails to
communicate for a specified period of time or the lithium battery string is moved,
anti-theft lock will be triggered. As a result, the lithium battery string cannot be
used, reducing the battery theft rate.
Prerequisites
You have the rights for Site Device iLock, Enable Lock, and Disable Lock.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Security Protection > Device iLock.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, click Battery iLock and perform operations as required.

6.1.3.3 Rectifier iLock
If the rectifier iLock is enabled, the anti-theft locking is triggered when a rectifier
fails to communicate for a specified period of time. As a result, the rectifier cannot
be used, reducing the rectifier theft rate.
Prerequisites
You have the rights for Site Device iLock, Enable Lock, and Disable Lock.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Security Protection > Device iLock.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, click Rectifier iLock.
Step 3 Enable or disable an iLock.
● Batch operation: Click Set iLock Parameters. In the Set iLock Parameters
dialog box, set the parameters and click OK.
● Single operation: Click in the Operation column of the row that contains
the target data record. In the Set iLock Parameters dialog box, set the
parameters and click OK.
----End
6.1.3.4 Offline unlock tool
If a device is locked and cannot be unlocked on the NetEco, you can use the
offline unlock tool to generate an offline unlocking code to unlock the device.

● The original unlock code of the module, that is, the ESN of the device, has
been queried.
● You have the rights for Edit Customer Code.
● The customer code has been set on the Site Business Settings page and
delivered to the device.
● A protocol converter is required when you use the app to unlock the device.
Context
Online lock: You need to connect devices to the NetEco, and the NetEco monitors
the lock status.
Offline lock: Devices do not need to connect to the NetEco.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Security Protection > Device iLock.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, click Offline unlock tool.
Step 3 Set Module Unlocking Original Code, Customer Code, and Description, and
click Generate Unlocking Code.
Step 4 After an unlock code is generated, use the unlock code on the site app to unlock
the device offline. For details, contact technical support engineers.
----End
6.2 Intelligent Maintenance
This section describes how to perform intelligent site O&M.
6.2.1 Power Supply Dashboard
6.2.1.1 Real-time Power Supply Analysis
The Real-time Power Supply Analysis page provides the real-time power supply
and distribution statistics of the entire network and identifies the maximum
backup power capability and remaining backup power capability of batteries
based on the maximum discharge capability in the history, helping O&M
personnel prevent site breakdown.
Prerequisites
● You have the Power Supply Dashboard and Report Management
permissions.

Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Intelligent Management > Power Supply
Dashboard.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Real-time Power Supply Analysis.
Step 3 Click in the upper right corner of the page to set the indicators to be displayed.
Step 4 On the Analysis List page, view the real-time information about the site power
supply status.
Step 5 Click in the Operation column to view the power supply and distribution
details of the current site controller.
----End
6.2.1.2 Hybrid Power Supply Dashboard
Power supply dashboard provides a unified entry for users to query hybrid power
supply and consumption. Users can quickly obtain the running status of hybrid
power plants and query the running status of sites with one click. Key site
information is displayed in a table.
Prerequisites
● You have the Power Supply Dashboard and Report Management
permissions.
● Data must have been summarized in Report Management.
● To use the range comparison function, apply for the license control item
Hybrid Dashboard Range Comparison and import the license to the system.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Intelligent Management > Power Supply
Dashboard.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Hybrid Power Supply Dashboard.
Step 3 In the upper left corner, select the scenario to be queried from the Scenario drop-
down list.
Step 4 In the upper left corner, select the site to be queried from the Site drop-down list
and click OK.
Step 5 Set the query period and click Query.
The page displays the total power supply, power supply duration, fuel
consumption details, and genset running duration distribution of the site in charts.
Step 6 In the report area in the lower part, click in the Operation column of the site.
On the Site Dispatch Detail page that is displayed, query the historical data of
this site.
Step 7 Click in the Operation column of the site to view the site range comparison

2. Set the period, comparison time, and click Query.
3. Switch to a counter and view the comparison data between sites under this
counter.
4. Click Export in the upper right corner of the window to export the queried
site comparison data to the local PC.
Step 8 Export the statistics of all sites.
● Click Export in the upper right corner of the main page to export the queried
site data to the local PC.
● Click Send Email in the upper right corner of the main page, set the email
sending rules, and the system sends the statistics at a scheduled time.
----End
6.2.2 Shutdown Site Analysis
Shutdown site analysis helps you learn about the region where shutdown sites are
located, the shutdown reason, and the shutdown duration, improving O&M
efficiency.
Prerequisites
You are authorized to perform Shutdown Site Analysis.
Context
If any load of a site is powered off, the site is considered a shutdown site. The
Shutdown Site Analysis page dynamically updates the real-time information
about site devices and alarms. The following alarms result in site shutdown: LLVD,
BLVD, or DC load unavailable.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Intelligent Maintenance > Shutdown Site
Analysis.
Step 2 Perform the following operations as required.

6.2.3 Risky Site Analysis
This section describes how to view the details about the sites at risk.
Prerequisites

A risky site is a site with shutdown risks. The Risky Site Analysis page dynamically
updates the real-time information about site devices and alarms. Battery and
diesel generator alarms are generated for risky sites.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance Management > Intelligent Maintenance > Risky Site
Analysis.
Step 2 Click to view the details about the site.
Step 3 Optional: Click Export to export the details to a CSV or an Excel file.
Step 4 Optional: Click History Details to view the details about the sites at risk on the
current day.
Specify the query period and click Query to view the details about the sites at risk
in the query period.
----End
6.2.4 Site Expansion Analysis
The Site Expansion Analysis page collects statistics on the mains, batteries, and
loads of sites, and displays the recommended battery and power capacity for the
sites in charts and tables.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Site Expansion Analysis.
Context
When there is load increases, you need to check whether the battery backup
power and power rectification capability meet the requirements after the load
addition. Site Expansion Analysis provides suggestions on the battery and power
capacity of a site to be added after load power increase in a chart and a table.
The following table describes some parameters on the Site Expansion Analysis
page.
Table 6-19 Parameters related to site configuration analysis
Parameter Description
Average load current (A) Average load current in the past 30
days
Battery backup time (h) Battery backup time (h) = Existing
battery capacity (Ah)/Average load

Parameter Description
Insufficient Rectifier Power (kW) Power capacity to be added (kW) =
Current maximum load power (kW) +
Maximum power of the loads to be
added (kW) + Maximum battery
charge power after load addition (kW)
– Rated output power of the rectifier
group without considering backup
(kW)
Maximum battery charge power after Maximum battery charge power after
load addition (kW) load addition (kW) = Equalized
charging voltage (56.4 V) x Charge
current limit coefficient (C10) x Battery
capacity after load addition (Ah)/1000
Rated power of the rectifier group ● Rated output power of the rectifier
(kW) group (kW) = Rated power of the
rectifier group (kW)/Number of
rectifiers x (Number of rectifiers –
1)
● Rated output power of the rectifier
group (kW) = Rated power of a
single rectifier (kW) x (Number of
rectifiers – 1)
● Rated output power of the rectifier
group (kW) = Rated current of a
single rectifier (A) x 58 V x
(Number of rectifiers – 1)
Insufficient Battery Capacity (Ah) Lithium battery under-configuration
capacity (Ah) = [Currently required
battery capacity (Ah) + Battery
capacity required for the loads to be
added (Ah)] – Existing battery capacity
(Ah)
Lead-acid battery under-configuration
capacity (Ah) = Currently required
battery capacity (Ah) + Battery
capacity required for the loads to be
added (Ah)
Currently required battery capacity Currently required battery capacity
(Ah) (Ah) = Maximum energy consumption
of loads within the designed backup
time in the past three months (kWh) x
1000/48 V

Parameter Description
Battery capacity required for the loads ● For lead-acid batteries: Battery
to be added capacity required for the loads to
be added = (W_avg x t x 1.25 x
1.04)/(V x η)
NOTE
W_avg: average power of the loads to
be added; t: designed backup time;
1.25: aging coefficient; 1.04: voltage
coefficient; V: 48 V; η: capacity
coefficient, see Table 6-20.
● For lithium batteries: Battery
capacity required for the loads to
be added = (W_avg x t)/(V x K x
DOD)
NOTE
W_avg: average power of the loads to
be added; t: designed backup time; V:
48 V; K: aging coefficient 0.8; DOD:
depth of discharge, which is 0.85
Existing battery capacity (Ah) Existing battery capacity (Ah) = [Rated
capacity of battery string 1 (Ah) x SOH
of battery string 1 (%)] + [Rated
capacity of battery string 2 (Ah) x SOH
of battery string 2 (%)] + ... + [Rated
capacity of battery string N (Ah) x
SOH of battery string N (%)]
Table 6-20 Battery capacity coefficient
Designed Backup Time(h) η (Capacity Coefficient)
0.5 0.4

7.1 Energy Aggregation
7.1.1 Energy Dashboard
When NetEco receives an invitation from a power grid company or a first-level
aggregation platform, it queries the dispatched data and the current dispatchable
capacity through the energy dashboard.
Prerequisites
● You have the Energy Dashboard permissions.
● The site supports and has activated the Peak regulation or Frequency
modulation. For details, see License Allocate.
Procedure
Step 1 Perform the following operations as required:

7.1.2 Intelligent Peak Shaving
Plan scheduling: The power grid company or level-1 aggregation platform invites
the scheduling plan of the next day on the same day. Allows the level-2
aggregation platform to reserve adjustment space in advance for the plan
scheduling of the next day.
Prerequisites
● You have the Intelligent Peak Regulation permissions.
● The site supports and has activated the Peak regulation. For details, see
License Allocate.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Energy Management > Energy Aggregation > Intelligent Peak Shaving
from the main menu.
Step 2 In the Intelligent Peak Shaving window, you can view the scheduling resources of
devices on the entire network in a visualized manner.

The task Description
View the peak adjustment dashboard ● Number of invitations: number of
peak regulation times received from
the power grid company or the
level-1 aggregation platform.
● Response Times: indicates the
number of planned peak adjustment
times (excluding the number of
times that the analysis is in progress
and that the timeout period is
exceeded).
● Number of bid winning times: the
number of received planned and
urgent scheduling times.
● Invited capacity: capacity for
delivering scheduling.
● Response Capacity: indicates the
total scheduling capacity, that is, the
sum of the bid-winning capacity in
each plan.
● Bid-winning capacity: capacity
scheduled by the receiving plan.
– If the bid value is positive, it
indicates the discharge capacity
of the station.
– If the winning bid is negative, it
refers to the charging capacity of
the station.
● Adjustment precision: indicates the
accumulated average precision of all
sites that have participated in
adjustment on the entire network.
View the distribution of site ● Adjustable site
scheduling capabilities.
● Uncontrolled Site: Click to view the
site name, ID, and reason for
uncontrolled site.
Viewing the Task Dashboard View the total scheduling tasks and
their distribution.
Viewing Report Data View the report data in the lower area.
To view the site scheduling details, click
View in the Operation column.
----End

Plan scheduling: The power grid company or level-1 aggregation platform invites
the scheduling plan of the next day on the same day. Allows the level-2
aggregation platform to reserve adjustment space in advance for the next-day
plan scheduling.
Prerequisites
● You have the Intelligent Frequency Regulation permissions.
● The site supports and has activated the Frequency modulation. For details, see
License Allocate.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Energy Management > Energy Aggregation > Intelligent Frequency
Regulation from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Intelligent Frequency Regulation page, you can view the scheduling
resources of devices on the entire network in a visualized manner.

8
System
This chapter describes the operations related to the system.
8.1 System Setting
This chapter describes how to set the system.
8.1.1 System Configuration
You can refresh the system time, display the view, configure the protocol switch,
and change the FTP password by system configuration.
8.1.1.1 Refreshing System Time
The system supports refreshing the NetEco server time and the client time
simultaneously.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > System Configuration.
Step 2 In the navigation pane on the left, choose System Configuration > System Time.
Step 3 On the System Time page, click Refresh to refresh the NetEcoserver time and the
client time.
NO TICE
The NetEco server time and the client time are automatically refreshed once every
1 second. The refresh stops when you close the page.

This section describes how to modify the Business Settings.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Site Business Settings.
Context
1 us gal = 3.785 L
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > System Configuration.
Step 2 In the navigation tree on the left, choose Site Business Settings.
Step 3 Choose the service to be set related parameters, and click Apply.
----End
8.1.1.3 Risk Analysis Benchmark Settings
You can customize benchmarks for different sites to improve the accuracy of risk
analysis.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Risk Analysis Benchmark Settings.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > System Configuration.
Step 2 In the navigation pane on the left, click Risk Analysis Benchmark Settings.
If You Need To... Then...
Query the risk benchmark 1. Select Battery or Mains for Device Type.
information of a device
2. Enter a risk name.
type

If You Need To... Then...
Set risk benchmarks
1. Click in the Operation column to open the
Benchmark settings dialog box.
2. Click Batch Set. In the displayed Batch Set
dialog box.
3. Select monitored batteries in the right pane,
enter the risk benchmarks in the lower part,
and click OK.
4. Click in the Operation column to edit the
risk benchmarks
● NOTE
After setting the risk benchmarks, you can view
details about risky devices on the Risk Analysis
Details page.
Enable the risk ● Batch operation: Select the records for which
benchmarks you want to enable the risk benchmarks, and
click Enable in the upper right of the page.
● Single operation: Click in the Status
column.
NOTE
If Status is set to ON, batteries and mains are monitored
in real time.
Disable risk benchmarks ● Batch operation: Select the records for which
you want to disable the risk benchmarks, and
click Disable in the upper right of the page.
● Single operation: Click in the Status
column.
NOTE
If Status is set to OFF, batteries and mains are not
monitored.
----End
8.1.1.4 Modifying the FTP Password
This section describes how to modify the FTP password. To improve user security,
users are advised to change their passwords periodically.
Context
The following figure shows the default information about the ftpuser and nftpuser
users, as shown in Table 8-1.

User Initial Password Description
ftpuser Changeme_123 ● Common user.
● The ftpuser user is an
FTP service operator
responsible for file
transfer between the
NetEco server and
clients or NEs.
nftpuser Changeme_123 ● Common user.
● User nftpuser is used
for uploading and
downloading files for
the NMS to access the
NetEco through the
LAN.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > System Configuration.
Step 2 In the left navigation pane of the System Configuration page, click FTP
Password Setting.
Step 3 Set the user name and password, and click Confirm.
----End
8.1.1.5 Configuring the Protocol Switch
By default, the NetEco communicates with devices only through security protocols
and arithmetic. If the NetEco wants to communicate with devices through non-
secure protocols or arithmetic, perform operations described in this section. This
operation will undermine the security of the transmission between the NetEco and
NEs. Therefore, exercise caution when deciding to perform this operation.
Prerequisites
● You have the operation rights for Protocols Configuration.
● The mediation software has been installed.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > System Configuration.
Step 2 Choose Protocols Configuration in the navigation pane.
Step 3 Select parameter to ON or OFF as required, and click Confirm.

netecopvmsconsrv-0-0 processes, and restart them for the settings to take effect.
For details, see Stopping Product Processes and Starting Product Processes in
the Administrator Guide.
----End
8.1.1.6 NAT Configuration
If NAT is performed on the network between device and the NetEco server, you
need to configure NAT on the NetEco to ensure normal communication between
southbound device and the NetEco.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > System Configuration.
Step 2 Choose NAT Configuration in the navigation pane.
Step 3 On the NAT Configuration area, configure the NAT IP address.
Step 4 In the displayed dialog box, click Confirm.
Step 5 Restart all NetEco service to bring the configurations into effect. For details, see
Stopping Product Services and Starting Product Services in the Administrator
Guide.
----End
8.1.1.7 Configuring the Historical Data
You can set parameters such as the threshold for clearing historical data and the
storage duration.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Historical Data Storage Policy.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > System Configuration.
Step 2 In the navigation tree on the left, choose Historical Data Storage Policy.
Step 3 On the Historical Data Storage Policy page, set related parameters and click
Apply.
----End
8.1.2 Broadcast Messages
This section describes how to send broadcast messages.

When a user wants to notify all online users of a message, the user can send a
broadcast message so that the users of other clients can view the messages sent
by the user in time.
Prerequisites
● You have been attached to the Administrators role or have permission to
send messages.
● You have logged in to the system, and can receive and view broadcast
messages.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > Broadcast Messages.
Step 2 On the Broadcast Messages page, enter the message content in the Content text
box.
Step 3 Click Send.
NO TE
● The client can receive its own broadcast messages and displays messages in the
information output window at the bottom.
● A user can use the same IP address to send broadcast messages for a maximum number
of 60 times per minute. If the limit is exceeded, the broadcast message function will be
locked for 10 minutes.
----End
Related Tasks
Go to the Broadcast Messages page to view historical broadcast messages.
NO TE
A maximum of 1000 broadcast messages can be displayed. If the number of broadcast
messages exceeds 1000, the earliest broadcast messages are automatically deleted.
8.1.3 Notifications
The notifications feature allows the system to send messages remotely. Users can
set parameters related to notifications to enable the system to communicate with
the short message service gateway (SMSGW), short message service (SMS)
modem, or mail server. In this way, users can obtain important function
notifications in a timely manner, such as alarms and events, in the form of SMS
messages or emails.
8.1.3.1 Notification Process
Before using the notifications feature, O&M personnel need to commission the
communication function of the notifications feature and set notification
parameters so that the notifications can be manually or automatically sent to

Figure 8-1 Configuration process of the notifications feature
1. Commission the notifications feature.
Before using the notifications feature, O&M personnel need to set parameters
for communication between the system and the SMSGW, SMS modem, or
mail server so that the system supports the notifications feature.
– Notifications are sent in the form of SMS messages, emails. Table 8-2
describes the remote notification sending modes.

Form Sending Description
Mode
SMS SMS Third-party SMS server. The notifications feature
messa server provides default SMS server settings and allows
ge you to reconfigure SMS server parameters to
ensure successful interconnection between the
system and SMS server.
SMS SMSGW settings will not take effect after the
modem SMS modem is set. Sending SMS messages by
the SMS modem is slower than by the SMS
server. Therefore, SMS server is recommended.
Email Mail Third-party email server. The notifications feature
server allows O&M personnel to set parameters for
email servers to establish communication
between email servers and the system.
– Table 8-3 lists the indicators and specifications of notifications.
Table 8-3 Notification sending specifications
Sendin Sending Specifications
g Rate
Mode
SMS 30 1. The network delay between the node where
server notificatio the campmessagingservice process resides and
ns per the SMS server does not exceed 1 ms.
second
2. The response duration of the SMS server does
not exceed 20 ms.
SMS 10 CloudSOP services must be deployed on physical
mode notificatio machines.
m ns per
minute
Mail 10 1. The network delay between the node where
server notificatio the campmessagingservice process resides and
ns per the email server does not exceed 1 ms.
second
2. The response duration of the email server
does not exceed 90 ms.
2. Set notification parameters.
Before notifications are sent, O&M personnel need to set the following items:
– Recipient: indicates a user or user group that receives notifications.
– Traffic control: sets the time range and capacity limit for sending emails

notification contents as a notification template. In this way, they do not
need to manually edit notification contents each time they want to send
notifications. This method improves O&M efficiency.
– Notification rule: indicates the information to be sent in notifications, for
example, all alarms of a certain alarm source, certain specific alarms, all
alarms of a certain alarm severity, or information about an event.
3. Send notifications.
– Automatically sending notifications
The system automatically sends information that meets notification rules
to recipients based on the configured notification rules.
– Manually sending notifications
When O&M personnel need to send certain notifications to relevant
personnel, the O&M personnel can manually edit the contents to be sent
on the GUI and send notifications to relevant personnel in the form of
SMS messages or emails so that they can learn the notification contents
in a timely manner.
8.1.3.2 Commissioning Notifications
After the software and hardware of the system are installed, initialize the system.
That is, set the parameters for the interconnection between the system and the
SMS, SMS modem, and email server during the commissioning to ensure that SMS
messages and emails can be sent properly.
8.1.3.2.1 Commissioning SMS Server Notifications
When you need to send notifications by SMS message, you need to set the SMS
server parameters and verify the settings to ensure that SMS messages can be
sent properly.
Prerequisites
● You have the SMS Settings permission.
● You have obtained the following information from the SMS server provider:
– Type of the communication protocol used between the notifications
service and SMS server and related protocol parameters.
– Domain name/IP address, code protocol, port number, username, and
password of the SMS server.
– Sender mobile number.
● The node where the campmessagingservice process resides properly
communicates with the SMS server. For details, see "Checking the
Connectivity of the SMS Server > Installation and Commissioning" in the
Product Documentation.
Context
● If both an SMS server and SMS modem are configured, the SMS modem is

slower than using an SMS server. You are advised to use the SMS server.
● To send notifications to relevant personnel, you need to enter their personal
data, such as mobile numbers and email addresses. You are obligated to take
considerable measures, in compliance with the laws of the countries
concerned and the user privacy policies of your company, to ensure that users'
personal data is fully protected.
● For security purposes, personal data such as mobile numbers and email
addresses are anonymized on the GUI and encrypted during transmission.
● If the SMS server requires secure connections and provides the corresponding
certificate, you need to configure the SMS server certificate and CRL in
notifications. After the certificate is configured, periodically check whether
there are notifications service certificate alarms related to the SMS server. If
there is an alarm indicating that the SMS server certificate is about to expire,
update the certificate in time. If there is an alarm indicating that the SMS
server certificate has expired, update the certificate immediately. If the trusted
CA has updated the CRL, update the SMS server certificate and CRL in a
timely manner.
For data security purposes, the notifications feature supports certificates
signed using the SHA256withRSA, SHA384withRSA, or SHA512withRSA
algorithm.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > Notifications..
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose SMS Settings > Protocol. Check whether the
parameter settings are consistent with those used by the SMS server.
● If yes, go to Step 3.
● If no, set the parameters to make them consistent with those used by the
SMS server, and click Apply.
Step 3 Perform the following operations to set SMS server parameters:
1. In the navigation pane, choose SMS Settings > SMS Server.
2. Set the domain name and port number of the SMS server, and the sender
mobile number.
Table 8-4 Parameters on the SMS server settings page
Parameter Description
Server domain SMS server domain name or IP address obtained
name/IP address from the SMS server provider.
SMS protocol Encoding protocol used by the SMS server to
interconnect with the SMS gateway. SMPP 3.3, SMP
3.4, CMPP 2.x, CMPP 3.x, SGIP, SMGP, and CNGP are
supported.

Parameter Description
SMS server port Port number for interconnection with the SMS
server. For details, see NetEco Communication
Matrix.
SMS server Username of the user registered on the SMS server.
username
NOTE
The user must be registered on the interconnected SMS
server and the username must be complete.
SMS server password Password for the SMS server user.
Sender mobile Number allocated by the SMS server.
number
NOTE
Recommended SMS message format:
– [SMS prefix] + SMS content
– SMS content + [SMS suffix]
SMS message prefix The beginning of an SMS or message. It may
contain the sender ID, timestamp, and message
subject, which are used to identify the message
source and provide basic message details. Use
square brackets ([]) to separate the prefix from the
message body, for example, [123].
SMS message suffix The end of an SMS or message. It may contain the
sender ID, timestamp, and message subject, which
are used to identify the message source and provide
basic message details. Use square brackets ([]) to
separate the suffix from the message body, for
example, [123].
Recipient mobile Mobile phone number used to check whether SMS
number messages can be received during a test.
Failed retry times Number of times for resending after a message fails
to be sent.
Configure certificate Toggle this parameter on if the SMS server requires
and CRL secure connection and this parameter is available on
the page. For details, see "Updating the Email
Server Certificate for Notifications" in
Administrator Guide
Security protocol Ensure that the SMS server certificate has been
configured. By default, TLSv1.3 is selected.

Parameter Description
Settings take effect Test the connectivity of the SMS gateway and then
determine whether to enable it. For details, see
Checking the Connectivity of the SMS Server in
the product documentation.
– If this parameter is enabled, an SMS notification
will be sent after the test is successful.
– If this parameter is disabled, the configuration is
saved as a draft and no SMS notification will be
sent.
3. If long SMS messages and status reply are required, click Advanced. The
supported short message length varies according to protocols. For details
about the SMS message length supported by each protocol and the status
reply setting, see Table 8-5.
Table 8-5 Advanced parameters
Parameter Description
Allow long SMS – Long SMS messages are supported.
messages Each SMS message contains a maximum of 134
characters by default. Messages that exceed the
maximum length are segmented into multiple
messages before sending to the SMS server. Once
received, the segments are combined by header
and sent to the receiver as one complete SMS
message.
– Long SMS messages are not supported.
Each SMS message contains a maximum of 140
characters by default. Messages that exceed the
maximum length are segmented into multiple
messages before sending to the SMS server and
sent to the receiver as multiple messages.
NOTE
– To send a long message, set this parameter to Yes.
– You can customize the maximum length of an SMS
message as required. For the configuration method, see
"Changing the Maximum Length of Each SMS
Message" in Administrator Guide.

Parameter Description
Enable status replies Whether to send a reply with the sending status.
– If this parameter is set to Yes, the SMS server
automatically returns the message sending status
to the notifications service after the message is
sent. When you use the notifications service to
send messages to multiple mobile numbers, a
message sending status is returned each time a
message is sent.
– If this parameter is set to No, no message
sending status is returned. The sender does not
know whether the message is successfully sent.
4. Click Test to check whether the system is properly connected to the SMS
server.
– If "Test succeeded" is displayed, they are properly connected. Click Apply.
– If the test fails, rectify the fault based on the error information or contact
technical support.
----End
8.1.3.2.2 Commissioning Email Server Notifications
When you need to send notifications by email, you need to set the email
notification parameters and verify the settings to ensure that emails can be sent
properly.
Prerequisites
● You have the Email Server Settings permission.
● The interconnected email server supports Simple Mail Transfer Protocol
(SMTP) and the SMTP port is available.
– In common connection mode, the port 25 is recommended.
– In STARTTLS connection mode, the port 587 is recommended.
– In SSL or TLS connection mode, the port 465 is recommended.
● You have obtained the server information from the SMTP server administrator,
such as the domain name or IP address, port number, whether identity
authentication is required, username, and user password.
● A valid email address has been registered.
● The node where the campmessagingservice process resides properly
communicates with the email server. For details, see "Checking the
Connectivity of the Email Server > Installation and Commissioning" in the
Product Documentation.
Context
● For security purpose, you are advised to use the default STARTTLS version

data, such as mobile numbers and email addresses. You are obligated to take
considerable measures, in compliance with the laws of the countries
concerned and the user privacy policies of your company, to ensure that users'
personal data is fully protected.
● For security purposes, personal data such as mobile numbers and email
addresses are anonymized on the GUI and encrypted during transmission.
● If the SMTP server requires secure connections and provides the
corresponding certificate, you need to configure the email server certificate
and CRL in notifications. After the certificate is configured, periodically check
whether there are notifications service certificate alarms related to the email
server. If there is an alarm indicating that the email server certificate is about
to expire, update the certificate in time. If there is an alarm indicating that
the email server certificate has expired, update the certificate immediately. If
the trusted CA has updated the CRL, update the email server certificate and
CRL in a timely manner.
For data security purposes, the notifications feature supports certificates
signed using the SHA256withRSA, SHA384withRSA, or SHA512withRSA
algorithm.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > Notifications.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Email Server Settings. Set the domain name or IP
address of the SMTP server, sender email address, and server port.

Table 8-6 Parameters on the Email Server Settings page
Parameter Description
SMTP server Domain name or IP address obtained from the email server
domain name/IP provider.
address

Parameter Description
Sender email Email address allocated by the email server.
address
NOTE
The email address must be registered on the peer SMTP server and
must be complete. You are advised not to use a personal email
address.
Charset Charset of the SMTP server.
Security protocol If SMTP server certificate authentication (Applies when
an email server certificate for SMTP server is already
installed. TLS is recommended.) is selected, select a secure
connection protocol and configure the certificate and CRL.
For details, see "Updating the Email Server Certificate for
Notifications" in Administrator Guide.
To ensure data security, it is advisable to use the STARTTLS
protocol.
Server port Port number of the SMTP server. For details, see NetEco
Communication Matrix.
Require identity If the SMTP email server does not require identity
authentication authentication, do not select this option. Otherwise, emails
for the SMTP will fail to be sent.
server
NOTE
If the SMTP server requires user identity verification, obtain the
username and user password from the SMTP server administrator.
● The username must be the same as that contained in the value
of Sender email address. You are advised not to use a personal
username.
● If no authorization code is available for logging in to the SMTP
email server, set the password to the password of Sender email
address. Otherwise, set the password to the authorization code
for logging in to the SMTP email server.
Efficient sending ● Set it to Yes for the email server that can send multiple
emails through a single connection to improve the email
sending efficiency.
● Set it to No for the email server that can send only one
email through a single connection. Otherwise, some
emails may fail to be sent.
Settings take Test the connectivity of the email server and then determine
effect whether to enable it. For details, see Checking the
Connectivity of the Email Server in the product
documentation.
● If this parameter is enabled, an email notification will be
sent after the test is successful.
● If this parameter is disabled, the configuration is saved as
a draft and no email notification will be sent.

server.
● If the button is dimmed, check whether all mandatory items are set or

● If "Test succeeded" is displayed, they are properly connected. Click Apply.
● If the test fails, rectify the fault based on the error information or contact
technical support.

8.1.3.2.3 Commissioning the SMS Modem Using Notifications
The notifications service provides the function of setting the SMS modem. When
O&M personnel need to send an SMS message, they need to connect to the SMS
modem, set the SMS modem parameters, and verify the settings to ensure that
the SMS message can be sent properly.
Prerequisites
● The SMS modem has been connected to a serial port of the physical machine
and has been commissioned.
● Set this parameter to the network system of the SIM card in the SMS modem
obtained from the carrier.
– 2G: GSM
– 3G: CDMA
– 4G: LTE or FDD LTE, which needs to be confirmed with the carrier.
● You have obtained the baud rate of the SMS modem from the instructions
delivered with the SMS modem.
– The baud rate of the SMS modem of the E-TEK GSM modem TD-8412 or
E-TEK GSM modem TD-8422 model is 115200.
– The baud rate of the SMS modem that supports the outdoor IoT 4G
module is 9600.
● You have obtained the serial port of the physical machine connected to the
SMS modem.
– For the SMS modem that supports the outdoor IoT 4G module, the serial
port of the physical machine is ttyUSB_4G.
– For the SMS modem of the E-TEK GSM modem TD-8412 and E-TEK GSM
modem TD-8422 models, the serial port of the physical machine is COM1
or COM2.
Context
● After the SMS modem is set, if the SMS modem fails to send SMS messages,
the system attempts to use the SMS server to send SMS messages. Sending
SMS messages by the SMS modem is slower than by the SMS server.
Therefore, SMS server is recommended.

NO TE
● The SMS modem is not supported in virtualization scenarios.
● The SMS modem cannot send SMS messages concurrently due to hardware
restrictions. According to the evaluation of software and hardware capabilities, the
SMS modem can send 50 SMS messages within 5 minutes.
● To send notifications to relevant personnel, you need to enter their personal
data, such as mobile numbers and email addresses. You are obligated to take
considerable measures, in compliance with the laws of the countries
concerned and the user privacy policies of your company, to ensure that users'
personal data is fully protected.
● For security purposes, personal data such as mobile numbers and email
addresses are anonymized on the GUI and encrypted during transmission.
● The serial port communicates with the SMS modem using the local hardware,
which is secure.
Procedure
● Set parameters for interconnecting with the SMS modem.
a. Choose System > System Settings > Notifications.
b. In the navigation pane, choose SMS Modem Settings.
c. On the SMS Modem Settings page, set Settings take effect to Yes.
d. Set the node where the SMS modem is deployed, network system, serial
port used by the SMS modem to connect to the physical machine, and
baud rate of the SMS modem.
Table 8-7 Parameters on the SMS Modem Settings page

randomly allocates an SMS modem node.
● If the campmessagingservice process is deployed on
only one node, you can select default or the

which the SMS modem connects. In a single-node

8.1.3.3 Sending Notifications
Notifications can be sent automatically or manually, which requires recipients or
recipient groups be configured in advance. If notifications need to be sent to
specified personnel based on fixed rules, you can use the automatic notification
sending mode. In this mode, you need to set notification rules. If notifications
need to be sent based on the service triggered by events, you can use the manual
notification sending mode. In this mode, you need to configure notification
templates.
8.1.3.3.1 Creating a Recipient
O&M personnel need to specify recipients to meet message sending requirements.
Prerequisites
You have the Notified User Management permission.
Context
● To send notifications to relevant personnel, you need to enter their personal
data, such as mobile numbers and email addresses. You are obligated to take
considerable measures, in compliance with the laws of the countries or
regions concerned and the user privacy policies of your company, to ensure
that users' personal data is fully protected.
● For security purposes, personal data such as mobile numbers and email
addresses are anonymized on the GUI and encrypted during transmission.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > Notifications from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Notified User Management > Users.
Step 3 On the Users page, click Create, as shown in Figure 8-3.
Figure 8-3 Users page
Step 4 Set the user information. Either the mobile number or email address must be set,

NO TE
● The username is case-sensitive.
● The mobile number can be in the following format:
Mobile number with country/region code (for example, 0040+mobile number and
+40+mobile number)
Step 5 Click OK.
----End
Related Tasks
● Modifying recipient information
If you need to modify information about a recipient, click in the
Operation column of the row that contains the user in the list.
● Querying a recipient
If you need to query information about one or more recipients, select User
Name, Mobile Number, or Email from the drop-down list in the upper right
corner of the Users page, and enter a mobile number, email address, or
keyword of a username in the search box. Then, click . The users meeting
the search criterion are displayed in the list.
● Deleting a recipient
If you need to delete a recipient that is no longer used, click in the
Operation column of the row that contains the user in the list.
● Importing recipients and user groups
To import the information about multiple users and user groups, click Import
on the Users page. In the Import Users dialog box, upload the user
information file to import multiple recipients and user groups at a time.
● Exporting recipient information
If you need to export information about recipients for query and
management, click Export All on the Users page. In the dialog box that is

opening the recipient information file on your local PC, you need to enter the
password set in the Advanced dialog box for authentication.
NO TICE
If you click Cancel in the dialog box that is displayed, the personal data such
as mobile numbers and email addresses in the recipient information file is not
anonymized. The operation must be authorized by the customer. Operations
beyond the customer's approval are strictly forbidden. Upon completion of the
service, the personal data involved must be securely deleted under the
supervision of the customer. In sensitive markets, it is prohibited to transfer
the personal data out of the customer network without the customer's
authorization.
8.1.3.3.2 Creating a Notified User Group
The notifications feature provides the function of creating a notified user group.
When a notification needs to be sent to users, O&M personnel create a user group
to send notifications by group.
Prerequisites
You have the Notified User Management permission.
Context
● To send notifications to relevant personnel, you need to enter their personal
data, such as mobile numbers and email addresses. You are obligated to take
considerable measures, in compliance with the laws of the countries
concerned and the user privacy policies of your company, to ensure that users'
personal data is fully protected.
● For security purposes, personal data such as mobile numbers and email
addresses are anonymized on the GUI and encrypted during transmission.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > Notifications from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Notified User Management > User Groups.
Step 3 On the User Groups page, click Create.
Step 4 Enter user group information and add users to the user group.

NO TE
● On the Users tab page, you can click Create to add notified users. Newly created users
are added to this user group by default.
● You can add a maximum of 200 users to each user group.
● When adding users to a user group, you can search for users by User Name, Mobile
Number, or Email on the Notified User and O&M Users tab pages.
● Notified users refer to users who receive short messages or emails. O&M users are users
who log in to the NetEco, and they can be created through user management. The
menu entry is as follows: Choose System > System Management > User Management
from the main menu.
● If Mobile Number and Email are both left empty for a user, the user cannot be added
to the user group.
● The user group name is case-sensitive.
Step 5 Click OK.
----End
Related Tasks
● Modifying notified user group information
If you need to modify information about a notified user group, click in the
Operation column corresponding to the notified user group in the User
Groups list.
● Copying a notified user group
If you need to create a notified user group similar to an existing notified user
group, you can create it by copying the existing group and then editing the
copied group as needed. To copy an existing group, click in the Operation
column corresponding to the group to be copied in the User Groups list.
● Searching for a notified user group
If you need to query information about one or more notified user groups, set
a search criterion by entering a keyword in the search box in the upper right
corner of the User Groups page. Then, click . The group records meeting
the search criteria are displayed in the notified user group list.
● Deleting a notified user group
If you need to delete a notified user group that is no longer used, click in
the Operation column for the user group in the User Groups list.
8.1.3.3.3 Setting Traffic Control
To prevent network congestion and control SMS message fees, the system
provides the function of setting traffic control. You can set the traffic control for
sending emails and SMS messages based on the specified time range and capacity
limit. If the traffic for sending SMS messages or emails reaches the specified value
within a specified period, the emails or SMS messages that are generated in the
period and exceed the capacity limit will be discarded or sent with a delay.
Prerequisites

● To send notifications to relevant personnel, you need to enter their personal
data, such as mobile numbers and email addresses. You are obligated to take
considerable measures, in compliance with the laws of the countries or
regions concerned and the user privacy policies of your company, to ensure
that users' personal data is fully protected.
● For security purposes, personal data such as mobile numbers and email
addresses are anonymized on the GUI and encrypted during transmission.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > Notifications from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Traffic Control.
Step 3 On the Email Limit and SMS Limit tab pages, click to enable traffic control
and set Period, Max. messages, and Action respectively according to Table 8-9,
as shown in Figure 8-5.
Figure 8-5 Traffic control page
Table 8-9 Traffic control parameters
Setting Mode Parameter Description
Email Limit Period Set the period by hour or
minute. The default value is 5
minutes.

Setting Mode Parameter Description
Max. size Mail capacity limit. The value
range is from 1 to 1000 MB. The
default value is 5 MB.
Action Set the action to Discard or
Send with a delay. The default
value is Discard.
SMS Limit Period Set the period by hour or
minute. The default value is 5
minutes.
Max. size Maximum number of SMS
messages that can be sent. The
value range is from 1 to 1000.
The default value is 50
messages.
Action Set the action to Discard or
Send with a delay. The default
value is Discard.
NO TE
The email limit rule is similar to the SMS limit rule. The following uses the email limit rule
as an example:
● If Period is set to 5 minutes, Max. size is set to 50 MB, and Action is set to Discard, the
part of the content that exceeds 50 MB within 5 minutes will be discarded.
● If Period is set to 5 minutes, Max. size is set to 50 MB, and Action is set to Send with a
delay, the part of the content that exceeds 50 MB within 5 minutes will be sent with a
delay.
Step 4 Click Apply.
----End
8.1.3.3.4 Setting Rules for Automatically Sending Notifications
You cannot browse alarms or events on the alarm management page when you
are out of office, for example, during off-work hours or business trips. You can set
notification rules to receive notifications about concerned alarms or events by
SMS message, email, or webhook message. In this way, you can handle alarms or
events in a timely manner.
Prerequisites
● You have the Set Notification Rules permission.
● You have the Notified User Management permission.

● Notifications by email, webhook, and SMS message are sent through third-
party servers and devices. Therefore, the sending capabilities (such as the
delay in receiving notifications by email) vary with third-party server, device,
and network. For details about the notification sending capabilities, see
Notification Process.
● The delay time cannot be set on the page in an event notification rule.
● By default, rules are sorted by state (enabled or disabled) and enabled rules
are displayed preferentially. The rules in the same state are sorted by last
update time in descending order.
● A maximum of 1000 remote notification rules can be created.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Device Management > Alarm Management > Alarm Settings.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Notification Rules.
Step 3 On the Notification Rules page, click Create and choose Alarm Notification
Rules or Event Notification Rules.
Step 4 In the Basic Information area, set the rule name, description, and whether to
enable the rule.
Step 5 In the Conditions area, set the severities, alarm status, alarms or events, alarm or
event sources, and advanced conditions (which are used to filter alarms or events
based on the alarm or event parameters). The parameters of an event rule are
similar to those of an alarm rule. The following uses an alarm rule as an example.
For details about the alarm rule parameters, see Table 8-10.

Notification Method, Delay Settings, and Recipient Time Zone areas. When the
alarms meeting the rule are reported, the system sends notifications to recipients
of specified groups through email or SMS message, or to specified third-party app
groups through webhook URLs. For details about the alarm rule parameters, see
Table 8-10.
The following uses the parameters for creating an alarm rule as an example, and
they are similar to those for creating an event rule.
Table 8-10 Parameters for creating a notification rule
Area Parameter Description
Conditions Alarms ● If you select Designated alarms, the rule
takes effect only for the alarms you
added.
Alarm ID and Group Name: Unique
identifiers of an alarm.
● If you do not select Designated alarms,
the rule takes effect for all alarms by
default.
Alarm sources ● All alarm sources: The rule takes effect
for the alarms of all alarm sources.
● Custom alarm sources: The rule takes
effect only for the alarms of specified
alarm sources.
NOTE

available only for the users who can manage

Related Tasks
● Deleting a rule: You can select a redundant rule from the rule list and click

from the rule list and click Disable. To use a disabled rule, select the rule and
click Enable.
● Migrating rules: You can click Export on the rule page of the source system to
export rules. Then, click Import in the target system to import the rule file
exported from the source system to the target system.
● Backing up, collecting statistics on, and reviewing rules, and modifying rules
in batches: You can click Export on the rule page to export rules and back up,
review, and collect statistics on the rules. If you need to modify rules in
batches, you can modify the rules in batches in the exported rule file. Then,
click Import on the rule page to import the modified rule file.
NO TE

compliance with the laws of the countries or regions concerned and the user privacy
policies of your company, to ensure that users' personal data is fully protected.

8.1.3.4 Viewing and Exporting Notification Logs
Notification logs are recorded for SMS messages or emails sent using
Notifications. This service provides the functions of viewing and exporting
notification logs. If users want to obtain notification logs, they can export
notification logs to a local computer, to facilitate auditing the sending status.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for View Notification Logs.

Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > Notifications.
Step 2 Choose View Notification Logs from the navigation pane to view basic
information about the notification logs.
Step 3 (Optional) Set the time period during which notification logs to be viewed and
exported are generated and click Filter.
NO TE
● Users can view logs on multiple pages. A maximum of 100,000 logs can be displayed. If
the number of logs exceeds 100,000, the logs that are generated at the earlier time are
deleted automatically.
● By default, 10 logs are displayed on each page.
● If filter criteria are not set, the data of the previous day is displayed by default.
Step 4 Click Export All. In the dialog box that is displayed, click OK.
NO TE
● The logs generated during the specified time period are exported. A maximum of
100,000 logs can be exported.
● If filter criteria are not set, the data of the previous day is exported by default.
● The exported data is saved as a .zip package. The package contains .csv files. Every
10,000 lines of data are saved in one .csv file, and 100,000 lines of data are saved
respectively in 10 .csv files.
----End

8.1.4 BTS Synergy Configuration
You can configure the wireless MAE to be connected to obtain device information
from it.
Prerequisites
● Ensure that the NetEco communicates properly with MAE. If the NetEco uses
the NAT networking, ensure that the original IP address of the NetEco is on

● A third-party northbound user with MML command rights has been created
on the MAE.
Procedure
1. Choose System > System Settings > BTS Synergy Configuration.
2. On the BTS Synergy Configuration page, click MAE Interface Configuration
on the left.
3. Click Add. On the tab page in the lower pane, enter information about the
NMS to be interconnected, and click Apply.
NO TE
● Currently, only four MAE can be added on the NetEco.
● Set IP Address to the IP address of the MAE. A backup IP address is used
automatically if the IP address becomes invalid.
● Set User Name to the name of the northbound user. For details about how to
create a northbound user, see "Assigning MML Command Rights to MAE
Northbound Users" in Software Installation and Commissioning Guide.
4. On the BTS Synergy Configuration page, click NE Device Bind on the left.
5. In the Relation List area, click .
6. In the Add Site dialog box, select the wireless devices and target sites to be
synchronized.
7. Click Apply.
8.1.5 AI Model Management
8.1.5.1 AI Asset Service
This section describes AI model operations.
8.1.5.1.1 Deploying AI Services
NO TE
The operations described in this section do not apply to the model fine-tuning service.
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > AI Model Management from the main
menu
Step 2 Click the AI Asset Service tab.
Step 3 Click Add AI Asset Service.
Step 4 Select the name of the asset to be deployed and click Deploy in the Operation
column.
Step 5 In the displayed Deployment Configuration dialog box, configure related
information.

Parameter Description
Service name The value is a string of 1 to 32 characters and can
include only uppercase letters, lowercase letters,
digits, underscores (_), and hyphens (-). It must
start with a letter.
Deployed Resource The value is a string of 1 to 64 characters and can
include only uppercase letters, lowercase letters,
digits, and underscores (_).
NOTE
– This parameter is displayed only when Resource type
is set to Yarn.
– YARN queues are used to deploy Spark models.
Inference – Always online: After a model is deployed,
deployment type inference is always performed.
– On-demand startup: A model exits an inference
process automatically after it is complete. To
perform inference again, the process needs to be
restarted in the backend.
Resource type – VM
– Yarn
Inference node label Select one label from the drop-down list based on
the site requirements.
NOTE
Spark and YARN models do not support this parameter.
Visualization node Select one label from the drop-down list based on
label the site requirements.
NOTE
Only a non-Spark or non-YARN model of the web_only
type supports this parameter.
Node allocation – Automatic allocation
policy
– Full node deployment
– Number of custom instances
– Maximum allocation based on resource
requirements
This parameter is supported when Deployment
type is set to Always online or the model package
type is Spark.

Parameter Description
Number of Set the number of instances based on service
deployment requirements and system resources.
instances
Value range: an integer ranging from 1 to 1000. The
total number of instances on all nodes cannot
exceed 1000.
You are advised to set the number to less than 10.
More instances occupy more system resources.
For distributed deployment, the value ranges from 2
to 50.
2. Configure resources.
Set the resource limit of the current Spark model or YARN resources.
3. Configure model parameters.
NO TE
Passwords must meet the following requirements:
– The value contains 8 to 1024 characters.
– The value contains at least two types of the following characters:
1. Lowercase letters
2. Uppercase letters
3. Digits
4. Special characters: `~!@#$%^&*()_-=+\|[{}];:'",<.>/? and space
4. (Optional) Configure data sources.
NO TICE
In the active/standby scenario, the data source name of the active server must
be the same as that of the standby server.
a. Add a data source.
▪
Data Source: The value is globally unique. The value can include a
maximum of 64 characters, including letters, digits, and underscores
(_).
▪
Interface Type: Select a value from the drop-down list. The value
can be Zenith, Redis, SFTP, or FTPS.
▪
Host IP: Host IP address.
▪
Host Port: Host port number. The value ranges from 0 to 65535.
▪
User Name: User for data source management. The value can
include a maximum of 64 characters.
▪
Password: Password of the user who manages the data source. The

NO TE
Passwords must meet the following complexity requirements:
○ The value contains 8 to 64 characters.
○ The value contains at least two types of the following characters:
1. Lowercase letters
2. Uppercase letters
3. Digits
4. Special characters: `~!@#$%^&*()_-=+\|[{}];:'",<.>/? and space
○ The value is not the same as the user name.
b. Select data sources as required.
5. Click Deploy.
----End
8.1.5.1.2 Starting or Stopping an AI Service
This section describes how to start or stop a deployed AI model.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > AI Model Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 Click the AI Asset Service tab.
Step 3 Start or stop a service.
● Stop an AI service.
a. Select the AI service to be stopped.
b. Click Stop in the Operation column.
c. In the confirmation dialog box, click OK.
● Start an AI service.
a. Select the AI service to be started.
b. Click Start in the Operation column.
----End
8.1.5.1.3 Switching the AI Service Version
This section describes how to switch the AI service and precautions.
NO TE
● The version of a running, faulty, or stopped AI service can be switched.
● The versions of AI services with the same name can be changed only through version
switch. The original versions cannot be used through model reinstallation after
uninstallation.
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > AI Model Management from the main

Step 3 Select the AI service whose version needs to be switched and click Switch Version.
Step 4 In the displayed Switch Version dialog box, select the AI service version to be
switched to.
Step 5 Click Switch Version.
----End
8.1.5.1.4 Uninstalling an AI Service
NO TE
After an AI service is uninstalled, its AI model package can still be found in AI Asset
Packages. You can redeploy the AI service by referring to 8.1.5.1.1 Deploying AI Services.
The versions of AI services with the same name can be changed only through version
switch. The original versions cannot be used through model reinstallation after
uninstallation.
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > AI Model Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 Click the AI Asset Service tab.
Step 3 Select the AI service to be uninstalled and choose More > Uninstall in the
Operation column.
The Confirm dialog box is displayed.
Step 4 Click OK.
----End
8.1.5.1.5 Setting the Fields to Be Displayed on the AI Asset Service Page
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > AI Model Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 Click the AI Asset Service tab.
Step 3 Click in the upper right corner.
The Field Displayed in the List window is displayed.
Step 4 Select the fields to be displayed based on the site requirements.
Step 5 Click OK.
----End
8.1.5.1.6 Configuring a Model
Configuring Deployment Information
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > AI Model Management from the main

Step 3 Click Configurations in the Operation column corresponding to the service name.
Step 4 Click the Deployment Configuration tab.
Step 5 Modify or view Parameter Configuration.
NO TE
If there are unconfigured parameters during the configuration, the default values in the
model package are used when you click Deploy.
● Service name can be modified only when multiple service models are to be
deployed.
● Basic configurations of a deployed model cannot be modified.
● Configure data sources: Select data sources as required.
Stop the AI model before modifying data sources. After the modification, click
Save Data Source Configurations on the right of the data source setting
page.
Step 6 In the Model instance information area, view information about model instances
and change the number of model instances.
1. Select the AI model for which the instance quantity needs to be modified or
instances need to be deleted, and click . View information about the
deployed instances of the model.
2. Modify the number of instances.
NO TE
Instances can be added or reduced for Spark and YARN models. For other models,
instances can only be added.
a. Click Instance Expansion or Instance Scaling on the right of Model
Instance Information.
b. Set the number of instances.
The value is an integer ranging from 1 to 1000. You are advised to set the
number to less than 10. More instances occupy more system resources,
which may result in insufficient resources. For distributed deployment, the
value ranges from 2 to 50.
c. Click OK.
3. Delete an instance.
a. Click in the upper right corner of the instance to be deleted.
b. In the displayed dialog box, click OK.
----End

NO TE
YARN and ODAE models do not support this configuration.
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > AI Model Management from the main

Step 3 Click Configurations in the Operation column corresponding to the service name.
Step 4 Click the Evaluation Policy Configuration tab.
Step 5 Configure an evaluation policy. For details about the evaluation parameters, see
Table 8-19.

accuracy

samples

Parameter Description
Retraining Triggering Policy
● If a model meets the configured rules, local model retraining is automatically
triggered.
● Federation models do not support this configuration.
Time rule Interval for triggering retraining.
● Triggered At Intervals: Trigger retraining by minute or
hour.
● Triggered By Period: Trigger retraining daily, weekly, or
monthly.
Available time If this parameter is set to Every month and the date of the
period last day in a month is earlier than the value of this
parameter, retraining tasks are not executed in the month.
For example, if retraining tasks are configured to be
executed on the 31st day of each month and there are only
30 days in June, retraining tasks are not executed in June.
This parameter is not displayed when Time rule is set to
Triggered At Intervals.
Maximum If the total training duration exceeds the configured value,
training time retraining tasks are automatically stopped.
Unit: hour
Value range: a positive number greater than 0

reserved instances stopped during retraining.

Optimal Upgrade Policy
The inspected model packages obtained from the AI marketplace on the can be
directly upgraded without the optimal selection.

Parameter Description
Optimal selection Parameter description:
● If the rule is set to F1, >, Old version, +, and 0.5000 and
the F1 value of the retraining model is greater than the
F1' value of the model of an earlier version, the optimal
selection condition is met, and you can upgrade the
model. The F1' value is the F1 value of the model of an
earlier version plus 0.5000.
● If the rule is set to F1, >, Absolute rule, and 0.5000 and
the F1 value of the retraining model is greater than
0.5000, the optimal selection condition is met, and you
can upgrade the model.
Indicator name:
● recall: recall rate
● precision: precision
● f1: harmonic average value of the precision and recall
rate
● event: event root alarm identification accuracy
● incident: incident root alarm identification accuracy
● topn: single-fault root alarm sorting identification
accuracy
Comparison operator: The options are > (greater than), <
(less than), <= (less than or equal to), >= (greater than or
equal to), ≠ (not equal to), and = (equal to).
Retraining model upgrade comparison rule:
● Old version: The retraining model is compared with the
model of the previous version. When the retraining
model meets the conditions, an upgrade is performed.
● Absolute rule: Evaluation indicator threshold. If the
indicator value of the retraining model meets the
threshold, an upgrade is performed.
Calculation operator: The options are +, -, and *. This
parameter is available only if the Retraining model
upgrade comparison rule is set to Old version.
Error/indicator value:
● If the Retraining model upgrade comparison rule is
set to Old version and the calculation operators is set to
+ or -, this parameter ranges from 0 to 1.
● If the Retraining model upgrade comparison rule is
set to Old version and the calculation operators is set to
*, this parameter ranges from 0 to 10000.
● If the Retraining model upgrade comparison rule is
set to Absolute rule, this parameter ranges from 0 to 1.
Click to add a preferred option.

Parameter Description
Retraining ● Upgrade: The model is automatically upgraded if the
upgrade conditions specified by Optimal selection are met.
● No upgrade: Only optimal selection is performed. No
automatic upgrade is performed.
● Automatic model upgrade
Y version change ● Upgrade
upgrade
● No upgrade
Step 6 Click Save.
----End

8.1.5.1.7 Model Details
Configuring Concept Drift Detection
NO TE
Spark, YARN, and ODAE models do not support this function.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > AI Model Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 Click the AI Asset Service tab.

Step 4 Click on the right of Model Evaluation/Feature Trend Chart and select
Concept Drift Setting.
Step 5 Set concept drift detection parameters based on Table 8-22.
Table 8-22 Parameters

menu

Step 3 Click the service name to go to the service details page.
Step 4 Click Configure Interface corresponding to Inference URL.
The Configure Interface window is displayed.
Step 5 Set the parameters based on the site requirements.
● Rate indicates the maximum number of accesses per second.
– For always online models, the rate is the access rate of the API of each
case instance.
– For on-demand startup models, the rate is the access rate of this API.
● Concurrency indicates the maximum number of concurrent connections. The

connections of the API of each case instance.
– For on-demand startup models, the concurrency is the number of
concurrent inference tasks.
● Buffer Queues indicates the maximum number of inference tasks that can be
cached in a queue. This parameter is available only to on-demand startup
models.
Step 6 Click OK.

back.

NO TICE

digits, special characters (-_.&), and spaces.
● Passwords must meet the following requirements:
– The value contains 8 to 128 characters.
– The value contains at least two types of the following characters:
1. Lowercase letters
2. Uppercase letters
3. Digits
4. Special characters: `~!@#$%^&*()_-=+\|[{}];:'",<.>/? and space
