8.1.5.2.4 Configuring a Model Package
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > AI Model Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 Click AI Asset Package on the left.
Step 3 Click Configurations in Operation column corresponding to the asset name. The
Model Config window is displayed.
1. Click the switch next to Model upload to enable or disable the function.
2. Set the maximum number of model packages that can be retained for the
selected assets in the model package repository to an integer ranging from 1
to 500. The default value is 50.
NO TE
If the number of imported model packages exceeds the maximum value, the system
automatically ages the model package that is not used for the longest time among
the selected assets.
If the number of model packages in an asset does not reach the maximum value but
the total number of model packages of all assets in the model package repository
reaches the maximum value or aging is triggered when the usage of the model
package repository exceeds 70%, the model package that is not used for the longest
time among all assets is automatically aged.
Step 4 Click OK.
----End
8.1.5.3 Model O&M Monitoring
You can monitor model service instances. The instance information that can be
monitored includes the number of restarts, instance statuses, and the trends of the
CPU usage, and memory usage. You can also monitor VM nodes. The node

instances deployed on the node.
Click in a blank area to query the overall Network AI Engine Infer Service
topology.
: Switch the topology structure to a hierarchical layout.
: Switch the topology structure to a radial layout.
: Click any one of these icons in the topology to display the deployed
inference services of the current AI feature.
: Click any one of these icons in the topology to display the attributes
(deployed instances) and data (the invoking times, invoking success rate, average
response duration, and others) of the current model service.
: Click any one of these icons in the topology to display the attributes and
data of the current instance.
: Click any one of these icons in the topology to display the attributes of the
current node.

be set as required.

8.2 System Management
This chapter describes system management operations.

User management provides functions such as user rights management ,area
management, and user maintenance and monitoring.
The user management function allows users with different responsibilities to be
granted proper permissions and allows users to adjust permissions based on
service changes. This ensures that users have the necessary permissions to
perform related tasks, thereby ensuring that other management tasks are carried
out in an orderly manner, preventing unauthorized operations and insecure
operations.
8.2.1.1 User Authorization
After system installation and commissioning, security administrators need to grant
different operation rights to users of different roles based on service planning.
8.2.1.1.1 Authorization Process
This section describes the user authorization process and methods.
Figure 8-11 shows the user authorization process.
Figure 8-11 User authorization process
You can bind a user to role and grant permissions to the user in the following
ways:
● Create a role and grant permissions to the role, create a user, and attach the

and grant permissions to the role.
8.2.1.1.2 Planning Authorization
Authorization planning classifies users based on their responsibilities. During user
authorization, first perform authorization planning to reduce the workload on
authorization and permission maintenance. Appropriate authorization planning
improves system security and usability.
The authorization planning principles are as follows:
● Users with the same responsibilities are attached to the same role. Users with
different responsibilities are attached to different roles.
● If multiple roles require the same application operations, plan a common
application operation set for these roles. If not, plan application operation sets
or grant application-level permissions to each role separately.
● If multiple roles require the same device operations, plan a device operation
set for these roles. If not, plan device operation sets or grant device-level
permissions to each role separately.
8.2.1.1.3 (Optional) Creating a Device Set
To efficiently grant permissions to users who manage the same objects from
different subnets or device types, security administrators need to plan and create a
device set before role authorization.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Managed Objects.
Step 3 On the Device Sets tab page, click Create and set basic information.
NO TE
You can quickly create a device set by clicking Copy in the Operation column of a device
set and adjusting the information.
Step 4 Click Select by Topology and Select by Device Type in Member Information to
select devices for the device set.
Step 5 Click OK.
----End
Related Tasks

● To modify a device set, click the name of the device set, click or Edit, and
modify basic information and members of the device set.
NO TE
If the device set to be modified is associated with a user, the modification will take
effect the next time the user logs in.
● To delete a device set, click Delete in the Operation column of the row that
contains the device set.
NO TE
If the device set to be deleted is associated with a user, the user will lose management
permissions on all the objects in this set.
8.2.1.1.4 (Optional) Creating an Operation Set
If operation sets are planned during authorization planning, security
administrators need to create operation sets before user authorization. Authorizing
roles using operation sets improves authorization efficiency.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Operation Sets.
Step 3 On the Operation Sets page, click the Application Operation Set or Device
Operation Set tab, and click Create.
NO TE
You can quickly create an operation set by clicking Copy in the Operation column of an
operation set and adjusting the information.
Step 4 On the page that is displayed, set Operation set name and Description.
Step 5 Select the permissions to be included in the operation set from the list of available
permissions.
Step 6 Click OK.
----End
Related Tasks
For details about how to perform related operations on operation sets, see
8.2.1.4.3 Common Operations for Operation Set Information Maintenance.

If the default roles provided by the system cannot meet user authorization
requirements in the authorization plan, security administrators need to create
roles and grant them permissions based on the plan.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
Context
● If only a few roles are required, create them one by one.
● If multiple roles are required or the roles are obtained from the files exported
from other systems, create the roles by importing files.
Procedure
● Creating a single role
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Roles.
Step 3 On the Roles page, click Create.
NO TE
You can quickly create a role by clicking Copy in the Operation column of the row that
contains the desired role and modifying the information.
Step 4 On the displayed page, set basic role information.
Step 5 Select the users to be attached to the role.
After role authorization is complete, the users you have selected have the
permissions included in this role.
Step 6 Click Next and select the managed objects included in the role based on the
authorization plan.
● All Objects: shows all the resources that can be managed by the system. It is
the default managed object provided by the system and cannot be modified
or deleted.
● Subnets: shows all manageable subnets in the system. If a subnet is selected
for a user, this user can manage the subnet and all its managed objects,
including devices and subnets.
● Devices: If a user needs to manage specified devices, you can grant
permissions by device.
● Resource Groups: If a resource group is selected for a role, the role can
manage all the resources in the group. Resources are grouped by resource
type.
Step 7 Click Next. Based on the authorization plan for application-level permissions and
device-level permissions of the role, set application-level permissions of the role
on the Application-Level tab page and set device-level permissions for each

operations that can be bound to it are displayed in Operations. Then, you can
select the devices operations to be authorized.
Step 8 Click OK.
----End
● Creating multiple roles at a time
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Roles.
Step 3 On the Roles page, click and choose Batch Import Roles.
Step 4 In the Batch Import Roles dialog box, click a template name to download the
template, and fill in the template.
NO TE
● The system provides two template formats: Role Template.xls and Role Template.xlsx,
and you can edit the template in .csv format. Select a template format as required.
● If you need to import the associated permissions, you can refer to the exported .zip
package that contains associated permission information as the template (file format,
file name, and table template) of the file to be imported.
Step 5 Set Import associated permissions and Permission import mode based on the
type of the file to be imported.
● If you need to import associated permissions, ensure that the file to be
imported is in .zip format and contains the information about associated
permissions of the roles.
● If Permission import mode is set to Based on file data or Combined, the
existing data in the system will be modified in batches and cannot be
restored. You are advised to export the existing data for backup in advance.
Step 6 Click the folder icon. In the dialog box that is displayed, select the modified
template.
Step 7 Click Create.
After the import is complete:
1. In the dialog box that is displayed, view the number of records that are
successfully imported and those that fail to be imported.
2. In the Result list, view the imported roles and their details.
3. If a partial failure occurs, modify the information about the roles that fail to
be imported based on the details, and import them again.
4. Click Download Report to download the report for this import. The report
cannot be downloaded after you close the dialog box.
Step 8 Click OK.
Step 9 Assign permissions to the roles created in batches based on the authorization

2. On the Managed Objects or Operation Rights tab page, click Edit, and
assign permissions to the role.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
If a user logs in to a third-party system in SSO mode, role information (excluding
permissions) about this user can be synchronized to the third-party system. To
ensure that this user has the same permissions on the third-party system as those
on the system, create the same role for the user on the third-party system and
bind the same permissions to the role.
Related Tasks
For details about how to maintain role information, see 8.2.1.4.2 Common
Operations for Role Information Maintenance.
8.2.1.1.6 (Optional) Configuring User Policies
User policies refer to rules for system accesses and operation control. The system
provides the default account policy and password policy. After authorization is
planned, you need to configure user policies if the default account policy or
password policy does not meet requirements or if IP address access control or
login time control is required for users.
For details about how to configure user policies, see 8.2.6 User Policy
Management.
8.2.1.1.7 Creating a User and Attaching the User to a Role
After a security administrator creates a user and attaches the user to a role, the
user has the permissions of this role.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
● An SMS server or email server has been configured in notifications to send
SMS or email verification codes, if you need to modify the contact
information, such as the mobile number or email address.
NO TE
● Navigation path for configuring notifications: Choose System > System Settings >
Notifications from the main menu.
Context
● If only a few users are required, create them one by one.
● If multiple users need to be created or users are obtained from the files
exported from other systems, create users in batches.
● If a user has logged in to the NetEco, disabling the user will forcibly log out

● Creating a single user
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Users.
Step 3 On the Users page, click Create.
NO TE
To quickly create a user, you can click Copy (or click and choose Copy) in the
Operation column of the row that contains the desired user and then adjust the
information.
Step 4 On the page that is displayed, set the basic user information.
Users are classified into three types: Local, Third-party, and Remote, with
different parameters for each user type.
● If the user is authenticated locally and is used to log in to the local system,
set the user type to Local.
● If the user is a machine-machine user for interconnecting with a third-party
system, set the user type to Third-party. Third-party users can log in only
through the third-party authentication interface, and are not allowed to log in
using a web browser.
● If the user is a user on the remote server who needs to log in to the local
system, set the user type to Remote. Ensure that the user exists on the
remote server. (For example, when an LDAP or RADIUS server is
interconnected, you need to create a user with the same username on the
local system.)
Step 5 Click Advanced Settings, set the user policy information, and click Next.

Step 6 Select the role to which the user is attached and click Next.
Click a role name to view the permissions of the role.
You can create a role and attach the user to the new role. For details about how
to create a role, see 8.2.1.1.5 Creating a Role and Granting Permissions. After
creating a role, you can click Refresh on the role list page and select the new role
for the user.
NO TICE
When creating a user as the system administrator user, you are advised not to
attach the new user to both the Administrators and SMManagers roles to ensure
system security. A user attached to both the Administrators and SMManagers
roles has the maximum permissions of all resources in the system. Exercise caution
when using such users to perform operations and do not perform any operations
that affect system security. For example, do not share or disclose the usernames or
passwords of these users.
Step 7 Select a login time policy and a client IP address policy for the user.
● A login time policy controls the time range when the user is allowed to log in
to the NetEco. Security administrators can modify login time policies on the
Login Time Policies page. For details, see 8.2.6.4 Setting a Login Time
Policy.
● A client IP address policy controls the IP addresses from which the user can
log in to the NetEco. Such policies can be classified into common policies and
personal policies, and personal policies take precedence over common
policies.
– Common policy: A common client IP address policy can be bound to all
users and takes effect for all users bound with the policy. Security
administrators can modify common policies on the Client IP Address
Policies page. For details, see 8.2.6.3 Setting a Client IP Address Policy.
– Personal policy: A personal policy is automatically bound to a user and
takes effect only for the bound user. After logging in to the system, users
who have the Update ACL Policy permission can modify personal client
IP address policies on the Personal Client IP Address Policies page. For
details, see 8.2.1.4.7 Modifying Personal Client IP Address Policies.
Step 8 Click OK.
----End

NO TE

passwords on the page.

8.2.1.2 Permission Adjustment
Permissions can be maintained after security administrators perform initial user
authorization. If the managed resources or users, or user responsibility is changed,
security administrators need to adjust user permissions.
8.2.1.2.1 Permission Adjustment After Subnets Are Added
After a subnet is added, security administrators need to plan the user who
manages the subnet and the device-level permissions for the new subnet, and
then assign the permissions to the role to which the user is attached.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
● Permissions for the new subnet have been planned.
Context
The operations in this section will change user permissions and the changes take
effect when the user logs in next time. Exercise caution when performing these

Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Roles.
Step 3 Check whether the role has been automatically assigned the permissions for the
new subnet.
● If the parent subnet of the new subnet has been added to the managed
objects of the role to which the user is attached and the operation sets bound
to the parent subnet contain the operations on the new subnet, the user is
automatically assigned the permissions for the new subnet after the subnet is
added. In this case, no further action is required.
● If the parent subnet of the new subnet has been added to the managed
objects of the role to which the user is attached, but the operation set bound
to the parent subnet does not contain operations on the new subnet, the user
does not have the permissions for the new subnet after the subnet is added.
In this case, go to Step 4.
● If the parent subnet of the new subnet is not added to the managed-object
scope of the role to which the user is attached, perform Step 5 and Step 6.
Step 4 Assign the role the device-level permissions for the new subnet.
1. On the Roles page, click the role name.
2. On the Operation Rights tab page, click the Device-Level tab.
3. Click Edit.
4. In the Authorization Objects list, select the parent subnet to which you want
to add a subnet. In the Operations list, select the device-level permissions
you want to assign to the new subnet.
5. Click OK.
Step 5 Add the new subnet to the managed-object scope of the role.
1. On the Roles page, click the role name.
2. On the Managed Objects tab page, click Edit.
3. On the Subnets tab page, select the new subnet.
4. Click OK.
Step 6 Assign device operation sets to the new subnet.
1. On the Roles page, click the role name.
2. On the Operation Rights tab page, click the Device-Level tab.
3. Click Edit.
4. In the Authorization Objects list, select the new subnet. In the Operations
list, select the device-level permissions you want to assign to the new subnet.
5. Click OK.
----End

After a device is added, security administrators need to plan the user who
manages the device and the device-level permissions for the new device, and then
assign the permissions to the role to which the user is attached.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
● Permissions for the new device have been planned.
Context
The operations in this section will change user permissions and the changes take
effect when the user logs in next time. Exercise caution when performing these
operations.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Roles.
Step 3 Check whether the role has been automatically assigned the permissions for the
new device.
● If the subnet where the new device resides has been added to the managed
objects of the role to which the user is attached and the operation sets bound
to the subnet contain the operations on the new device, the user is
automatically assigned the permissions for the new device after the device is
added. In this case, no further action is required.
● If the subnet where the new device resides has been added to the managed
objects of the role to which the user is attached and the operation sets bound
to the subnet do not contain the operations on the new device, the user does
not have the permission for the new device after the device is added. In this
case, go to Step 4.
● If none of the preceding assumptions are satisfied, the new device is beyond
the managed objects of the role to which the user is attached. In this case, go
to Step 5.
Step 4 Assign the role the permissions for the new device.
1. On the Roles page, click the role name.
2. On the Operation Rights tab page, click the Device-Level tab.
3. Click Edit.
4. Select the device-level permissions to be assigned to the new device.
5. Click OK.
Step 5 Add the new device to the managed objects of the role.
1. On the Roles page, click the desired role name. Click the Managed Objects
tab.

4. Click OK.
Step 6 Assign device-level permissions to the new device.
1. On the Roles page, click the role name. On the Operation Rights tab page,
click the Device-Level tab.
2. In the Authorization Objects list, select the device by referring to Step 5. In
the Operations list, select the operation sets to be assigned to the device.
3. Click OK.
----End
8.2.1.2.3 Permission Adjustment After Networking Modification
After networking is modified, permissions for managed objects involved in the
modification may need to be transferred to another role. In this case, security
administrators need to adjust the role permissions.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
● Permissions for the subnet whose networking is modified and for the devices
in the subnet have been planned.
Context
The operations in this section will change user permissions and the changes take
effect when the users log in next time. Exercise caution when performing these
operations.
Procedure
Step 1 Check whether the networking is globally reorganized.
● If so, plan users and roles again. For details, see 8.2.1.1 User Authorization.
● If not, go to Step 2.
Step 2 Adjust permissions for the moved devices.
● If devices are moved out of the network, users who manage the network lose
the permissions for the moved devices. In this case, no further action is
required.
● If devices are moved to the network, adjust permissions by referring to
8.2.1.2.2 Permission Adjustment After Devices Are Added.
----End
8.2.1.2.4 Permission Adjustment After the Objects Managed by Users Are Changed
If the permissions for an object need to be transferred from one role to another
while the managed-object topology is not changed, security administrators need

● You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
● Permissions for the target managed object have been planned.
Context
The operations in this section will change managed objects and permissions of the
users and the changes will take effect when the users log in next time. Exercise
caution when performing these operations.
Procedure
1. Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
2. In the navigation pane, choose Roles.
3. Click a role name. On the Managed Objects tab page, view the managed
objects included in the role and add or delete managed objects based on new
requirements.
4. On the Operation Rights tab page, modify the device-level permissions for
the modified managed objects.
8.2.1.2.5 Permission Adjustment After Permissions of Users Are Changed
If certain permissions attached to a role need to be added or deleted due to
responsibility changes, security administrators need to adjust the role and
permissions of this role.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
Context
● If role authorization is not performed based on operation sets, adjust the
permissions of the role by modifying the role.
● If role authorization is performed based on operation sets, adjust the
permissions of the role by modifying the operation sets.
● This operation will change the permissions of users attached to the role and
the changes will take effect when the users log in next time. Therefore,
exercise caution when performing this operation.
Procedure
● Modifying a role
a. Choose System > System Management > User Management from the
main menu.
b. In the navigation pane, choose Roles.
c. Click the role to be modified. On the Operation Rights tab page, click
Edit.
d. On the Application-Level or Device-Level tab page, view the operation

● Modifying an operation set
a. Choose System > System Management > User Management from the
main menu.
b. In the navigation pane, choose Operation Sets.
c. On the Application Operation Set or Device Operation Set tab page,
click the name of the desired operation set. On the page that is displayed,
click Edit.
d. View the permissions included in the operation set and add or delete
permissions.
NO TE
One operation may be included in multiple operation sets. Permissions of a role
can be deleted only when the operations are not included in any operation sets
attached to this role.
e. Click OK.
8.2.1.2.6 Permission Adjustment After a User's Role Is Changed
If the position of an employee has changed, security administrators need to adjust
the role to which the account of the employee is attached to change the
permissions of the employee.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
Context
● Disabling and deleting users will force a logout of logged-in users. Therefore,
exercise caution when performing these operations.
● A position change occurs in the following situations:
– An employee is recruited.
– The role of an employee (as a user) is changed.
– An employee resigns.
Procedure
● An employee is recruited.
a. Choose System > System Management > User Management from the
main menu.
b. In the navigation pane, choose Users.
c. On the Users page, click Create.
d. On the page that is displayed, set basic user information and click Next.
e. Select the role to which the user is attached and click Next.
f. Set access control information about the user and click OK.

main menu.
b. In the navigation pane, choose Users.
c. Click the name of the desired user and then click the Roles tab.
d. Click Edit in the upper right corner of the page.
e. Add or delete the role of a user based on the user position changes.
● An employee resigns.
a. Choose System > System Management > User Management from the
main menu.
b. In the navigation pane, choose Users. On the page that is displayed,
perform the following operation based on whether the account needs to
be retained:
▪
If you want to retain the account, click Disable in the Operation
column of the row that contains the user to disable the user.
▪
If you do not want to retain the account, click Delete in the
Operation column of the row that contains the user to delete the
user.
8.2.1.3 Regional User Permission Management
To improve management efficiency, security administrators divide the network into
regions based on service requirements and allow different personnel to manage
users and services in different regions. In a region, region administrators can
create roles, assign permissions to the roles, and attach users to roles to grant
them corresponding permissions. This implement user management in the region.
8.2.1.3.1 Planning Authorization in a Region
Before region management, the security administrator must plan authentication in
the region properly to reduce the workload of authorization and permission
maintenance in regions.
Figure 8-12 shows the process of authorization planning in a region. For details
about the authorization process, see Table 8-24.
Figure 8-12 Process of authorization planning in a region

Step Description
Plan regions. Plan regions based on the physical networking and service
requirements. You are advised to plan regions based on
administrative zones and managed objects.
● Plan the users who belong to the same administrative zone
to the same region.
● Plan the administrative zones that have the same managed
objects to the same region.
● A maximum of 70 regions can be planned.
Plan the Each region has region administrators. Plan the permissions and
authorization managed objects within the authorization scope of the region
scope of a administrators after planning regions.
region
administrator.
Plan Plan users who have same responsibilities to the same role.
managed Analyze permissions required for each role to fulfill their
objects, responsibilities and classify the permissions into operation sets.
operation
sets, roles,
and users in a
region.
8.2.1.3.2 Process of Managing a New Region
This section describes the region management process.
Figure 8-13 shows the region management process.

Creating a Region and an Administrator Role
Security administrators can create regions as planned to facilitate region-based
management and improve permission management efficiency.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Regions.
Step 3 On the page that is displayed, click +.
Step 4 On the Basic Information page, enter basic information about the region, and
click Next.
Step 5 On the Administrator Role page, enter the region administrator role name, and
click Next.

NO TE
After a region is created, the system automatically generates an administrator role. This
role has the User Management, Query Security Log, and Update ACL Policy permissions
by default.
Step 6 On the Authorization Scope-Managed Objects page, select the managed objects
that can be authorized in the region, and click Next.
Step 7 On the Authorization Scope-Operation Rights page, select the permissions that
can be authorized in the region, and click OK.
----End
Related Tasks
Security administrators can perform the following operations on the region as
required:
● To delete the region due to service changes, click in the upper right corner
of the region.
NO TE
Before deleting the region, you need to delete all users, roles, and operation sets from
the region.
● To modify information about a region:
a. On the Overview tab page of the target region, click to modify basic
information about the region.
b. On the Managed Objects or Operation Rights tab page, click Edit to
modify the authorization scope that can be managed by the region.
● To export role information by region: On the Roles tab page of the target
region, click and choose Export All Roles to export role information in
the target region.
NO TE
● If the number of roles exceeds 500, role information is exported to multiple files.
That is, each file contains information about a maximum of 500 roles.
● The file is exported in .csv or .xlsx format and downloaded to your local PC as
a .zip package.
● To export user information by region: On the Users tab page of the region,
click , choose Export All Users to export user information in the region.
Alternatively, select multiple users to be exported, click , and choose
Export Selected Users to export the information about the selected users in
the region.
● For details about other region maintenance operations, see 8.2.1.3.3
Maintaining an Existing Region.
Creating a Region Administrator
After creating a region, security administrators need to create a region

region.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Regions.
Step 3 In the region list, click the desired region.
Step 4 On the Users tab page, click Create to create a user.
Step 5 On the page for creating a user, set basic user information, click Advanced
Settings to set personal policy information, and click Next.
Parameters are described in Table 8-23.
Step 6 On the Select Roles page, select the region administrator role to which this user
needs to be attached and click Next.
Step 7 On the Access Policies page, set an access control policy for the user and click
OK.
If the existing access control policies do not meet requirements, you can create an
access control policy by following the instructions provided in 8.2.6.3 Setting a
Client IP Address Policy and 8.2.6.4 Setting a Login Time Policy.
After security administrators create a region administrator, the new region
administrator will be displayed in the user list of the region. The region
administrator can use this user account to log in to the NetEco and manage user
permissions in the region.
----End
Related Tasks
Security administrators can perform the following operations as required:
● To view region administrator information: Click the desired region
administrator name to view basic information, permissions, and access
policies of this administrator.
NO TE
Security administrators can view information about all region administrators. Region
administrators can view information about region administrators in their own regions.
● To export users in a region: Click , and choose Export All Users or

NO TE
● The Password, Mobile number, and Email address fields are not exported.
Therefore, the values of these fields are empty in the exported user information
file.
● Security administrators can export information about all the users (including users
in regions) in the system.
● The files are exported in .csv or .xlsx format and downloaded to your local PC as
a .zip package.
Creating Operation Sets in a Region as Planned
Region administrators can create operation sets in a region to improve permission
management efficiency in the region.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a region administrator.
Context
If the authorization scope of a region administrator contains a default operation
set, the region automatically inherits the default operation set.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Operation Sets.
Step 3 On the Operation Sets page, click the Application Operation Set or Device
Operation Set tab, and click Create.
NO TE
You can quickly create an operation set by clicking Copy in the Operation column of an
operation set and adjusting the information.
Step 4 On the page that is displayed, set Operation set name and Description.
Step 5 Select operations for the operation set.
If unnecessary operations exist in the list, click Delete to delete them.
NO TE
A region administrator can create operation sets only based on the authorization scope of
the region administrator role.
Step 6 Click OK.
----End
Related Tasks
After creating an operation set in the region, you can perform the following

operation set.
● To modify an operation set: Click the name of the operation set to be
modified, and then click Edit.
● To delete one or more operation sets: Click Delete in the Operation column
of the row that contains the desired operation set. Alternatively, select
multiple operation sets, and click Delete above the list.
● To import operation sets: Click and choose Import Operation Sets.
NO TE
If some permissions in an operation set are not within the authorization scope of the
administrator, the operation set does not contain these permissions after being
imported.
● To export all operation sets except the full operation sets, click and
choose Export All Operation Sets.
● To export selected operation sets: Click and choose Export Selected
Operation Sets.
Creating a Role in a Region and Granting Permissions
A region administrator can create common roles in the region to centrally grant
permissions to users in this region.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a region administrator.
Context
● If only a few roles are required in a region, create them one by one.
● If multiple roles are required in a region or the roles are obtained from the
files exported from other systems, create roles in batches.
● The operations in this section will change user permissions. Exercise caution
when performing these operations.
Procedure
● Creating a single common role in a region
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Roles.
Step 3 On the Roles page, click Create.
NO TE
You can quickly create a role by clicking Copy in the Operation column of the row that
contains the desired role and modifying the information.
Step 4 On the page that is displayed, enter the basic role information, select users to be

NO TE
If a user has been attached to a regional administrator role, this user cannot be attached to
a common role.
Step 5 Select the managed objects included in the role based on the role plan during
authorization planning.
● All Objects: shows all the resources that can be managed by the system. It is
the default managed object provided by the system and cannot be modified
or deleted.
● Subnets: shows all the subnets that can be managed by the system. If a
subnet is selected for a user, the user can manage the subnet and all its
managed objects, including devices and subnets.
● Resource Groups: If a resource group is selected for a role, the role can
manage all the resources in the group. Resources are grouped by resource
type.
Step 6 Based on the authorization plan for application-level permissions and device-level
permissions of the role, set application-level permissions of the role on the
Application-Level tab page and set device-level permissions for each managed
object included in the role on the Device-Level tab page.
When you set device-level permissions for a managed object, select the managed
object first and then select the device operation sets to be bound to it. After you
select a managed object, device operation sets that can be bound to it are listed in
Operations.
Roles in regions can only be authorized by operation set. For details about how to
create an operation set in a region, see Creating Operation Sets in a Region as
Planned.
Step 7 Confirm role information and click OK.
----End
● Creating multiple common roles in a region
Security administrators can import roles of any region. Region administrators
can import only roles of their own regions.
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Roles.
Step 3 On the Roles page, click and choose Batch Import Roles.
Step 4 In the Batch Import Roles dialog box, click a template name to download the
template, and fill in the template.
NO TE
● The system provides two template formats: Role Template.xls and Role Template.xlsx,
and you can edit the template in .csv format. Select a template format as required.
● If you need to import the associated permissions, you can refer to the exported .zip
package that contains associated permission information as the template (file format,

type of the file to be imported.
● If you need to import associated permissions, ensure that the file to be
imported is in .zip format and contains the information about associated
permissions of the roles.
● If Permission import mode is set to Based on file data or Combined, the
existing data in the system will be modified in batches and cannot be
restored. You are advised to export the existing data for backup in advance.
Step 6 Click the folder icon. In the dialog box that is displayed, select the modified
template.
Step 7 Click Create.
After the import is complete:
1. In the dialog box that is displayed, view the number of records that are
successfully imported and those that fail to be imported.
2. In the Result list, view the imported roles and their details.
3. If a partial failure occurs, modify the information about the roles that fail to
be imported based on the details, and import them again.
4. Click Download Report to download the report for this import. The report
cannot be downloaded after you close the dialog box.
Step 8 Click OK.
Step 9 Assign permissions to the roles created in batches based on the authorization
plan.
1. On the Roles page, click a role name.
2. On the Managed Objects or Operation Rights tab page, click Edit, and
assign permissions to the role.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
If a user logs in to a third-party system in SSO mode, role information (excluding
permissions) about this user can be synchronized to the third-party system. To
ensure that this user has the same permissions on the third-party system as those
on the system, create the same role for the user on the third-party system and
bind the same permissions to the role.
Related Tasks
You can perform the following operations as required:
● Viewing role information in a region: Click the name of the role whose
information will be viewed.
● Modifying role information in a region: Click the name of the role whose
information will be modified, and then click Edit.
● Deleting roles: To delete a single role, click Delete in the Operation column
of the row that contains this role. To delete multiple roles, select the roles to

NO TE
You cannot delete the region administrator role or the roles of the user you are logged
in as.
● Exporting role information: Click , and choose Export All Roles.
Alternatively, select the desired roles, click , and choose Export Selected
Roles.
NO TE
● Each file contains information about a maximum of 500 roles. If the number of
roles exceeds 500, the role information is exported to multiple files.
● A region administrator can export only roles of the region to which the region
administrator belongs.
● The file is exported in .csv or .xlsx format and downloaded to your local PC as
a .zip package.
● Saving information about role permissions: On the Operation Rights tab
page, click Save to save the information about the role permissions.
NO TE
● Information about permissions of the Administrators and SMManagers roles
cannot be saved.
● The exported file is in .csv or .xlsx format and can be downloaded to your local PC.
Creating a User in a Region and Attaching the User to a Role
After a region administrator creates a user and attaches the user to a role, the
user has the permissions of this role.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the NetEco as a region administrator.
● An SMS server or email server has been configured in notifications to send
SMS or email verification codes, if you need to modify the contact
information, such as the mobile number or email address.
NO TE
● Navigation path for configuring notifications: Choose System > System Settings >
Notifications from the main menu.
Context
● If only a few users are required in a region, create them one by one.
● If multiple users need to be created in a region or the users are obtained from
the files exported from other systems, create users in batches.
● The operations in this section involve user permission adjustment, which will
forcibly log out corresponding online users. Therefore, exercise caution when
performing this operation.
Procedure

menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Users.
Step 3 On the Users page, click Create to create a user.
NO TE
● To quickly create a user, you can click Copy (or click and choose Copy) in the
Operation column of the row that contains the desired user and then adjust the
information as required.
● If you are copying from a region administrator, the new user will be detached from the
region administrator role.
Step 4 On the displayed page, set basic user information.
Users are classified into three types: Local, Third-party, and Remote, with
different parameters for each user type.
● If the user is authenticated locally and is used to log in to the local system,
set the user type to Local.
● If the user is a machine-machine user for interconnecting with a third-party
system, set the user type to Third-party. Third-party users can log in only
through the third-party authentication interface, and are not allowed to log in
using a web browser.
● If the user is a user on the remote server who needs to log in to the local
system, set the user type to Remote. Ensure that the user exists on the
remote server. (For example, when an LDAP or RADIUS server is
interconnected, you need to create a user with the same username on the
local system.)
Step 5 (Optional) Click Advanced Settings, set parameters such as Max. online sessions,
and click Next.
Parameters are described in Table 8-23.
Step 6 Select the role to which the user is attached and click Next.
Click a role name to view information about the role, such as its permissions and
managed objects.
NO TE
A user cannot be attached to both the region administrator role and a common role of the
region.
Step 7 Set access control information for the user and click OK.
● A login time policy controls the time range when the user is allowed to log in
to the NetEco. Region administrators can view login time policies on the
Login Time Policies page. For details, see 8.2.6.4 Setting a Login Time
Policy.
● A client IP address policy controls the IP addresses from which the user can
log in to the NetEco. Such policies can be classified into common policies and
personal policies. Personal policies take precedence over common policies.
– Common policy: A common client IP address policy can be bound to

Address Policies page. For details, see 8.2.6.3 Setting a Client IP
Address Policy.
– Personal policy: A personal policy is automatically bound to a user and
takes effect only for the bound user. After logging in to the system, users
who have the Update ACL Policy permission can modify personal client
IP address policies on the Personal Client IP Address Policies page. For
details, see 8.2.1.4.7 Modifying Personal Client IP Address Policies.
----End
● Creating multiple users at a time
Region administrators can import users only to their own regions.
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Users.
Step 3 On the Users page, click and choose Import Users.
Step 4 In the Import Users dialog box, click a template name to download the template
and enter user information in the template.
NO TE
The system provides two template formats: User Template.xls and User Template.xlsx,
and you can edit the template in .csv format. Select a template format as required.
Step 5 Set Import Mode to Create users.
NO TE
If the passwords for the non-remote users are not configured in the imported file, set the
passwords on the page.
Step 6 Click the folder icon. In the dialog box that is displayed, select the modified
template.
Step 7 Click Create. After the import is complete:
1. In the dialog box that is displayed, view the number of records that are
successfully imported and those that fail to be imported.
2. In the Result list, view the imported users and their details.
3. If a partial failure occurs, modify the information about the users that failed
to be imported based on the details, and import them again.
Step 8 Click OK.
----End
Related Tasks
● Viewing information about users in a region: Select a username and view
Basic Information, Roles, and Access Policies.
● Modifying information about users in a region: Select a username and then

Operation column of the row that contains the user.
● Deleting users in a region: To delete a single user, click Delete in the
Operation column of the row that contains the user. To delete multiple users
at a time, select the users and click Delete.
● Disabling a user in a region: Click Disable in the Operation column of the
row that contains the user.
● Enabling a user in a region: Click Enable in the Operation column of the row
that contains the user.
● Unlocking users:
– To unlock a user: Click and choose Unlock in the Operation column
of the row that contains the user.
– To unlock multiple users at a time: Select the users to be unlocked, click
, and choose Batch Unlock Users.
● Exporting users in a region:
a. Click and choose Export All Users. Alternatively, select the desired
users, click , and choose Export Selected Users.
b. Set File format and export user information.
NO TE
● Password, Mobile number, Email address, and Department of users are not
exported. Therefore, these fields do not exist or are left empty in the exported
user information file.
● Security administrators can export information about all the users (including
users in regions) in the system.
● The files are exported in .csv or .xlsx format and downloaded to your local PC
as a .zip package.
8.2.1.3.3 Maintaining an Existing Region
After a region and users in the region are created, the region enters the user
maintenance period. Security administrators can adjust regions and region
administrator permissions as required. Region administrators can maintain user
permissions in their regions.
Modifying User Information in a Region in Batches
Region administrators can modify user information (such as Max. online sessions
and Login Time Policy) in batches, improving system security.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a region administrator.
Context
● You can use the following methods to modify user information in batches:

Modify Users.
– Modifying an exported user information file
Export user information as a file, edit the file, and then import it to the
system.
● The operations in this section involve user permission adjustment, which will
forcibly log out corresponding online users. Therefore, exercise caution when
performing this operation.
Procedure
● Using the Batch Modify Users function
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Users.
Step 3 In the user list, select one or more users, click , and choose Batch Modify
Users.
The information about the user you are logged in as cannot be modified. The
information about a maximum of 100 users can be modified at a time.
Step 4 Check that the users on the User List page are consistent with those you want to
modify, and click Next.
To modify a user in User List, perform the following operations:
● If some users to be modified are not in the list, click Cancel and select the
users to be modified again.
● If some users who do not need to be modified are in the list, click Delete in
the Operation column of the rows that contain these users to delete them.
Step 5 On the Modification Items page, modify user information.
NO TE
● After the login time policy is modified, the users who do not meet the policy
requirements will be forcibly logged out.
Step 6 Click OK. On the Modification Result page, view the modification results and
details.
----End
● Modifying an exported user information file
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Users.
Step 3 Click and choose Export All Users. Alternatively, select the desired users,

NO TE
● Password, Mobile number, Email address, and Department of users are not exported.
Therefore, these values are empty in the exported user information file.
● Security administrators can export information about all the users (including users in
regions) in the system.
Step 4 In the Export Settings dialog box, set File format and click OK.
Step 5 Modify the exported user information file.
● When modifying the user information file, do not change the sequence of the
fields, the file format, or the file name.
● If you change the value of a field to empty or an invalid value, the value of
this field will not be changed.
● The following user information cannot be modified:
– Type, Region, Last Login, Created On of all users
– Information about the user you are currently logged in as
– Password, Login Time Policy, Client IP Address Policy, Personal Client
IP Address Policy, and Password Validity Period (Days) of remote users
– Password and Auto-Logout If No Activity Within of third-party users
Step 6 Click and choose Import Users.
Step 7 In the Import Users dialog box, select Modify users.
Step 8 Click the folder icon, select the modified file, and click Modify.
Step 9 After the import is complete:
1. In the dialog box that is displayed, view the number of records that are
successfully modified and those that fail to be modified.
2. In the Result list, view the imported users and their details.
3. If a partial failure occurs, modify the information about the users that failed
to be imported based on the details, and import them again.
Step 10 Click OK.
----End
Moving Roles from the Default Region to a Custom Region
Security administrators can move roles from the default region to a custom
region. The roles are then managed by the destination region administrator.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
● A region has been created. For details about how to create a region, see
Creating a Region and an Administrator Role.

The operations in this section will detach roles from related users, managed
objects, and permissions. Therefore, exercise caution when performing this
operation.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Roles.
Step 3 Select the roles to be moved, click , and choose Move Selected Roles.
NO TE
Default roles, such as Administrators and SMManagers, and roles to which the current
user is attached cannot be moved.
Step 4 Confirm the roles to be moved in the list. Click Next.
Step 5 Select the destination region.
NO TE
After a role is moved to another region, related users, managed objects, and permissions
will be detached from the role.
Step 6 Click Move. On the Moving Result page, view the moving results and details.
After a role is moved, it is displayed in the role list of the destination region.
Step 7 Click OK.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
After moving a role to another region, assign managed objects and permissions to
the role again.
Moving Users from the Default Region to Another Region
Security administrators can move local users from the default region to another
region. The local users are then managed by the destination region administrator.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
● A region has been created. For details about how to create a region, see
Creating a Region and an Administrator Role.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main

Step 3 Select the users to be moved, click , and choose Move Selected Users.
NO TE
Users attached to default roles, default users, third-party users, remote users, and the
current user cannot be moved.
Step 4 Confirm the users to be moved in the list. Click Next.
Step 5 Select the destination region.
NO TE
After a user is moved out of the region, related roles will be detached from the user. If the
user has logged in to the NetEco, this operation will forcibly log out the user. Therefore,
exercise caution when performing this operation.
Step 6 Click Move. On the Moving Result page, view the moving results and details.
After a user is moved, it is displayed in the user list of the destination region.
Step 7 Click OK.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
After a user is moved to another region, attach the user to roles again.
Moving Common Roles Out of a Region
When managing multiple regions, security administrators may need to adjust
some common roles across regions. For example, security administrators may need
to move a common role to another region.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
● A region has been created. For details about how to create a region, see
Creating a Region and an Administrator Role.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Regions.
Step 3 Click the region to which to-be-moved roles belong to open the page of this
region.
Step 4 Click the Roles tab, select the roles to be moved, click , and choose Move
Selected Roles.

NO TE
After a role is moved to another region, related users, managed objects, and permissions
will be detached from the role. Perform this operation with caution.
Step 7 Click Move. On the Moving Result page, view the moving results and details.
After a role is moved, it is displayed in the role list of the destination region.
Step 8 Click OK.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
After moving a role to another region, assign managed objects and permissions to
the role again.
Moving Users Out of a Region
When maintaining multiple regions, security administrators need to adjust the
responsibilities of some users across regions. For example, security administrators
may need to move a local user in a region to another region.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
● A region has been created. For details about how to create a region, see
Creating a Region and an Administrator Role.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Regions.
Step 3 Click the region to which to-be-moved users belong to open the User
Management page for the region.
Step 4 Select the users to be moved, click , and choose Move Selected Users.
NO TE
Third-party users, remote users, and region administrators cannot be moved.
Step 5 Confirm the users to be moved in the list. Click Next.
NO TE
To modify a user in User List, perform the following operations:
● If some users to be moved are not in the list, click Cancel and select the users to be
moved again.
● If some users who do not need to be moved are in the list, click Delete in the

NO TE
● After a user is moved, related roles will be detached from the user.
● Moving users out of a region will forcibly log out the users if they have logged in to the
NetEco. Therefore, exercise caution when performing this operation.
Step 7 Click Move. On the Moving Result page, view the moving results and details.
After a user is moved, it is displayed in the user list of the destination region.
Step 8 Click OK.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
After a user is moved out of a region, attach the user to roles again.
8.2.1.3.4 Monitoring Online Users in a Region
Online user sessions in a region can be monitored so that region administrators
can learn about the online users of this region in the system, and the access
addresses, access time, and roles of the users. When detecting an unauthorized
user operation, the region administrators can send a message to the user or
forcibly log out the user.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a region administrator.
Context
● A user session refers to a connection between a user and the system. A
session starts when the user logs in to the system and ends when the user
logs out of the system. The maximum number of online sessions for a user is
specified by the Max. online sessions parameter. Online sessions of users
(excluding third-party users) consume license resource items.
● User sessions in a region can be monitored only by the administrators of the
region.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Online Users.
The status information about all online users in the region is displayed in the list.
Step 3 Click Monitor in the Operation column of the row that contains the desired user
to view the operation records of the user.
● The User Operations list displays only operations performed after you open
the page. Operations of the monitored users are updated in the User

Warning. The value of Level is the same as that recorded in operation logs.
Step 4 When detecting that a user is performing risky operations, click Log Out in the
Operation column of the row that contains the user to forcibly log out the user.
NO TICE
This operation may forcibly log out online users. Therefore, exercise caution when
performing this operation.
Step 5 To send a message to a user, click Send Message in the Operation column of the
row that contains the user.
----End
8.2.1.4 User Maintenance
Security administrators can maintain user, role, and operation set information, and
reset other users' passwords. Users can modify their personal information.

information, and modifying user information.

You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.

8.2.1.4.4 Modifying User Information in Batches
Security administrators can modify user information (such as Max. online
sessions and Login Time Policy) in batches, improving system security.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
Context
● You can use the following methods to modify user information in batches:
– Using the Batch Modify Users function
Select the users whose information needs to be modified and click Batch
Modify Users.
– Modifying an exported user information file
Export user information as a file, edit the file, and then import it to the
system.
● The operations in this section involve user permission adjustment, which may
forcibly log out online users. Therefore, exercise caution when performing this
operation.
Procedure
● Using the Batch Modify Users function
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Users.
Step 3 In the user list, select one or more users, click , and choose Batch Modify
Users.
The information about the system administrator and the user you are currently
logged in as cannot be modified. The information about a maximum of 100 users

modify, and click Next.
To modify a user in User List, perform the following operations:
● If some users to be modified are not in the list, click Cancel and select the
users to be modified again.
● If some users who do not need to be modified are in the list, click Delete in
the Operation column of the rows that contain these users to delete them.
Step 5 On the Modification Items page, select the user information to be modified as
required.
If the existing time policies do not meet the requirements, click Create to create a
policy and then click Refresh. Then, you can select the new time policy from the
time policy list.
NO TE
● After the login time policy is modified, the users who do not meet the policy
requirements will be forcibly logged out if Log out of sessions that do not comply
with client IP address or login time policies is selected in the account policy.
Step 6 Click OK. On the Modification Result page, view the modification results and
details.
----End
● Modifying an exported user information file
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Users.
Step 3 Click and choose Export All Users. Alternatively, select the desired users,
click , and choose Export Selected Users.
NO TE
Assign permissions to users directly in the account policy must be enabled before you
import or export associated permissions.
Step 4 In the Export Settings dialog box, set File format, and click OK.
Step 5 Modify the exported user information file.
● When modifying the user information, do not change the sequence of the
fields, the file format, or the file name.
● If you change the value of a field to empty or an invalid value, the value of
this field will not be changed.
● The following user information cannot be modified:
– Type, Region, Last Login, Created On of all users
– Information about the user you are currently logged in as and the system
administrator user
– Password, Login Time Policy, Client IP Address Policy, Personal Client

– Password and Auto-Logout If No Activity Within of third-party users
Step 6 Click and choose Import Users.
Step 7 In the Import Users dialog box, set Import Mode to Modify users.
Step 8 Click the folder icon, select the modified file, and click Modify.
Step 9 After the import is complete:
● In the dialog box that is displayed, view the number of records that are
successfully modified and those that fail to be modified.
● In the Result list, view the imported users and their details.
● If a partial failure occurs, modify the information about the users that failed
to be imported based on the details, and import them again.
Step 10 Click OK.
----End
8.2.1.4.5 Changing Personal Passwords
If passwords are leaked or remain unchanged for a long time, users can change
their personal passwords by setting personal information. For security purposes, it
is recommended that passwords be changed periodically (for example, every three
months). When a user logs in to the NetEco for the first time or after the security
administrator resets the user password, the user needs to change the password.
Context
● If you cannot change your password, contact security administrators.
● If Log out session after password change is enabled in the password policy,
all sessions of the user are logged out after the user changes the password on
the Personal Settings page. Exercise caution when performing this operation.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > Personal Settings from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Change Password.
Step 3 On the Change Password page, enter Old password and set New password and
Confirm password.
Step 4 Click Apply.
NO TE
Generally, user information is more secure if a password is changed more frequently. If a
user forgets the password due to frequent password changes, the user needs to contact
security administrators to reset the password.

In the following cases, the system prompts a user to change the password after
the user logs in to the NetEco:
● The user uses the initial password for login.
● The password for login is about to expire.
● The password that the system administrator user uses for login has expired.
● The user account is enabled again after being disabled, but the user uses the
password when the account was disabled for login.
8.2.1.4.6 Modifying Personal Information
If your personal information, such as the mobile number and email address,
changes or needs to be supplemented, you can maintain your personal
information in Personal Settings to ensure its accuracy.
Prerequisites
An SMS server or email server has been configured in notifications to send SMS or
email verification codes, if you need to modify the contact information, such as
the mobile number or email address.
NO TE
Navigation path for configuring notifications: Choose System > System Settings >
Notifications from the main menu.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > Personal Settings from the main menu.
NO TICE
● For personal information, such as mobile numbers and email addresses,
considerable measures must be taken in compliance with the laws of the
countries or regions concerned and the user privacy policies of the company
involved, to ensure that the personal data is fully protected.
● For security purposes, personal information, such as mobile numbers and email
addresses, is anonymized on the page, and is transferred through HTTPS
encrypted transmission channels.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Modify Personal Info.
Step 3 On the Modify Personal Info page, modify personal information. For details, see
Table 8-28.

8.2.1.4.7 Modifying Personal Client IP Address Policies
A client IP address policy provides a control mechanism for checking the
accessibility of the IP address used by an external access request. In this way, users
can log in to the NetEco only from specified IP address segments. With the
Update ACL Policy permission, you can configure your personal client IP address
policies. ACL is short for access control list.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > Personal Settings from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Personal Client IP Address Policies.
Step 3 On the Personal Client IP Address Policies page, create, view, or modify your
client IP address policies.
NO TE
The client IP address policies you created or modified take effect for the user you are
currently logged in as.
----End
8.2.1.4.8 Resetting a User Password
If a user forgets the password or cannot change the password, this user needs to

reset the password.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator or the system
administrator user.
Context
● If a user has logged in to the NetEco and Force logout upon password reset
in the password policy is selected, resetting the password for the user will
forcibly log out this user. Therefore, exercise caution when performing this
operation.
● You are not allowed to reset the password for the system administrator user.
The password for the system administrator user cannot be retrieved. You can
reset the password for the system administrator user only by reinstalling the
system. Therefore, remember the password.
● For account security, it is recommended that third-party users contact security
administrators to periodically reset their passwords.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Users.
Step 3 Click Reset Password in the Operation column of the row that contains the
desired user.
Step 4 Set a new password and click OK.
----End
8.2.1.5 Monitoring Online Users
Online user sessions can be monitored so that security administrators can learn
about the online users in the system, and the access addresses, access time, and
roles of these users. When detecting an unauthorized user operation, security
administrators can send a message to the user or forcibly log out the user.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
Context
● A user session refers to a connection between a user and the system. A
session starts when the user logs in to the system and ends when the user
logs out of the system. The maximum number of online sessions for a user is
specified by the Max. online sessions parameter. Online sessions of local
users and remote users (excluding third-party users) consume license resource

Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Online Users.
The information about all online users is displayed in the list.
Step 3 Click Monitor in the Operation column of the row that contains the desired user
to view the operation records of the user.
● The User Operations list displays only operations performed after you open
the page. Operations of the monitored users are updated in the User
Operations list in real time.
● Level indicates the risk level of an operation, including Risk, Minor, and
Warning. The value of Level is the same as that recorded in operation logs.
Step 4 To send a message to a user, click Send Message in the Operation column of the
row that contains the user. Alternatively, select multiple users and click Send
Message to the upper right of the user list to send messages to these users.
Step 5 When detecting that a user is performing risky operations, click Log Out in the
Operation column of the user to forcibly log out the user. Alternatively, select
multiple users and click Log Out to the upper right of the user list to forcibly log
out these users.
NO TICE
This operation may forcibly log out online users. Therefore, exercise caution when
performing this operation.
----End
8.2.2 Personal Settings
This feature enables users to customize personal settings.
8.2.2.1 Changing Personal Password
If passwords are disclosed or remain unchanged for a long time, users can change
their personal passwords by setting personal information. To improve user security,
it is recommended that passwords be changed periodically (for example, every
three months).
Context

Step 1 Choose System > System Management > Personal Settings.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Change Password.
Step 3 On the Change Password tab page, enter Old password and set New password
and Confirm password.
Step 4 Click Apply.
NO TE
User information is more secure if a password is changed more frequently. If a user forgets
the password due to frequent password changes, contact security administrators to reset
the password.
----End
