Step 10 to that of the standby system and use the modified file as the IdP metadata file for
the standby system to interconnect with the SP system. For details about how to change
the IP address in the metadata file, see 8.2.8.4.4 Obtaining the Metadata File of the
Standby System.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
● After configuring the SAML SSO on all the systems, check whether the SAML
SSO is configured successfully as follows:
Step 1 On the login page of the SP system, click Other Login Methods.
Step 2 Click the name of the desired IdP.
Step 3 On the login page of the IdP system, enter the username and password, and click
Log In.
Step 4 On the page for the SP system, check that the displayed username is
username(SAML).

This section describes how to configure SAML SSO interconnection on the third-
party IdP system.
Prerequisites
● User information has been configured in the IdP system.
● If the IdP to be interconnected requires the CA certificate of the NetEco for
authentication, contact the system administrator to obtain the trust certificate
of the NetEco issued by the CA.
Procedure
Step 1 Obtain the metadata file of the SP.
1. Log in to the NetEco as the admin user.
2. Choose System > System Management > Authentication from the main
menu.
3. In the navigation pane, choose SSO Configuration > SAML SSO
Configuration.
4. Click Export Metadata to export the metadata file.
5. Use a browser to open the metadata file, right-click the file, and choose Save
As from the shortcut menu. Save the metadata file to the IdP\metadata
directory in the IdP installation path, and set the file name, for example, SP-
metadata.xml.
Step 2 Configure the metadata file of the SP on the IdP and configure the path of the
metadata file in the metadata-providers.xml file from the IdP installation files.
Add the following information to the metadata-providers.xml file:
<MetadataProvider id="LocalMetadata1" xsi:type="FilesystemMetadataProvider" metadataFile="IdP
installation directory\IdP\metadata\SP-metadata.xml"/>
NO TE
If the file contains multiple values of MetadataProvider id, ensure that each value is
unique.
Step 3 Configure EntityID of the NetEco (SP information) in the attribute-filter.xml file
from the IdP installation files.
Step 4 Restart the IdP system.
Step 5 Save the idp-metadata.xml file to configure the SP.
Step 6 After configuring the IdP, you need to configure the SP on the NetEco to establish
a trust relationship between the NetEco and IdP for SAML SSO interconnection.
For details, see 8.2.8.4.2 Configuring SAML SSO.
----End
Operation Example
The following uses Shibboleth IdP v3 as an example to describe how to configure

1. Log in to the NetEco as the admin user.
2. Choose System > System Management > Authentication from the main
menu.
3. In the navigation pane, choose SSO Configuration > SAML SSO
Configuration.
4. Click Export Metadata to export the metadata file.
5. Use a browser to open the metadata file, right-click the file, choose Save As
from the shortcut menu. Save the file to the Shibboleth IdP installation path
\Shibboleth\IdP\metadata directory, and set the file name, for example, SP-
metadata.xml.
NO TE
Replace Shibboleth IdP installation path with the actual installation path of the IdP
system.
Step 2 Configure the metadata file path in the Shibboleth IdP installation path
\Shibboleth\IdP\conf\metadata-providers.xml file. Add the following
information to the metadata-providers.xml file:
<MetadataProvider id="LocalMetadata1" xsi:type="FilesystemMetadataProvider" metadataFile="Shibboleth
IdP installation path\Shibboleth\IdP\metadata\SP-metadata.xml"/>
NO TE
If the file contains multiple values of MetadataProvider id, ensure that each value is
unique.
Step 3 Configure EntityID of the NetEco in the Shibboleth IdP installation path
\Shibboleth\IdP\conf\attribute-filter.xml file. In the attribute-filter.xml file,
change the value of value to that of EntityID in the metadata file of the NetEco.
<afp:AttributeFilterPolicy id="SP-metadata">
<afp:PolicyRequirementRule xsi:type="basic:AttributeRequesterString" value="https://
10.247.47.124:31943" />
<afp:AttributeRule attributeID="eduPersonPrincipalName">
<afp:PermitValueRule xsi:type="basic:ANY" />
</afp:AttributeRule>
<afp:AttributeRule attributeID="uid">
<afp:PermitValueRule xsi:type="basic:ANY" />
</afp:AttributeRule>
<afp:AttributeRule attributeID="mail">
<afp:PermitValueRule xsi:type="basic:ANY" />
</afp:AttributeRule>
</afp:AttributeFilterPolicy>
Step 4 Restart the Shibboleth IdP system.
1. Go to the Shibboleth IdP installation path\Shibboleth\ProcRun directory and
double-click the shibd_idpw.exe file.
2. Click Stop to stop the IdP system, and then click Start to start the IdP system.
After the IdP system is restarted, click OK.
NO TE
If the restart fails, check whether any process on the local host conflicts with the

\IdP\metadata\idp-metadata.xml directory to configure the SP.
Step 6 After configuring Shibboleth, you need to configure the SP on the NetEco to
establish a trust relationship between the NetEco and Shibboleth for SAML SSO
interconnection. For details, see 8.2.8.4.2 Configuring SAML SSO.
----End
8.2.8.4.4 Obtaining the Metadata File of the Standby System
If the system is deployed in active/standby mode and the login URLs of the active
and standby systems are different, you can modify the metadata file of the active
system to obtain the metadata file of the standby system.
Procedure
Step 1 On the active system, obtain the metadata file.
1. Choose System > System Management > Authentication from the main
menu.
2. In the navigation pane, choose SSO Configuration > SAML SSO
Configuration, and perform the following operations:
– On the SP Configuration tab page, click Export Metadata to export the
metadata file of the current system that functions as the IdP.
– On the IdP Configuration tab page, click Export Metadata to export the
metadata file of the current system that functions as the SP.
Step 2 Use Notepad to open the exported metadata file and change all the redirect URLs
in the file to the redirect URL of the standby system.
NO TE
If the redirect URL is customized, replace the value of entityID with the corresponding IP
address.
In the following example, the redirection URL of the active system is
10.212.196.x:31943. Replace the italicized part in the metadata file with the
actual value.
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<md:EntityDescriptor xmlns:md="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:metadata" entityID="https://
10.212.196.x:31943/unisso/federation/issuer/2e0b66e021f9484abb8896e184829c1a"
ID="M39f9d9dc929d4067ad44c3081694cb3">
...
<md:SPSSODescriptor protocolSupportEnumeration="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:protocol"
WantAssertionsSigned="true" AuthnRequestsSigned="true">
...
<md:SingleLogoutService ResponseLocation="https://10.212.196.x:31943/unisso/federation/sp/logout"
Location="https://10.212.196.x:31943/unisso/federation/sp/logout"
Binding="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-Redirect"/>
<md:SingleLogoutService ResponseLocation="https://10.212.196.x:31943/unisso/federation/sp/logout"
Location="https://10.212.196.x:31943/unisso/federation/sp/logout"
Binding="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-POST"/>
<md:NameIDFormat>urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:transient</md:NameIDFormat>
<md:AssertionConsumerService Location="https://10.212.196.x:31943/unisso/saml/AssertionConsumer"
Binding="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-POST" isDefault="true" index="0"/>
</md:SPSSODescriptor>

corresponding configuration for the standby system on the peer system.
----End
8.2.9 License Management
License management allows you to initially load, revoke, update and export a
license file and query and export license information. These functions help
manage licenses in an effective way and ensure that you can use the system
properly.
8.2.9.1 Initially Loading a License
After the system is deployed, you need to load valid license files on the GUI to
ensure that you can use the system properly.
Prerequisites
● You have the operation rights for Update License.
● You have obtained the license files to be loaded.
Procedure
Step 1 Log in to the management system.
Step 2 Click Import License.
Step 3 Click next to License Files.
Step 4 Select the license file to be imported and click Open.
Step 5 Click Upload to view the current license information.
Step 6 Click Apply.
You can log in to management system only after the license is initially loaded
successfully.
NO TE
● During the initial loading, the default value of Update mode is Full, which cannot be
changed.
● After the license is imported, some NetEco services automatically start. To restart all
NetEco services, wait for about 3 minutes and then perform.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
● After the license is initially loaded, log in to the system again to make the
license take effect.
● After the license fails to be initially loaded, locate the causes based on the

On the License Overview page, you can view the license file status and
consumption of resource items or sales items. If a license file is about to expire or
the resource items or sales items do not meet service requirements, you can
update the license in a timely manner to ensure that you can properly use the
system.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > License Management.
Step 2 On the License Overview page, view the license file status and consumption of
resource items or sales items.
If a license file is about to expire or the resource items or sales items do not meet
service requirements, you can apply for and update the license in a timely manner.
NO TE
● The bar chart displays the license file status, and the ring chart displays the overall
status of resource items or sales items. You can click modules in different colors in the
bar chart and ring chart to view details in the list in the lower part.
● License 3.00 has sales items.
----End
8.2.9.3 Updating a License File
If a license file expires, or the resource control items or function control items in
the license do not meet service requirements, you need to update the license file
so that you can continue to use the system properly.
Figure 8-22 shows the process of updating a license file.

8.2.9.3.1 Backing Up License Files
Before updating a license file, export the license file. You can use the backup to
restore the license file if the update failed.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Update License.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > License Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose License Files.
Step 3 In the license file list, select the license file to be backed up and click Export
License.
NO TE
● LSN indicates the license serial number which uniquely identifies a license file.
● License 2.00 files are exported in .dat format and license 3.00 files are exported in .xml
format.
A package in .zip format is exported. In the package, the license files are named
License_product name_license file type.file format. The license file types are as

● Fixed-term: demo
● Permanent commercial+fixed-term: permanent
● Commissioning: demo
----End
8.2.9.3.2 Obtaining a License Revocation Code
If the ESN of a commercial license is changed due to NIC replacement, server
replacement, VM reinstallation, or incorrect ESN, you can manually revoke the
license and obtain the revocation code to apply for a new license file without
manual approval, which increases the application efficiency.
Prerequisites
You have permission to perform the operation Revoke License.
NO TE
A fixed-term or commissioning license cannot be revoked. For a fixed-term, commissioning,
or invalid license file, the revocation code cannot be used for license application. You need
to apply for a new license file.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > License Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose License Files.
Step 3 Revoke licenses.
● To revoke a single license: In the license list, click Revoke License in the
Operation column of the row that contains the license file to be revoked.
● To revoke licenses in batches: In the license list, select the license files to be
revoked and click Revoke License above the list.
NO TE
When a license is revoked, it enters the grace period. After the grace period expires,
the resources and functions specified in the license are unavailable. Apply for a new
license and update the license before the grace period expires.
Step 4 In the dialog box that is displayed, click OK.
Step 5 In the license list, click on the left of a license file to view and obtain
Revocation code.
----End
8.2.9.3.3 Updating a License File
You need to update the license file in the following scenarios so that you can use
the system properly: 1. The current license file in the system is about to expire or
has expired. 2. A resource control item or function control item in the license file
does not meet service requirements. 3. The software service annual fee in the

● You have the Revoke License and Update License permissions.
● You have obtained the license file.
Context
● You are advised to export the current license file as a backup before updating
the license file.
● If a product in the system has license files that contain unmatched ESN, you
cannot update such a license file.
● If a license file has expired but is still in the grace period, it cannot be
normally updated. You need to apply for a new license file.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > License Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose License Update.
Step 3 On the Upload page, click the icon on the right of the License file upload field.
Step 4 Select the desired license file and click Open.
Step 5 Click Upload.
Step 6 On the Pre-verify page, choose a license file update mode as required. You can
choose full update or incremental update:
● Full: Replaces all license files for the same product in the system.
● Incremental: Replaces the license files of the same type for the same product
in the system, for example, an updated permanent commercial license file will
replace the original permanent commercial license files.
License files are classified into four types: permanent commercial, fixed-term,
permanent commercial+fixed-term, and commissioning. The license update mode
of a product is determined by the types of its license file and the license file used
for the update. Table 8-55 lists the mapping between update modes and license
file types.

NO TE
● A license file can be incrementally updated using a license file of the same version. For
example, a license 2.00 file can be incrementally updated using a license 2.00 file, but
not a license 3.00 file.
● A license file can be fully updated using a different license file version.
Step 7 View the comparison result of the license file before and after the update.
Alternatively, click Export Comparison in the upper right corner of the list to
export the comparison result to your local PC and view the result.
Risk Level indicates the level of a risk caused by license file update. Table 8-56
provides the causes for each risk level.
● For resource control items, the risk levels from high to low are Risk, Warning,
Suggestion, and No risk.
● For function control items, the risk levels are as follows:
– Risk: Supported by Current License is set to Yes and Supported by New
License is set to No.
– Suggestion: Supported by Current License is set to No and Supported
by New License is set to Yes.
– No risk: Update Type is set to Unchanged.
● For sales information items, the risk levels from high to low are Risk,
Warning, Suggestion, and No risk.
NO TE
Sales information items are available only when you update license 3.00 files.

Step 8 Click Next to update the license.
NO TE
If the pre-verification result contains a Risk item, updating the license may cause some
resources or functions to become unavailable. You need to determine whether to update
the license based on site requirements.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
If the license file fails to be updated, locate the causes based on the error
information and update the license file again.
8.2.9.4 Maintaining a License
During routine license maintenance, you can learn the license status and resource
usage, and set major-alarm thresholds so that you can update the license in a
timely manner and use the system properly.
8.2.9.4.1 Viewing License Information
During routine maintenance, you can query current license information to detect
problems (for example, a license is about to expire or capacity is insufficient) and
update the license file in a timely manner, ensuring proper running of the system.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > License Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose License Information.
Step 3 On the License Information page, select a product from the product drop-down
list.
Step 4 Click the Resource Control Items, Function Control Items, or Sales Information
Items tab to view related license information of this product.
NO TE
License 3.00 has sales information items.

tab page.
● Table 8-59 describes the license information on the Function Control Items
tab page.
● Table 8-60 describes the license information on the Sales Information Items
tab page.

fixed-term item, for example, the consumption
and capacity of the fixed-term item. The third
records information about the permanent
commercial item, for example, the consumption
and capacity of the permanent commercial item.

item has one record, which records information
about the commissioning item, for example, the
consumption and capacity of the commissioning
item.

8.2.9.4.2 Setting Alarm Thresholds
When the consumption of a resource control item or sales information item for
the license reaches or exceeds the threshold, an alarm is generated. In this way,
users can apply for and update license files in a timely manner to ensure proper
running of the system.
Prerequisites
You have the operation rights for Update License.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > License Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose License Information.
Step 3 On the License Information page, click the Resource Control Items or Sales

the Operation column.
● To modify the alarm thresholds for multiple items, select the items and click
Batch Modify.
● To modify the alarm thresholds for all items, click Modify All.
NO TE
If the license file in the system is license 2.00, you can set alarm thresholds only for
resource control items.
Step 4 In the displayed dialog box, set the threshold for each resource control item or
sales information item, and specify whether to send an alarm or to enable the
scheduled reminder when the consumption of an item reaches the threshold.
● Modify the alarm threshold for a single item in the displayed Modify Alarm
Threshold dialog box.
● Modify the alarm thresholds for multiple items in the displayed Batch Modify
Alarm Thresholds dialog box.
● Modify the alarm thresholds for all items in the displayed Modify All Alarm
Thresholds dialog box.
NO TE
– If Send alarms is selected, the alarm "The license resource exceeds the threshold"
or "The license sales item exceeds the threshold" is sent when the consumption of
a resource control item or sales information item reaches Alarm threshold. This
alarm is automatically cleared when the consumption falls below Alarm
threshold.
– If Send notifications is selected, the Information dialog box is displayed at an
interval specified by Notification interval in Step 6 when the capacity
consumption of a resource control item or sales information item reaches Alarm
threshold.
Step 5 Click OK.
Step 6 Optional: Set the prompting interval.
1. On the License Information page, click below Notification interval.
2. Set Notification interval.
3. Click OK.
----End
8.2.9.4.3 Viewing a License File
During routine maintenance, you can view the license file to obtain the license
revocation code and ESN so that you can check the license file status.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > License Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose License Files.

Paramete Description
r
LSN License serial number which uniquely identifies a license file.
File ● Valid and available: The license file is in the validity period. In
Status this case, you can use the resources and functions specified in
the license file.
● Invalid but available: The license file is in the grace period. In
this case, you can use the resources and functions specified in
the license file until the grace period expires.
● Invalid and unavailable: The license file is invalid. In this case,
the resources and functions specified in the license file cannot be
used and the license file needs to be updated.
Step 3 Click on the left to view details of the license file.
----End
8.2.9.5 Allocating a License
NetEco supports license allocation and management.
Prerequisites
You have applied for and imported the licenses of the corresponding features.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > License Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose License Allocate. Information about all the current
licenses is displayed.
Step 3 Allocate licenses.
● Manual allocation

The application security function provides the system security baselines (for
protocols, algorithms, and keys). The system checks for risks of protocols, keys,
and algorithms used by services and service policies (for example, the policy of
forcible password change after expiration) based on the baselines. Based on the
check results, it provides risk warnings and rectification suggestions. This allows
the security capabilities of different services to be monitored in a unified manner
and ensures system security.
8.2.10.1 Overview of Application Security
This section describes basic information about the application security function,
including its definition, benefits, scenarios, functions, and principles.
8.2.10.1.1 Definition
The application security function supports risk check on each service configuration
item based on the baselines and provides the service configuration check and
baseline management functions. Each service configuration item corresponds to a
baseline.
8.2.10.1.2 Benefits
The application security function allows you to monitor protocols, keys, and
algorithms used by services and service configuration items in a unified manner,
improving management efficiency. Security administrators can rectify security risks
based on the check results to ensure service security.
8.2.10.1.3 Scenarios
The application security function can be used to check for risks of service
configuration items and manage baselines.
● Risk check on service configuration items
During routine maintenance, security administrators can use the application
security function to check whether the protocols, keys, and algorithms used
by services and service policies (for example, the policy of forcible password
change after expiration) meet the baselines, ensuring service security.
● Baseline management
After initial installation, a default value is provided for each service
configuration item. If the default value does not meet actual requirements,
you can modify it.
8.2.10.1.4 Functions
The application security function supports risk check on service configuration
items and baseline management.
● Risk check on service configuration items
Checks whether service configuration items have risks based on baselines, and

Allows you to view and modify the baseline of a service configuration item,
and restore the values in the baseline to default.
8.2.10.1.5 Availability
This section describes the prerequisites for deploying and operating security
configuration. Based on the prerequisites, you can evaluate whether security
configuration can be used and make necessary preparations, such as checking
whether a license is required and obtaining hardware requirements.
License Requirements
Security configuration is not license-controlled.
Compatible Product Versions
Security configuration does not require the interaction with peripheral products.
8.2.10.1.6 Principles
Each service needs to register service configuration items with the application
security function. Security administrators determine whether to modify the
baselines as required. The application security function checks for risks of service
configuration items based on the baselines and provides risk warnings and
rectification suggestions, helping security administrators detect service security
risks and handle the problems in a timely manner.
Figure 8-23 shows the principles of the application security function.
Figure 8-23 Principles of the application security function
8.2.10.2 Viewing Security Baselines
A security baseline provides secure protocols and algorithms for services. The
system checks whether protocols and algorithms used by services are secure based
on the security baseline.

Step 1 Choose System > System Management > Application Security.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Configuration Management.
Step 3 On the Baseline Management page, click the card of required baseline.
Step 4 View the configured security baseline.
The security configuration function will check for risks of protocols, keys, and
algorithms used by services based on the security baseline, and provide risk
warnings and rectification suggestions.
----End
8.2.10.3 Checking Security Configurations
Security administrators can configure check policies based on service security
requirements to periodically check whether protocols, keys, and algorithms used
by services meet the security baselines, and rectify the risks based on the check
results. To meet special requirements such as compatibility requirements, you can
configure insecure protocols, keys, and algorithms on the panel for risk details.
Exercise caution when performing this operation. For service function security, you
are advised to use secure protocols, keys, and algorithms.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > Application Security.
Step 2 On the Configuration Check page, check a single item or check multiple items in
batches as required.
● Checking a single item
Click Check in the Operation column of the row that contains the required
item.
● Checking multiple items in batches
Click Check Now to check all items.
NO TE
Click Set Check Time and set the daily check time. The security configuration function
automatically checks service configuration items every day based on the set time.
Step 3 View check results.
● After the check is complete, view the results in the Check Result column.
● In the upper part of the Configuration Check page, view the result
distribution charts of all check items so that you can learn about the overall
risk information.
Step 4 Click the name of a required configuration item. On the panel that is displayed,

Configuration Item based on site requirements and click Save.
NO TE
If the modified value contains insecure protocols, keys, or algorithms, security risks may
occur. Exercise caution when performing this operation. For service function security, you
are advised to use secure protocols, keys, and algorithms.
----End
Related Tasks
● Adding an item to the whitelist: If a service configuration item does not have
security risks or is not used, click Add to Whitelist in the Operation column
of the row that contains the item to add it to the whitelist. The security
configuration function will exclude items in the whitelist when performing the
security check to improve the check efficiency.
● Removing an item from the whitelist: If the security check needs to include a
whitelist item, click Remove from Whitelist in the Operation column of the
row that contains the item.
8.3 Northbound Integration
Setting related parameters, the OSS is interconnected to NetEco to complete the
interconnection with NetEco.
8.3.1 NBI Definition
The northbound interface (NBI) links the element management system (EMS) and
the network management system (NMS).
The NMS obtains information about the network elements (NEs) managed by the
EMS and delivers operation commands through the northbound interface. The NE
information includes the alarm, configuration, performance, and inventory data.
Figure 8-24 shows the position of the northbound interface in the network.
Figure 8-24 Position of the northbound interface

performance, and inventory data of NEs to the NMS through the northbound
interface.
8.3.2 NBI Description of the NetEco Server
Three types of NBIs are supported for the NetEco: WebService, SNMP, and FTP.
Table 8-63 lists the differences between the WebService, SNMP, and FTP NBIs.

8.3.3 Setting the SNMP NBI
Setting related parameters, the OSS is interconnected to NetEco to complete the
interconnection with NetEco.
8.3.3.1 Configuring the SNMP NBI
Set basic parameters for the SNMP NBI so that the NBI will report collected alarm
information to the third-party system.
Prerequisites
You have the Northbound Interface permission.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > Northbound Integration > SNMP NBI.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose SNMP NBI > Basic Settings.
Step 3 On the Basic Settings page, configure IP addresses and ports.
Step 4 Expand SNMP Agent Settings, and configure the parameters. You are advised to
retain the default values.
Step 5 Expand Inform/Trap Settings and modify parameters based on negotiation with
the third-party system.
Step 6 Expand SNMPv3 Parameter Settings, and configure the parameters.

NO TE
The following algorithms and protocols are insecure. You are advised to use secure
algorithms and protocols, that is, the default values. To use the following algorithms or
protocols, enable insecure parameter options first. For details, see 8.3.3.4 Enabling and
Disabling Insecure Configurations.
● Security level: Not authenticated nor encrypted and Authenticated but not
encrypted
Authenticated and encrypted is recommended because it is more secure than Not
authenticated nor encrypted and Authenticated but not encrypted.
● Authentication protocol: HMAC-MD5, HMAC-SHA1, and HMAC-SHA2-224
HMAC-SHA2-512 is recommended, because it is more secure than HMAC-MD5, HMAC-
SHA1, and HMAC-SHA2-224.
● Encryption protocol: DES
AES-256 is recommended, because it is more secure than DES.
Step 7 Expand Heartbeat Period Settings, and configure the parameters.
Step 8 Expand SNMP NBI Advanced Parameter Settings, and set advanced parameters.
Step 9 Expand MIB Alarm Reporting Settings and select the desired alarm fields.
Step 10 Click Save.
----End
8.3.3.2 Configuring the Third-Party System Information
Create or modify a third-party system to receive alarms from the SNMP NBI in
real time.
Prerequisites
● You have the Query Northbound SNMP Interface Configuration Items and
Modify Northbound SNMP Interface Configuration Items permissions.
● You have obtained the IP address or domain name, port number, and protocol
type of the third-party system. For SNMPv1 and SNMPv2c, you also need to
obtain the read and write community names. For SNMPv3, you also need to
obtain the username, authentication password, and encryption password.
Procedure
1. Choose System > Northbound Integration > SNMP NBI from the main
menu.
2. In the navigation pane, choose SNMP NBI > Third-party System Settings.
3. On the Third-party System Settings page, click Create to add a third-party
system.
4. Configure the basic third-party system information.

NO TE
● SNMPv3 is recommended, because it is more secure than SNMPv1 and SNMPv2c.
To set Protocol version to SNMPv1 or SNMPv2c, enable insecure configurations
first. For details, see 8.3.3.4 Enabling and Disabling Insecure Configurations.
After the insecure configurations are enabled, select SNMPv1 or SNMPv2c and
save the configurations for them to take effect.
● If SNMPv3 is selected, the requirements for the authentication password and data
encryption password are as follows by default: To support low-complexity
passwords, set Password complexity verification on the Security Settings page
to Disable first.
● Contain 8 to 64 characters.
● Contain at least three of the following: lowercase letters, uppercase letters,
digits, special characters !"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^`{_|}~
● Not consist of only duplicate character strings.
● Not be the same as or the reverse of the username.
● Not use the same authentication password and data encryption password.
● If different third-party systems need to use the same username (for example,
in the active/standby scenario), the same password of the username must be
configured for the systems. Otherwise, the authentication fails.
● If SNMPv1 or SNMPv2c is selected, the requirements for the read community and
write community are as follows by default: To support low-complexity passwords,
set Password complexity verification on the Security Settings page to Disable
first.
● Contain 8 to 64 characters.
● Contain at least three of the following: lowercase letters, uppercase letters,
digits, special characters !"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^`{_|}~
● Not consist of only duplicate character strings.
5. Click Expand Filter Settings to configure filter criteria. Click Create.
– Engineering alarm: Before performing maintenance operations on an NE
(such as creation, expansion, or upgrade), you can add the NE to a
maintenance task. The maintenance operations may report a large
amount of data that you do not need to concern about, such as
engineering alarms. To prevent such alarms from interfering with normal
network monitoring, you can mask the alarms of this type.
– Alarm mode: RAT of the NE for which the alarm or event is generated.
You can filter alarms or events by RAT.
6. In the dialog box that is displayed, click OK.
7. Click Send Test Alarm in the Operation column of a specified third-party
system to test whether NetEco can successfully send alarms to the third-party
system.
– If yes, the system displays a message indicating that the alarm is
successfully sent and the third-party system can receive the test alarm
whose ID is 80011.
– If no, the system displays a message indicating that the alarm is
successfully sent but the third-party system cannot receive the test alarm
whose ID is 80011. In this case, check whether the third-party system
configuration is correct.

If the configuration is incorrect, reconfigure the third-party system.
After the configuration, resend the test alarm. If the third-party
system still cannot receive the test alarm whose ID is 80011, contact
technical support.
– If the system displays a message indicating that the alarm fails to be
sent, contact technical support.
Related Tasks
● Modifying a third-party system
To modify a configured third-party system, in the third-party system list, click
the Edit icon in the Operation column of the row that contains the third-
party system.
● Deleting a third-party system
To delete the interconnection information about a configured third-party
system, in the third-party system list, click the Delete icon in the Operation
column of the row that contains the third-party system.
8.3.3.3 Setting an SNMP NBI Security Policy
Security settings allow you to configure the lockout policy for the login IP address
after the SNMP NBI authentication fails.By default, if incorrect passwords are
entered for consecutive three times within 60 minutes, the login IP address will be
locked for 5 minutes.
Prerequisites
You have the Northbound SNMP Interface Configuration Management
operation right.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > Northbound Integration > SNMP NBI.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Security Settings.
Step 3 On the Security Settings page, retain the default settings.
NO TE
By default, the password complexity verification is enabled. If the verification is disabled,
weak passwords are allowed for third-party system configuration, which poses security
risks. You are advised to enable the verification.
Table 8-65 lists the SNMP NBI Security Policy parameters.
Table 8-65 Configuring SNMP NBI Security Policy
Parameter Description Default
Value
Maximum number Maximum number of consecutive login 3

Parameter Description Default
Value
Login period If the maximum number of consecutive 60
login failures is reached within this
period, the client IP address is locked.
The unit is minute.
Lockout duration Lockout period after consecutive login 5
failures. The unit is minute.
Step 4 Click Save.
----End
8.3.3.4 Enabling and Disabling Insecure Configurations
When the SNMP NBI interconnects with a third-party system, the SNMP protocol,
security level, authentication protocol, and encryption protocol are required. For
system security purposes, NetEco supports secure configurations by default. To
meet the requirements that some peer systems use insecure configurations,
NetEco allows you to enable and disable insecure configurations. After the
insecure configurations are enabled, you can select the insecure algorithms and
protocols on the page and save the configurations for them to take effect. For
security purposes, you are advised to use the secure configurations provided by
default.
Context
Insecure configurations in SNMP NBI are as follows:
● Protocol version: SNMPv1 and SNMPv2c
● Security level: Not authenticated nor encrypted and Authenticated but
not encrypted
● Authentication protocol: HMAC-MD5, HMAC-SHA1, and HMAC-SHA2-224
● Encryption protocol: DES
Procedure
● GUI mode
a. Choose System > System Management > Application Security from the
main menu.
b. On the Configuration Check page, click Northbound interface-SNMP
NBI insecure configuration switch.
c. On the panel that is displayed, click Modify, select Enable or Disable,
and click Save.
d. In the High Risk dialog box, select I understand the risks and want to
continue and click OK.
NO TE

● CLI mode
a. Use PuTTY to log in to the node where the active snmpagentservice
process instance of NBIService resides, as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
For details about how to obtain the status of the active snmpagentservice
process instance of NBIService and the IP address of the corresponding
node, see "Checking the Working Status and Management IP Address of
a Node" in Administrator Guide.
NO TE
If the NetEco is deployed in single-server mode, log in to the node where
NBIService resides. For details about how to obtain the IP address of the node
where NBIService resides, see "Querying the Management IP Address of the Node
Where a Process or Service Resides" in Administrator Guide.
b. Run the following command to switch to the ossuser user:
su - ossuser
Password: password for the ossuser user
c. Run the following command to go to the target directory:
cd /opt/oss/NetEco/apps/SnmpAgentService/tools
d. Run the following commands to enable or disable all insecure
configurations:
▪
Enabling all insecure configurations
sh updateConfiguration.sh -on
The following information is displayed. Read the information
carefully and confirm whether to continue.
The protocols/algorithms/security levels are insecure. Are you sure you want to continue?
[y/n]
Enter y and press Enter.
If information similar to the following is displayed, the insecure
configurations are enabled successfully. Otherwise, contact technical
support.
All insecure algorithms and protocols are enabled.
▪
Disabling all insecure configurations
sh updateConfiguration.sh -off
If information similar to the following is displayed, the insecure
configurations are disabled successfully. Otherwise, contact technical
support.
All insecure algorithms and protocols are disabled.

result.

8.5 About
This section describes how to manage certificates.
8.5.1 Certificate Management
Certificate Management involves service certificate management, CA
interconnection configuration, and certificate application. Service certificates can
be managed (including import, deletion, and periodic validity check) in a
centralized manner, and you can quickly apply for and use the certificates.
8.5.1.1 Managing Service Certificates
This section describes how to import certificates used by peer systems. Certificate
management supports the import of three types of certificates: identity
certificates, trust certificates, and revocation lists (CRLs). During routine
maintenance, periodically update the certificates to prevent security risks caused
by private key leakage. To prevent system function unavailability due to certificate
expiration, periodically check the certificate validity in certificate management or
check for alarms for expiring and expired certificates. If a certificate is about to
expire, update the certificate in time. If a certificate has expired or the private key
of a certificate is leaked, update the certificate immediately. If the trusted CA has
updated the CRL, update the CRL in a timely manner.
Prerequisites
● The service that requires certificate management has been deployed.
● You have the Query Certificate, Import Certificate, and Delete Certificate
permissions.
● You have obtained the certificate to be imported or updated and its
information, including passwords, certificate type, public key, private key,
private key password, and certificate chain of the identity certificate.

● If the page for configuring certificates of a service does not contain the
Identity Certificates, Trust Certificates, or Certificate Revocation Lists tab
page, the service does not have the corresponding type of certificate and the
corresponding tab page does not need to be displayed.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to "Checking Validity of
the NetEco Certificates".
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Service Certificate Management.
Step 3 On the Services page, click the service with certificates to be imported.
Step 4 Configure certificate information based on the certificate type.
1. On the Identity Certificates tab page, click Import.
2. On the Import Identity Certificate page, set related parameters, and click
Submit.
NO TE
Identity certificates include the Certificate library type and Independent public and
private keys type. The certificate type may vary depending on the service
requirements.
– Certificate library: The public key, private key, and certificate chain are in the
same keystore file. When importing the certificate, you only need to import one
file.
– Independent public and private keys: The public key and private key are separate
files.

NO TE
– The total number of identity certificates, trust certificates, and CRLs imported for a
service cannot exceed 200.
– When duplicate trust certificates and CRLs are imported, certificate management
does not send certificate change notifications, and services are not interrupted or
restarted.
Step 5 In the High Risk dialog box, read the information carefully and confirm whether

Step 6.
● If not, click Cancel.
NO TE
When an identity certificate is imported, a message is displayed, showing the
comparison between the imported certificate and the original certificate.
Step 6 In the dialog box that is displayed, click OK.
----End
Related Tasks
● Quickly filtering services by certificate validity period and certificate status
a. In the navigation pane, choose Service Certificate Management.
b. On the Services page, set Certificate validity period and Certificate
status.
● Restoring a historical certificate
If an identity certificate fails to be updated, is updated to an incorrect
certificate, or is deleted by mistake, you can perform the following operations
to restore the certificate:
a. In the navigation pane, choose Service Certificate Management.
b. On the Services page, click the service with a certificate to be restored.
c. Click the Identity Certificates tab.
d. Click Historical Certificates.
e. On the Historical Certificates page, click in the Operation column of
the row that contains the desired certificate.
● Deleting a historical certificate
a. In the navigation pane, choose Service Certificate Management.
b. On the Services page, click the service with a certificate to be deleted.
c. Click the Identity Certificates tab.
d. Click Historical Certificates.
e. On the Historical Certificates page, select the identity certificates to be
deleted and click Delete.
NO TE
A historical certificate cannot be restored after it is deleted. Exercise caution when
performing this operation.
● Exporting the trust chain
On the Identity Certificates tab page, click Export Trust Chain to export the
corresponding certificate trust chain.
NO TE
If the trust chain or related certificates are not imported, the exported trust chain may

On the Identity Certificates tab page, click in the Operation column of
the row that contains the desired certificate to export the public key and
certificate chain of the identity certificate. For details, see 8.5.1.5 Exporting
Public Keys and Certificate Chains of Identity Certificates.
● Deleting certificates
Select the certificates to be deleted and click Delete.
NO TE
Deleting certificates may cause the services that use the certificates to become
interrupted. Exercise caution when deleting certificates.
● Viewing certificate status
For an imported certificate, view the certificate status in the Certificate
Status column.
NO TE
If the Certificate Status column is not displayed for a service, the function does not
apply to the service, which is normal.
8.5.1.2 Managing All Certificates
You can use this function to quickly view certificate status and certificate validity
period of all certificates, and check whether a certificate is an initial certificate.
You can quickly update the certificates that have been updated online. During
routine maintenance, periodically update the certificates to prevent security risks
caused by private key leakage. To prevent system function unavailability due to
certificate expiration, periodically check the certificate validity in certificate
management or check for alarms for expiring and expired certificates. If the
private key is leaked or the certificate has expired, update the certificate
immediately. If a certificate is about to expire, update the certificate in time. If the
trusted CA has updated the CRL, update the CRL in a timely manner. If an initial
certificate is used, apply for a new one and replace the initial one in a timely
manner.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the certificate and its information, including passwords,
certificate type, public key, private key, private key password, and certificate
chain of the identity certificate, if you need to import and update a certificate
on this page.
● You have configured the interconnection with the CA server and obtained the
information about the certificate, such as the certificate format, common
name (CN), country or region (C), province or state (ST), city (L), organization
(O), department (OU), validity period, key algorithm, and key length, subject
alternative name, certificate type, key usage, and extended key usage, if you
need to update a certificate online on this page.
Context
Initial certificates are used only for network access and initialization during

Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose All Certificates.
Step 3 Perform operations according to Table 8-73.

8.5.1.3 Restoring Historical Certificates
If an identity certificate in use is overwritten by another identity certificate or is
deleted by mistake, you can use the historical certificate function to restore the
certificate to ensure that services are running properly.
Prerequisites
You have the Query Certificate and Import Certificate permissions.
Context
When you restore a historical certificate (identity certificate), the corresponding
services will load the certificate. During the loading, the corresponding functions
may be unavailable for a short period of time. Therefore, you are advised to
perform this operation in off-peak hours.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Service Certificate Management.
Step 3 On the Services page, click the service with a certificate to be restored.
Step 4 Click the Identity Certificates tab.
Step 5 Click Historical Certificates.
Step 6 On the Historical Certificates page, click in the Operation column of the row
that contains the desired certificate.
Step 7 In the High Risk dialog box, read the information carefully and confirm whether
to restore the certificate.
● If yes, select I understand the risk and want to continue, click OK, and go
to Step 8.
● If no, click Cancel.
Step 8 In the Information dialog box, read the information carefully and click OK.
----End
Related Tasks

In the historical certificate list, click in the row that contains the desired
certificate to view the certificate details.
● Deleting historical certificates
In the historical certificate list, select the identity certificates to be deleted
and click Delete.
NO TE
A historical certificate cannot be restored after it is deleted. Exercise caution when
performing this operation.
8.5.1.4 Importing a Shared CRL
This section describes how to import a shared CRL and apply the CRL to services.
Services can obtain the CRL by calling APIs to verify the validity of their own
certificates.
Prerequisites
● You have the Query Shared CRLs, Import Shared CRL, and Delete Shared
CRLs permissions.
● You have obtained the CRL to be imported.
Context
● Shared CRLs are used when:
– CRLs are imported to the list of CRLs and applied to services.
– After the CA proxy is interconnected with the CRL server, a CRL file is
automatically generated on the Shared CRLs page only when the CA
server interconnected with the CRL server is selected for the certificate
update task configured in certificate management. The generated CRL file
takes effect only after it is applied to services.
● When you share a CRL, the corresponding services will load the CRL. During
the loading, the corresponding functions may be unavailable for a short
period of time. Therefore, you are advised to perform this operation in off-
peak hours.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Shared CRLs.
Step 3 On the Shared CRLs page, click Import.
Step 4 Configure the certificate information as required, and click Submit.
Step 5 In the list on the Shared CRLs page, click in the Operation column of the row
that contains the desired CRL.
Step 6 In the Apply to Services dialog box, select the desired services based on site
requirements and click Apply.

● Viewing CRL details
In the list on the Shared CRLs page, click in the row that contains the
desired CRL to view its details.
● Deleting CRLs
NO TE
After a CRL is deleted, the files revoked by this CRL will become valid again. Exercise
caution when performing this operation.
To delete existing shared CRLs, perform either of the following operations:
Method 1: Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the
desired CRL.
Method 2: Select the desired CRLs, and click Delete in the upper right corner
of the list on the Shared CRLs page.
8.5.1.5 Exporting Public Keys and Certificate Chains of Identity Certificates
Certificate management allows you to export NetEco identity certificates (public
keys and certificate chains) to facilitate system interconnection and O&M. When a
third-party system interconnects with NetEco, if the third-party system needs to
authenticate NetEco, you need to obtain the identity certificate (public key and
certificate chain) of NetEco as the trust certificate and import it to the third-party
system. In O&M scenarios, you may also need to obtain the identity certificate
(public key and certificate chain) for fault locating.
Prerequisites
The Identity Certificates tab page is displayed in the certificate list page of the
corresponding service.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Service Certificate Management.
Step 3 On the Services page, select the desired service.
Step 4 On the Identity Certificates tab page, click in the Operation column of the
row that contains the desired certificate to export the public key and certificate
chain of the identity certificate.
NO TE
● If the public key and certificate chain of the identity certificate are independent, you
need to export them separately.
● If you do not upload a certificate chain when importing a certificate, the certificate
chain cannot be exported.

You can update a certificate online or apply for and update a certificate using a
CSR. Before updating a certificate online, you need to configure the CRL server
parameters and parameters for interconnecting with the CA. After the
interconnection is successful, you can quickly apply for a certificate using a
template and apply the obtained certificate to services. You can also apply for and
update a certificate using a CSR as follows: Create a CSR, export the CSR file,
apply for a public key and certificate chain, bind the public key and certificate
chain to the private key in the NetEco database to obtain a complete identity
certificate, and quickly apply the certificate to services.
8.5.1.6.1 Creating Certificate Application Templates
The certificate template management function improves the efficiency of and
standardizes certificate update. You can create different certificate application
templates with common certificate information for different service scenarios.
When updating a certificate, you can select an existing template to simplify
certificate update operations and prevent repeated configuration of basic
certificate information for certificate update.
Prerequisites
● You have the Query Certificate Application Template, Manage Certificate
Application Template, and Delete Certificate Application Template
permissions.
● You have obtained the certificate format, validity period, key algorithm, key
length, certificate type, key usage, and extended key usage from the
interconnected CA, ensuring that the planned certificate information is
correct.
● You have planned the information about the certificate, such as the certificate
format, common name (CN), country or region (C), province or state (ST),
city (L), organization (O), department (OU), email (E), validity period, key
algorithm, key length, subject alternative name, certificate type, key usage,
and extended key usage.
Context
● The validity period of a certificate applied from the CA is jointly determined
by the custom validity period, validity period of the CA root certificate,
maximum validity period supported by the CA, and validity period of the
application template supported by the CA. Generally, the validity period does
not exceed the maximum validity period supported by the CA and the validity
period of the application template supported by the CA. The actual validity
period depends on the interconnected CA.
● Table 8-76 describes the certificate formats, key algorithms, key lengths,
certificate types, key usages, and extended key usages supported by certificate
management.

Parameter Description
Certificate Format of the certificate.
format
● PEM
● PKCS12
Key Key algorithm of the certificate.
algorithm
● RSA
● ECDSA
Key length ● Length of the RSA certificate key:
– 8192
– 6144
– 4096
– 3072
– 2048
NOTE
RSA whose length is shorter than 2,048 bits is insecure. You are
advised to use the certificate encrypted by RSA whose length is
3,072 bits or longer.
● Length of the ECDSA certificate key:
– 521
– 384
– 256
Certificate Type of the certificate.
type
● End Entity: Select this type if you do not need to use the
key corresponding to the certificate to issue certificates.
● CA: Select this type when you need to use the
corresponding key to issue certificates.

Parameter Description
Key usage Usage of the certificate key.
● Digital signature: Select this usage if the certificate
requires identity authentication and data integrity
authentication.
● Non-repudiation: Select this usage if the certificate
requires authentication on the digital signature of the
public key to prevent the signing entity from denying its
requests.
● Key encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
requires an encryption protocol.
● Data encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
encrypts its application data.
● Key agreement: Select this usage if the public key is used
by the sender and receiver to encrypt data during
communication through plaintext agreement.
● Certificate signing: Select this usage if the public key
authenticates the certificate signature. It can only be used
as a proxy certificate.
● CRL signing: Select this usage if the public key of the
certificate is used to verify the signature on the revocation
information.
● Encipher only: Select this usage if Key agreement is
selected. The public key is used only for encrypting data
during the key agreement.
● Decipher only: Select this usage if Key agreement is
selected. The public key is used only for deciphering data
during the key agreement.
Extended Extended usage of the certificate key.
key usage
● Server authentication: TLS WWW server authentication.
Select this usage when Digital signature, Key
encipherment, or Key agreement in Key usage is
selected.
● Client authentication: TLS WWW client authentication.
Select this usage when Digital signature or Key
agreement in Key usage is selected.
● Email protection: Email protection. Select this usage when
the Digital signature, Non-repudiation, Key
encipherment, or Key agreement in Key usage is
selected.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.

Templates.
Step 3 Click Create.
Step 4 Configure the template parameters as required.
Step 5 Click OK.
Step 6 In the dialog box that is displayed, click OK.
----End
Related Tasks
● Modify the certificate template.
Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the desired
certificate template to modify the template information.
● Delete the certificate template.
Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the desired
certificate template to delete the template.
8.5.1.6.2 Updating Certificates Online
You can quickly apply for a certificate using an existing certificate template, or
apply for certificate by manually configuring certificate information. You can then
apply the obtained certificate to each service. For a service that has an identity
certificate, you can quickly update its identity certificate using wizard-based
update. During routine maintenance, periodically update the certificates to
prevent security risks caused by private key leakage. To prevent system function
unavailability due to certificate expiration, periodically check the certificate validity
in certificate management or check for alarms for expiring and expired certificates.
If the private key is leaked or a certificate has expired, update the certificate
immediately. If a certificate is about to expire, update the certificate in time.
Prerequisites
● The interconnection with the CA server has been configured.
● You have the Query Certificate Application Task and Manage Certificate
Application Task permissions.
● You have obtained the information about the certificate, such as the
certificate format, common name (CN), country or region (C), province or
state (ST), city (L), organization (O), department (OU), email (E), validity
period, key algorithm, key length, subject alternative name (SAN), certificate
type, key usage, and extended key usage.
Context
● The time difference between NetEco and the CA server must be less than 5
minutes. Otherwise, the certificate application fails.
● The validity period of a certificate applied from the CA is jointly determined
by the custom validity period, validity period of the CA root certificate,
maximum validity period supported by the CA, and validity period of the

period of the application template supported by the CA. The actual validity
period depends on the interconnected CA.
● Table 8-77 describes the certificate formats, key algorithms, key lengths,
certificate types, key usages, and extended key usages supported by certificate
management.
● When you update a certificate (identity certificate or trust certificate), the
corresponding services will load the certificate. During the loading, the
corresponding functions may be unavailable for a short period of time.
Therefore, you are advised to perform this operation in off-peak hours.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to "Checking Validity of
the NetEco Certificates".
Table 8-77 Certificate information

● Online Application: You can quickly apply for a certificate using an existing certificate

requirements.

a. (Optional) Create a certificate application template. For
details, see 8.5.1.6.1 Creating Certificate Application
Templates.
b. Select a template from the Template drop-down list.
c. Select a CA interconnection configuration from the CA
Server drop-down list.
d. Set parameters as prompted based on site requirements.
NOTE
After a certificate application template is selected, you can change
the values of some parameters as required. For example, if the
validity period set in the certificate application template is 5 years,
but the required validity period of an online certificate application
task is 10 years, you can set the validity period to 10 years for the
task.
– Updating without a template
Set the parameters of the online certificate application task
as prompted based on site requirements.
3. Click OK.
4. In the list of online certificate update tasks, check Task Status
of the created task.
– Pending: The online certificate application task is applying
for a new certificate. Refresh the page and check the task
status.
– Successful: The online certificate application task is created
successfully.
– Failed: The online certificate application task fails to be
created. Rectify the fault based on the failure causes. Click
in the Operation column. In the dialog box that is displayed,
click Details and rectify the fault based on the details. If the
problem persists, contact technical support.
5. To apply the obtained certificate to services, perform Step 3.5.a
to Step 3.5.c.
a. Click in the Operation column of the row that contains
the certificate.
b. Select the services to which you want to apply the certificate.
c. Click OK.
d. In the High Risk dialog box, read the information carefully
and confirm whether to apply certificate to services.

Mode Procedure
▪
If so, select I understand the risk and want to continue,
click OK, and go to Step 3.5.e.
▪
If not, click Cancel.
e. Check whether the certificate is successfully applied to the
corresponding services.
If the message "Certificates applied to services successfully."
is displayed, the certificate is successfully applied to the
service. Click OK. Otherwise, handle the problem according to
the information in the dialog box.
NOTE
When a certificate is applied to services, the identity certificate and
certificate chain of the certificate are applied to the services.

update

In the service list, the current identity certificates of the services whose
Source is empty are not obtained through online application.

default value is 50 years.
5. (Optional) Select services as required and click Configure CA
Server. In the dialog box that is displayed, configure the CA
server interconnected with the certificates of the selected
services.
6. (Optional) Click Modify in the Operation column of the row
that contains the corresponding service. On the page that is
displayed, set certificate parameters as required and click OK.

confirm whether to update the certificates of the services.
– If so, select I understand the risk and want to continue,
and click OK.
– If not, click Cancel.
9. In the list of online certificate update tasks, check Task Status
of the created task.
– Pending: The wizard-based certificate update task is applying
for a new certificate. Refresh the page and check the task
status.
– Successful: The certificates of all services are successfully
updated.
– Partially successful: The certificates of some services are
successfully updated.
– Failed: The certificates of all services fail to be updated.
NOTE
If the task status is Partially successful or Failed, click in the
Operation column of row that contains the corresponding task. In the
dialog box that is displayed, click Details and rectify the fault based on
the information. If the fault persists, contact technical support.

NO TE
If the certificate applied for in a task has been applied to a service, the task cannot be
deleted.
● Restoring a historical certificate
If a certificate fails to be updated, is updated to an incorrect certificate, or is
deleted by mistake, you can perform the following operations to restore the
certificate:
a. In the navigation pane, choose Service Certificate Management.
b. On the Services page, click the service with a certificate to be restored.
c. Click the Identity Certificates tab.
d. Click Historical Certificates.
e. On the Historical Certificates page, click in the Operation column of
the row that contains the desired certificate.
8.5.1.6.3 Updating Certificates Using CSR Management
You can use the CSR management function to create a CSR, export the CSR file,
and submit the CSR to the CA to apply for a public key and certificate chain. After
applying for the public key and certificate chain, you can use the CSR
management function to bind the public key and certificate chain to the CSR and
private key in the database to form a complete identity certificate. After the
binding, the certificate can be quickly applied to services. During routine
maintenance, periodically update the certificates to prevent security risks caused
by certificate expiration and private key leakage. To prevent system function
unavailability due to certificate expiration, periodically check the certificate validity
in certificate management or check for alarms for expiring and expired certificates.
If the private key is leaked or a certificate has expired, update the certificate
immediately. If a certificate is about to expire, update the certificate in time.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the information about the CSR to be created, such as the
common name (CN), country or region (C), province or state (ST), city (L),
organization (O), department (OU), email (E), key algorithm, key length,
subject alternative name, certificate type, key usage, and extended key usage.
● Before updating a certificate, you have applied for and obtained the public
key and certificate chain from the CA.
Context
● Table 8-79 describes the key algorithms, key lengths, certificate types, key
usages, and extended key usages supported by certificate management.
● When you update a certificate (identity certificate), the corresponding services
will load the certificate. During the loading, the corresponding functions may
be unavailable for a short period of time. Therefore, you are advised to
perform this operation in off-peak hours.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to "Checking Validity of

Parameter Description
Key Key algorithm of the certificate.
algorithm
● RSA
● ECDSA
Key length ● Length of the RSA certificate key:
– 8192
– 6144
– 4096
– 3072
– 2048
NOTE
RSA whose length is shorter than 2,048 bits is insecure. You are
advised to use the certificate encrypted by RSA whose length is
3,072 bits or longer.
● Length of the ECDSA certificate key:
– 521
– 384
– 256
Certificate Type of the certificate.
type
● End Entity: Select this type if you do not need to use the

● CA: Select this type when you need to use the
corresponding key to issue certificates.

Parameter Description
Key usage Usage of the certificate key.
● Digital signature: Select this usage if the certificate
requires identity authentication and data integrity
authentication.
● Non-repudiation: Select this usage if the certificate
requires authentication on the digital signature of the
public key to prevent the signing entity from denying its
requests.
● Key encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
requires an encryption protocol.
● Data encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
encrypts its application data.
● Key agreement: Select this usage if the public key is used
by the sender and receiver to encrypt data during
communication through plaintext agreement.
● Certificate signing: Select this usage if the public key
authenticates the certificate signature. It can only be used
as a proxy certificate.
● CRL signing: Select this usage if the public key of the

during the key agreement.
● Decipher only: Select this usage if Key agreement is

during the key agreement.

selected.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.

1. Click Create CSR.
2. Select a CSR creation mode based on site requirements.
– Creating a CSR by selecting an existing template
i. (Optional) Create a certificate application template. For details, see
8.5.1.6.1 Creating Certificate Application Templates.
ii. Select a template from the Template drop-down list.
iii. Set parameters as prompted based on site requirements.
NO TE
After using an existing certificate application template, you can also modify
some parameters as required.
– Creating a CSR directly
Set parameters as prompted based on site requirements.
3. Click OK.
Step 4 Export the CSR file and apply for a public key and certificate chain from the CA.
1. Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the CSR to export
the CSR as a file. The CSR file is in .csr format.
NO TE
In the CSR management list, you can view the status of the created CSRs.
– Not exported: CSRs in the Not exported state can be modified, exported, and
deleted.
– To be bound: CSRs in the To be bound state can be modified, exported, bound,
and deleted.
– Bound: CSRs in the Bound state can be deleted and the corresponding certificate
can be applied to services.
2. Submit the CSR file to the CA to apply for a public key and certificate chain.
Step 5 Bind the obtained public key and certificate chain to the CSR and private key in
the database.
1. Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the desired CSR.
2. Enter certificate information and import the public key and certificate chain.
3. Click Submit.
4. In the dialog box, read the information carefully and confirm whether to bind
the certificate.
– If so, click OK. Go to Step 6.
– If not, click Cancel.
Step 6 Apply the certificate bound with the CSR to services.
1. Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the desired CSR.
2. Select the services to which you want to apply the certificate.
3. Click OK.
4. In the High Risk dialog box, read the information carefully and confirm

go to Step 6.5.
– If not, click Cancel.
5. Check whether the certificate is successfully applied to the corresponding
services.
If the message "Certificates applied to services successfully." is displayed, the
certificate is successfully applied to the service. Click OK. Otherwise, handle
the problem according to the information in the dialog box.
NO TE
When a certificate is applied to services, the identity certificate and certificate chain of
the certificate are applied to the services.
----End
Related Tasks
● Modifying a CSR
To modify the information about an existing CSR, click in the Operation
column of the CSR on the CSR Management page.
NO TE
A CSR that has been bound to a certificate cannot be modified.
● Deleting a CSR
On the CSR Management page, click in the Operation column of the
desired CSR to delete it.
NO TE
CSRs corresponding to certificates that have been applied to services cannot be
deleted. To delete such a CSR, delete the certificate of the corresponding services first.
8.5.1.7 Setting General Parameters of Certificate Management
This section describes general settings of certificate management, including
certificate alarm and CRL check configurations. You can enable or disable the
reporting of the alarms "Certificate Is About to Expire" and "Certificate Has
Expired". After alarm reporting is enabled, you can configure the certificate check
interval and alarm policy for expiring certificates. You can also enable or disable
the function of checking whether any CRL is empty as required.
Prerequisites
You have the Query Certificate Settings and Manage Certificate Settings
permissions.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.

requirements.
Table 8-80 Functions and handling procedures
Function Handling Procedure
Certificat 1. Enable or disable Enable certificate alarms.
e alarm
– To enable this function, go to 2.
configur
– To disable the function, go to 5.
ations
NOTE
By default, the certificate alarm function is enabled. After the function is
disabled, the alarms "Certificate Is About to Expire" and "Certificate Has
Expired" will not be reported. For security purposes, you are advised to
enable this function. You can change the values of Check interval and
Alarm generation threshold to prevent frequent alarm reporting. To
prevent services from becoming unavailable due to certificate expiration,
you need to update certificates periodically (for example, every three
months).
2. Set Time unit and Check interval.
3. Enable or disable Major and Minor (the "Certificate Is About to
Expire" alarms) for identity certificates and trust certificates, and
set the time for alarm reporting.
NOTE
After certificate alarm reporting is enabled, Major and Minor cannot be
both disabled at the same time. When alarms of the two levels of severity
are both enabled, if the remaining validity period of a certificate reaches
the specified threshold (90 days or 2,160 hours by default, based on the
time unit) set for Minor, a minor alarm indicating that the certificate is
about to expire is reported. If the remaining validity period of a certificate
reaches the specified threshold (30 days or 720 hours by default, based on
the time unit) set for Major, the severity of the alarm indicating that the
certificate is about to expire changes from Minor to Major.
4. Enable or disable the CRL alarm reporting function and set the
CRL alarm threshold.
NOTE
The CRL alarm reporting function is enabled by default. After this function
is disabled, when a CRL is about to expire or has expired, no corresponding
alarm will be reported. For security purposes, you are advised to enable
this function. After this function is enabled, when the remaining validity
period of a CRL is less than or equal to its lifecycle (the difference
between Next Update Time and Effective From) multiplied by Alarm
generation threshold (the default value is 5%), a minor alarm indicating
that the CRL is about to expire will be generated.
5. Click Apply.
CRL Enable or disable the function of checking whether any CRL is empty
check based on site requirements. After the function is enabled, no alarm
configur is reported if a CRL is empty. Instead, the CRL is recorded in the
ation system log.
NOTE
For security purposes, you are advised to enable the CRL check.
