Step 4 After the configuration is complete, click Submit.
----End
Related Tasks
● Searching for the pre-shared password
Choose Protocol Configuration > Privacy CA Protocol. On the Pre-Shared
Password Configuration tab page, enter a pre-shared password name in the
search box and click to find the pre-shared password. The Certificate
Authority Service supports fuzzy search by pre-shared password name.

Password Configuration tab page, click Modify on the right of the pre-
shared password to change the pre-shared password.
● Deleting the pre-shared password
Choose Protocol Configuration > Privacy CA Protocol. On the Pre-Shared
Password Configuration tab page, click Delete on the right of the pre-shared
password to delete the pre-shared password.
8.5.3.10 Global Configuration
On the Global Configuration page, you can configure the CMP port status or
privacy CA protocol port status and TLS certificate.
8.5.3.10.1 Configuring a Port
On the Port Management page, you can view the current port status and
manually enable or disable the HTTP port, TLS one-way authentication port, TLS
two-way authentication port or privacy CA protocol port.
Prerequisites
You have the permission to view the port configuration, port status configuration,
and port protocol configuration.
Context
● In the multi-tenant deployment scenario, you need to contact the system
administrator to configure a port.
● For details about the port description, see Table 8-96.

Protoc Po Description Value
ol rt
CMP 26 The port protocol can be configured as Default value:
80 follows: Auth peer via

Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Authority Service from the main menu.
Step 2 Choose Global Configuration > Port Management from the navigation tree on
the left.
Step 3 Enable or disable a port as required.
● Click Enable to enable the port.
● Click Disable to disable the port. After the port is disabled and the service is
restarted, the certificate cannot be applied for through the port. Exercise
caution when performing this operation.
NO TE
● Actual status indicates the real status of the port on the CA server. If Enable is
displayed, the port is enabled and can be used to apply for a certificate. If Disable is
displayed, the port is disabled and cannot be used to apply for a certificate.
● Configured status indicates the required status of the port. If Enable is displayed, the
port needs to be enabled. If Disable is displayed, the port needs to be disabled. After
the service is restarted, the system port of the CA server is enabled or disabled based on

● In the displayed dialog box, click Restart Now, and then click OK to make the
port configuration take effect.
● In the displayed dialog box, click Restart Later. You can then restart the
service on the Global Configuration > Service Management page.
NO TE
● The service is unavailable during the restart. Wait for several minutes and perform other
operations after the restart is complete.
● On the Global Configuration > Service Management page, you can view the service
status. If the service status changes from Restarting to Running, the service is
restarted.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
Restarting the service
After enabling or disabling a port, if you click Restart Later in the dialog box that
is displayed, you need to choose Global Configuration > Service Management to
restart the service.
NO TE
After enabling the TLS one-way or two-way authentication port, you need to configure the
corresponding TLS certificate and restart the service for the port configuration to take
effect.
Related Tasks
● Querying the port status
On the Global Configuration > Port Management page, you can view the
current status of a port in the Actual Status column.
● Configure the port protocol
On the Global Configuration > Port Management page, click Configure
Protocol on the right of the port, and configure the protocol used by the port
as required.
NO TE
● Only ports 26801, 26802, and 26803 can be configured with a protocol.
● When Protocol is set to No auth peer via HTTPS or Auth peer via HTTPS, TLSv1.2
and TLSv1.3 are selected by default and cannot be deselected.
● When port has been associated with CMP configurations for which the function of
verifying expired certificate whitelists has been enabled. Therefore, the port
protocol cannot be changed to HTTPS. Choose Protocol Configuration > CMP. On
the Protocol Configuration tab page, click Modify in the Operation column of
the corresponding CMP configuration, and set Enable expired certificate whitelist
to No.

the TLS certificate, view the TLS configuration, and delete the TLS certificate.
Context
● TLS is designed to ensure data confidentiality and integrity between two
communication application programs.

administrator to configure the TLS certificate.
Procedure

the left.

must be updated accordingly. Otherwise, the TLS connection between the

▪

certificate.

TLS configuration take effect.

service on the Global Configuration > Service Management page.
NO TE
● The service is unavailable during the restart. Wait for several minutes and perform other
operations after the restart is complete.
● On the Global Configuration > Service Management page, you can view the service
status. If the service status changes from Restarting to Running, the service is
restarted.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
Restarting the service
After uploading or deleting the TLS certificate, if you click Restart Later in the
dialog box that is displayed, you need to choose Global Configuration > Service
Management to restart the service.
Related Tasks
● Viewing a TLS certificate
On the Global Configuration > TLS Configuration page, select Trust
certificate chain or Identity certificate from the drop-down list box to view
the certificate list of the corresponding type. Click the SN of the TLS

On the Global Configuration > TLS Configuration page, click Delete on the
right of the TLS certificate to delete the TLS certificate.
● Importing a CRL
On the Global Configuration > TLS Configuration page, click Import CRL on
the right of the TLS certificate to upload the CRL of the trusted certificate
chain for checking whether the peer server certificate is revoked.
NO TE
The CRL file to be uploaded must be in .crl or .pem format and the file size cannot
exceed 2 MB.
● Uploading a TLS certificate chain
On the Global Configuration > TLS Configuration page, click Upload
Certificate Chain on the right of the TLS certificate to upload the certificate
chain file. You can perform this operation only for TLS identity certificates that
are not configured with a certificate chain.
NO TE
If the key algorithm of the uploaded certificate public key is RSA with a 1024-bit key
size, the certificate file has security risks. You are advised to upload a secure certificate
file. If the key algorithm of the uploaded certificate public key is RSA with a 2048-bit
key size, the certificate file is of medium security. You need to confirm the operation in
the Information dialog box. You are advised to upload a secure certificate file.
8.5.3.10.3 Job Management
You can configure the Clearing expired certificates job to manage expired
certificates.
Context
● By default, the system does not start the Clearing expired certificates job.
You can start such a job as required and set the expiration days so that
expired certificates can be automatically deleted.
● If the number of certificates issued by the Certificate Authority Service
exceeds 80% of the maximum number supported by the Certificate Authority
Service, all expired certificates are automatically deleted.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Authority Service from the main menu.
Step 2 Choose Global Configuration > Job Management from the navigation tree on
the left.
Step 3 Click Policy Configuration and select whether to start the Clearing expired
certificates job based on actual requirements.
● If you select Yes for Start job, enter 7 to 365 days for Delete expiration days.
The default value 90 indicates that the system automatically deletes

NO TE
After the job is started, certificates that have expired for a certain number of days are
deleted every day. The certificates cannot be restored after being deleted.
● If you select No for Start job, the system does not automatically delete
expired certificates.
Step 4 Click Submit.
Step 5 Click OK.
----End
8.5.3.10.4 Restarting the Service
After configuring a port or TLS certificate, you can restart the corresponding
service for the configuration to take effect.
Prerequisites
You have the permission to view services, restart CA services, and restart access
services.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Authority Service from the main menu.
Step 2 Choose Global Configuration > Service Management from the navigation tree
on the left.
Step 3 Click Restart Service. During the restart, the service is unavailable.
Step 4 In the displayed dialog box, click OK to restart the service.
Step 5 In the displayed dialog box, click OK again. Wait for several minutes and perform
other operations after the restart is complete.
----End
8.5.3.10.5 Function Management
You can enable the end entity certificate application function for applying for end
entity certificates.
Prerequisites
You have the permission to modify and view functions.
Context
By default, the end entity certificate application function status is Disabled. You
can enable the end entity certificate application function as required.
Procedure

Step 3 Click Enable.
Step 4 In the displayed dialog box, click OK.
NO TE
● End entity certificate application enabled: On the Certificate Application > Certificate
Application page, the Apply by Basic Info tab is displayed. On the Apply by File
Upload tab page, you can select an end entity certificate profile.
● End entity certificate application disabled: On the Certificate Application > Certificate
Application page, the Apply by Basic Info tab is not displayed. On the Apply by File
Upload tab page, you cannot select an end entity certificate profile.
----End
8.5.3.11 Managing HSMs
On the HSM Management page, you can add, delete, modify, or view Hardware
Security Modules (HSMs) or HSM models.
8.5.3.11.1 Configuring the HSM Model
After the HSM model is configured, HSM models can be adapted to, implementing
flexible HSM interconnection.
Prerequisites
You have the permission to add, modify, view, copy and delete HSM models.
Context
● The HSM model is a group of rules and settings for HSM adaptation. It is an
important part of the HSM.
● The preset HSM model refers to the HSM model built in the system and
cannot be deleted. For details about the parameters, see Table 8-99. The
following table describes parameters of the preset HSM model. Set these
parameters based on the site requirements.
Table 8-99 Preset HSM model parameters

Vendor JAR Packages

Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Authority Service from the main menu.
Step 2 Choose HSM Management > HSM Model Management from the navigation

non-preset HSM model can be added. For details about the parameters, see Table
8-100.

Related Tasks
● Check the HSM model
On the HSM Model Management page, click the vendor of an HSM to view
details about the HSM. Including the Device vendor, Device model, and
Provider name.
● Change the HSM model
On the HSM Model Management page, click Modify in the Operation
column of an HSM model to change its Provider name, Provider package
name path and Associated JAR package signature certificate chain.
NO TE
For preset HSM models, only the Associated JAR package signature certificate chain
can be modified.
● Copy the HSM model
On the HSM Model Management page, click Copy in the Operation column
of the HSM model, In the displayed Copy the HSM model dialog box, enter
the Device model and click Submit. You can create the HSM model of which
the Device vendor, Provider name, Provider package name path, and
Associated JAR package signature certificate chain are the same as those
of the selected HSM model.
NO TE
● The preset and copied HSM models can be copied. Third-party HSM models cannot
be copied.
● A maximum of 16 HSM models are supported.

column of an HSM model to delete it. The deleted HSM model cannot be
used to create HSMs.
NO TE
An HSM model associated with an HSM instance cannot be deleted.
8.5.3.11.2 Configuring the HSM
By configuring the HSM, the certificate key will not appear in plaintext outside the
device at any time, implementing more secure key management.
Prerequisite
● You have the permission to add, modify, view, and delete HSMs.
● You have obtained the configuration file and associated JAR packages of the
SANSEC or TASS HSM. For details, see the following table.
Table 8-101 HSM details
HSM Configuration files Associate JAR packages
vendor
SANSEC swsds.ini swxajce_v5.3.2.10-r.jar,
crypto_v5.3.2.10-r.jar or
swxajce_v5.3.3.24.jar
TASS cacipher.ini TassEHSMApi-
v2.1.22-20210818.01.jar
Context
Generally, CA key encryption/decryption adopts an industry-recognized secure
encryption/decryption algorithm and uses a software-generated encryption/
decryption key. However, this type of software-based protection method is not
secure and may occupy some host resources or lead to slow processing. To tackle
this issue, the HSM can be used to manage keys more securely, making sure that
keys are not displayed in plaintext outside the device at any time.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Authority Service from the main menu.
Step 2 Choose HSM Management > HSM Management from the navigation pane.
Step 3 Click New and set required parameters. For detailed parameter descriptions, see
Table 8-102.

Paramete Description Value

9
FAQs
This section describes the common faults of the NetEco client, possible causes of
the faults, and troubleshooting methods.
9.1 How Do I Resolve the Nesting Problems of the
NetEco Page after Clearing the Cache from Firefox?
Symptom
1. On Firefox, log in to NetEco. Choose Tools > Clear Recent History from the
toolbar.
2. When opening a window in the NetEco client, the user is prompted to log in
again. For example, Choose System > System Management > Log
Management to open the Log Management window. the user is prompted
to log in again.
Possible Causes
The user login information is cleared as cache in the browser is cleared. Therefore,
NetEco cannot read user login information.
Procedure
Step 1 Click Refresh in the browser toolbar, or restart the browser.
Step 2 Log in to NetEco again.
----End

9.2 How Do I Process the Communication Between
NMS And Device Is Abnormal Alarm?
Symptom
A Communication Between NMS And Device Is Abnormal alarm is displayed in
the Current Alarms list.
Possible Causes
● The network cable is loose or damaged.
● The NetEco or the device is faulty.
● The handshake between the NetEco and the device times out.
Procedure
Step 1 Observe the alarm generation regularity and troubleshoot the faults, as listed in
the following table.

9.3 What Do I Do When the Number of Queried Data
Supplementary Collection or Report Query Tasks Is
Different from the Statistical Value
This section describes the symptom, causes, and troubleshooting method of the
fault that the number of queried data supplementary collection or report query
tasks is different from the statistical value.
Symptom
The number of queried data supplementary collection or report query tasks is
different from the statistical value.
Causes
● After data supplementary collection tasks are executed, the corresponding
task records are deleted.
● The system automatically clears the expired data.
Troubleshooting
1. Collect statistics on the data supplementary collection or report query tasks
again.
9.4 How Do I Solve the Problem that the Configuration
Management Window Is Not Fully Displayed
Symptom
When creating sites in the Configuration Management window, users need to
drag scroll bars to find the site component icons, which affects user experience.
Possible Causes
Resolution of the monitor is set improperly.
Procedure
Step 1 Set the resolution of the monitor to 1920*1080 or higher.
----End

9.5 How Do I Solve the Problem of Empty Data in
NetEco Reports?
Symptom
Some fields in a record on the NetEco report page are empty. Specifically, the data
of a certain day, week, month, or year is missing on the report page.
Possible Causes
● Report summarization mechanism
– Daily report data is collected at 02:00 every day. After the data is
summarized, you can query the report data.
– The weekly, monthly, and yearly report data is collected after the week,
month, and year. After the data is summarized, you can query the report
data.
● Possible causes for some empty fields in a record on the NetEco report page
– The component with missing data records is not installed at the site.
– The data records of the corresponding component in the time range are
missing in the historical data.
– The counter values of some time points are empty, and the data reported
by the monitoring module is invalid.
● Possible causes for missing daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly data on the
NetEco report page
– The query time is earlier than the site deployment time.
– The data records of the corresponding component in the time range are
missing in the site historical data.
● If the remaining fuel amount or refueling reminder report is empty, the device
may not report data or the alarm threshold may not be reached.
Procedure
1. On the main menu of the WebUI, choose Device Management >
Configuration > Planning Configuration and check whether the component
is displayed.
– If yes, go to 2.
– If no, check whether the component is installed on the site.
▪
If yes, add the component instance to the settings. On the next day,
check whether the report contains data records.
▪
If no, it is normal that no data is displayed.
2. On the main menu of the WebUI, choose Device Management >
Performance > Sync Historical Data Check whether data records of the site
component are available in the specific time range.

re-collect the data of the corresponding time range.
3. Check whether the counter value at the corresponding time point is empty.
– If yes, the data reported by the site is invalid. In this case, contact
technical support.
– If no, go to 4.
4. Manually aggregate the report data.
a. On the main menu of the WebUI, choose Device Management > Report
> Report Management.
b. Click Custom Summary in the lower left corner, click Specify Objects
Summary or Specify Report Summary, and set parameters as prompted.
c. If Task Status is Finish in the Operation column on the Custom
Summary page, choose Device Management > Report > Report
Management to check whether the report data has been restored. If the
data is still missing, contact technical support.
9.6 How Can I Set Pop-Up Windows for Firefox Mozilla?
Symptom
When users use Firefox Mozilla to log in to the NetEco to configure parameters,
the new parameter configuration page is blocked.
Possible Cause
The NetEco server is not configured to allow pop-up windows.
Procedure
NO TE
Operations on the browser may vary depending on browser versions but are similar to the
examples in the following steps. You are advised to perform the operations based on actual
situations.
1. Click in the upper right corner of Firefox Mozilla and choose Options.
2. In the Options page, click Content.
3. In the Content dialog box, select Block pop-up windows and click
Exceptions.
4. In the Allowed Sites - Pop-ups dialog box, enter https://IP address of the
server:31943 in the Address of website text box, click Allow , click Close .
5. In the Options tab page click OK.

9.7 How Can I Set Pop-Up Windows for Chrome
browser?
Symptom
When users use Chrome browser to log in to the NetEco to configure parameters,
the new parameter configuration page is blocked.
Possible Cause
The NetEco server is not configured to allow pop-up windows.
Procedure
NO TE
Operations on the browser may vary depending on browser versions but are similar to the
examples in the following steps. You are advised to perform the operations based on actual
situations.
1. Click in the upper right corner of Chrome browser and choose Settings.
2. In the Settings page, click Content settings in the Privacy area.
3. In the Content settings dialog box, select Do not allow any site to show
pop-ups (recommended) and click Manage exceptions in the Pop-ups area.
4. In the Pop-up exceptions dialog box, enter https://IP address of the
server:31943 in the Hostname pattern text box. Select Allow from the
Behavior drop-down list and click Done till the Content settings dialog box
is closed.
5. Close the Settings tab page.

9.9 How Do I Handle Device Access Exceptions?
Symptom
When the SMU is connected to the NetEco , the message "Incorrect random code"
is displayed on the access device page, as shown in the following figure.
Possible Causes

NO TE
The default username for communication between the NetEco and devices is emscomm,
and the default authentication password is Modifyme_123.
Solution
1. Logging In to the WebUI.
a. Set the laptop IP address to be in the same network segment as the SMU
IP address.
For example, if the SMU has an IP address of 192.168.0.10, a subnet
mask of 255.255.255.0, and a default gateway of 192.168.0.1, set the IP
address to 192.168.0.11, subnet mask to 255.255.255.0, and default
gateway to 192.168.0.1 on the PC.
b. Enter https://monitoring IP address (such as https://192.168.0.10) in the
address box of the browser, and then press Enter to enter the WebUI
login page.
NO TE
● Log in as the admin or engineer user. The preset password is Changeme.
● Change the preset password upon your first login to ensure the system
security.
2. Choose System Settings > NetEco.
Set Reathentication Pre-shared Key for communication between the device
WebUI and NetEco to the default password Modifyme_123.

3. Log in to the NetEco and reconnect the device.
9.10 How Do I Enable an In-Band Channel on MAE?
If the in-band channel is disabled by default for a BBU (the BBU managed by
iMaster MAE V100R020C10SPC210), you need to enable the in-band channel on
MAE when connecting the BBU to the NetEco. This section describes how to
enable an in-band channel on MAE.
Procedure
Step 1 In the browser address bar, enter https://IP address of the MAE:31943, and then
press Enter.
Step 2 Enter the User Name, Password, and click Login.
Step 3 Enter Main Topology in the search box and click .
Step 4 On the Main Topology page, right-click the wireless site for which the in-band
channel needs to be enabled, and choose MML from the shortcut menu.

Step 5 In the command editing area, enter MOD OP , and then press Enter.
Step 6 Enter emscommneteco ifor the operation name, select Enable from the User
status, and click Execute.
Step 7 Check whether a message indicating operation success is displayed in the
command output.
----End
9.11 How Do I Enable an In-Band Channel on BBU?
If the in-band channel is disabled by default for a BBU (V100R016C10 and later
versions), you need to enable the in-band channel on BBU when connecting the

Step 1 In the browser address bar, enter https://IP address of the BBU, and then press
Enter.
Step 2 Enter the User Name, Password, and click Login.
Step 3 On the main page, choose MML.
Step 4 On the MML tab page, enter SET OPSW:SWOP=ON;SET
OPLOCK:LOCKST=OFF;MOD OP:OP="emscommneteco",STATUS=ENABLE in the
command line. Click Exec.
Step 5 Check whether a message indicating operation success is displayed in the
command output.
----End

9.13 What Do I Do to Avoid the Logout When No
Operation Is Performed on the NetEco Page for a Long
Time?
When users use browser to visit the NetEco, avoid the Logout When No Operation
Is Performed on the NetEco Page for a Long Time.
Problem Description
To prevent other users from performing unauthorized operations, the NetEco
allows you to set related idle parameters. If you do not perform operations on the
NetEco page for a long time, you will automatically log out of the client and the
current page is switched back to the login page. In this case, you need to log in to
the client again. This is not friendly for some special application scenarios, such as
presentation or large-screen display.
Procedure
1. Choose System > System Management > Personal Settings.
2. In the navigation tree on the left, choose Personal Settings > Modify
Personal Info.
3. On the Modify Personal Info page, modify the property of Auto-logout if no
activity within .
4. Click Apply.
NO TE
To prevent other users from performing unauthorized operations during the absence of a
login user, you are advised to enable the Auto-logout if no activity within function after
the special application scenarios are finished.

9.15 How Do I Rectify a NetEco-device Intermittent
Disconnection Alarm?
This section describes how to rectify an intermittent disconnection alarm between
the NetEco and an device.
Symptom
Intermittent disconnection occurs due to unstable links between the NetEco and
devices, triggering disconnection alarms.

The preset duration for alarm masking rules is too short, leading to the fact that
lots of disconnection alarms are reported.
Procedure
1. Choose Device Management > Alarm Management > Alarm Settings.
2. In the navigation tree on the left, choose Masking Rule.
3. Create an alarm masking rule, set the alarm retention time, and mask
intermittent disconnection alarms.
a. Click + to create a masking rule for intermittent disconnection alarms.
b. Select Custom alarm sources in the alarm source area and add
communication alarms to be masked in Add Alarm Sources.
c. Set Duration (s) to ≤ 600 in Advanced Setting.
This is to mask disconnection alarms that are automatically cleared
within 10 min after their generation.
d. Click OK to save the alarm masking rule.
9.16 How Do I Install the Device Driver of the
Fingerprint Reader and Card Issuer?
Fingerprint and card number information can be entered on NetEco only after
installing the device driver of the fingerprint reader and card issuer.
Procedure
● Use the CD-ROM delivered with the fingerprint reader and card issuer to
install the device driver.
● Download the device driver of the fingerprint reader and card issuer from the
official website of Newabel. The download method is as follows:
a. Log in to the official website of Newabel (https://www.newabel.com/
Download/1.html).
User Name: Neteco
Password: Neteco123
b. Choose Service > Download > Software, download Fingerprint and
asset label issuer Driver, and install the driver.
----End
Result
After the driver is installed, perform the following steps to view the serial port
number on Windows 10:
1. On the system desktop of the PC, right-click the This PC icon and choose
Properties from the pop-up menu.

serial port number connected to the device.
9.17 How Do I Solve the Problem that the GIS Page Is
Blank When I Open the GIS Page Using Firefox?
Symptom
When a user logs in to the GIS page of the NetEco client using the Firefox browser,
the map is blank and cannot be loaded.
Possible Causes
The security level of the Firefox browser is high, and the third-party map service
page is blocked.
Procedure
Step 1 Enter about:config in the address box and click I understand the risk.
Step 2 Search for security.enterprise_roots.enabled.
Step 3 Click and set the value to true.
Step 4 Restart the Firefox browser.
----End
9.18 How Do I Solve the Problem that the View Page Is
Blank?
Symptom
After changing the IP address of a network device, a user finds that the IP address
conflicts with the NetEco IP address. The user then changes the device IP address
to another IP address. The NetEco page is blank.
Possible Causes
The network is abnormal due to IP address conflicts. The NetEco application
services cannot connect to the database. As a result, the services cannot be started
properly, and the NetEco client cannot access data.
Procedure
1. Log in to the PowerEcho.

9.19 How to Log In To the Server in SSH Mode?
This section describes how to log in to the server in SSH mode. For the purpose of
security, log in to the server in SSH mode after the operating system is installed.
You are advised to use the PuTTY tool in SSH mode.
Prerequisites
● The operating system is installed.
● The PC and the maintenance Ethernet port of the server communicate
normally.
● You have downloaded the PuTTY tool of the latest version to the local PC
from https://www.putty.org.
NO TE
If the third-party software fails to be accessed, the possible cause is that the software
version is too early. Log in to the official website of the third-party software to
download the latest version. If the software version is the latest, contact technical
support.
Procedure
Step 1 Decompress PuTTY.zip, and then double-click PuTTY.exe to start PuTTY.
Step 2 (Optional) To record operation logs, choose Session > Logging in the left pane,
select Printable output in the Session logging area, and then set the directory for
saving the operation logs in the Log file name area. See Figure 9-3.

Step 3 Choose Session in the left pane, then enter IP address of service network port in
the Host Name (or IP address) area, select SSH in the Connection type, select
Only on clean exit in the Close window on exit, and then click Open.See Figure
9-4.

If the window shown in Figure 9-5 is displayed, check whether the SRN of the
server is the same as that in the PuTTY Security Alert dialog box. If they are the
same, click Yes. Otherwise, click Cancel.
NO TE
To log in to the NetEco server using the KVM or Remote Management Port, you need to
run the following commands as user root to obtain the serial number of the server:
#cd /etc/ssh
#ssh-keygen -l -f ssh_host_rsa_key

Step 4 When the following information is displayed, type the user name and press Enter.
login as: user
Step 5 When the following information is displayed, type the user password and press
Enter.
password:
When the Last login: Mon Aug 11 14:47:55 2014 from 10.66.101.113 prompt is
displayed, you have logged in to the server.
----End
9.20 How Do I Use FileZilla to Transfer Files?
FileZilla is a dedicated file transfer tool that supports both FTP-based transfer and
resumable download. Therefore, you are advised to use FileZilla to transfer files.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the IP address of the destination server.
● The communication between the PC and the destination server is normal.
● You have obtained the file to be uploaded to the destination server and saved
the file to the PC.
● You have obtained the latest version of FileZilla.zip tool package at https://

NO TE
If the third-party software fails to be accessed, the possible cause is that the software
version is too early. Log in to the official website of the third-party software to
download the latest version. If the software version is the latest, contact technical
support.
● You have obtained the account for logging in to the destination server.
Procedure
Step 1 Double click filezilla.exe to start the FileZilla.
Step 2 In the FileZilla window, configure parameters by referring to Table 9-4.
Table 9-4 Parameters
Parameter Description
Host Enter the IP address of the destination server.
User Enter the user name and password of the server. The user has the
permission to access the destination directory.
Password
Port 22
Port 22 is the default port for SFTP.
Step 3 Click Quickconnect.
NO TE
● Ignore the dialog box displayed during the connection and click OK.
● If the connection is successful, directories on the destination server are displayed in the
Remote Site area.
Step 4 In the Remote site area, set the destination directory on the destination server.
For example, /opt.
After you set the directory, all files in the directory are displayed under the
Remote site area.
Step 5 In the Local site area, set the directory of the to-be-uploaded file on the PC. For
example, D:\FILE.
After you set the directory, all files in the directory are displayed under the Local
site area.
Step 6 Perform the following operations as required:
If... Then...
Uploads files In the Local site area, right-click the file to be uploaded
and choose Upload from the shortcut menu.

If... Then...
Downloads files In the Remote site area, right-click the file to be
downloaded and choose Download from the shortcut
menu.
NO TE
If the upload or download fails, click the Failed transfers tab in the lower left area of the
FileZilla window. Then right-click the file that fails to be transferred and choose Reset and
requeue selected files from the shortcut menu to resume the file transfer.
----End

9.23 Why the Video Freezes When the PTZ Control
Function Is Used?
Possible Causes
The camera model does not support the PTZ control function. Contact technical
support to rectify the fault.
9.24 Why Cannot I Hear the Alarm Sound on the
Current Alarms Page Using a Browser?
Question
Why Cannot I Hear the Alarm Sound on the Current Alarms Page Using a
Browser?
Answer
By default, the current browser version does not support the autoplay function.
The video or audio is not automatically played unless otherwise specified by users.
You can use either of the following methods to resolve this issue:
● The user interacts with the web page. For example, if the user clicks anywhere
on the page or accesses the Current Alarms page from the main menu again,
the browser considers that the user has interacted with the web page.
● The user adds the current website to the autoplay whitelist.
NO TE
The settings vary with browser versions. You need to select the path based on the
actual situation.
– Google Chrome
i. In the upper right corner of the browser, choose > Settings.
ii. Choose Privacy and security > Site settings. On the Site settings
page, click Sound.
iii. On the Sound page, set Default behavior to Sites can play sound.
iv. Click Add on the right of Allowed to play sound.
v. Enter https://NetEco IP address:31943 in the address box and click
Add.

i. In the upper right corner of the browser, choose > Settings.
ii. On the Settings tab page, choose Privacy & Security > Permissions
> Autoplay. Click the Settings button on the right of Autoplay.
iii. In the dialog box that is displayed, change Default for all websites
to Allow Audio and Video.
iv. Click Save Changes to save the modification.
– Microsoft Edge
i. In the upper right corner of the browser, choose > Settings.
ii. On the Settings tab page, select Cookies and site permissions.
iii. In the Cookies and site permissions page, click Media autoplay in
the Site permissions area.
iv. On the Media autoplay page, set Control if audio and video play
automatically on sites to Allow.
v. In the Allow area, click Add.
vi. In the dialog box that is displayed, enter https://NetEco IP
address:31943 and click Add.
9.25 How Do I Enable the MPPT Feature?
Question
In the weak mains scenario, if batteries are charged at a high rate, for example,
the AC input voltage is lower than the PSU undervoltage protection threshold, the
power supply will fail. After the MPPT function is enabled, the system
automatically tracks the mains capability and supplies power to loads and fully
charges batteries based on the maximum mains capability. When the load
fluctuates severely, the system does not power off.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > License Management.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Allocate License.
Step 3 Click Manual Allocate in the Operation column of the iGrid Maximum Power
Point Tracking LWM5ASQ00 row.
Step 4 Select a site and click Activate.
----End
9.26 How Do I Enable Three-Phase Dynamic Balance?
Answer
If the mains AC input is low, the AC three-phase currents are unbalanced. As a
result, the AC circuit breaker may trip when batteries are being charged. Enabling

from tripping and affects power supply.
Procedure
1. The software and hardware versions must meet the following requirements:
Table 9-7 Software/Hardware version requirements
Software/ Version Requirement
Hardware
Monitoring SMU02C or later
hardware
version
Monitoring iSitePower-ICT V100R024C10 or later
software
version
2. Enable the three-phase dynamic balance function in the AC input power
distribution device.
a. Choose Device Management > Device View > View.
b. In the navigation pane, select an AC input power distribution device
under the site.
c. Click the Configuration tab page.
d. In the Basic Parameters area, set Enable Three-Phase Dynamic
Balance to Enable.
9.27 How Do I Configure Automatic Collection on the
Data Collection and Analysis Page?
Question
To facilitate the collection of scheduled lithium battery collection tasks, how do I
configure automatic collection on the Data Collection and Analysis page?
Procedure
1. Obtain the NetEco configuration information and passwords of the sopuser
and ossuser users.
2. Modify the configuration information.
a. Use PuTTY to log in to the NetEco server as user sopuser in SSH mode.
b. Switch to the ossuser user.
su - ossuser
c. Navigate to the whitelist configuration file directory.

parameters:
sh config_AutoCollectionLog_Info.sh
isSupportedAuto current value is : 1
collectionCycle current value is : 5
select Action:
0:Config enable automatic collection (1: enabled; 0: disabled).
1:Configuring the Collection Period (3 to 30 Days)
2:Exit.
Action:
Check the current configuration information based on the command
output and modify the corresponding parameters.
▪
If Action is set to 0:
Enter the parameter value and press Enter (1: enabled; 0: disabled)
based on the following information:
Input the new value (1: enabled or 0: disabled)
▪
If Action is set to 1:
Enter the parameter value and press Enter (value range: 3–30) based
on the following information:
Input the new collectionCycle (between 3 and 30)
3. View the scheduled lithium battery collection tasks,.
a. Log in to NetEco using an account that has the Data Collection and
Analysis permission.
b. Choose Maintenance Management > Data Collection and Analysis >
Data Collection and Analysis.
c. In the right pane, view the scheduled lithium battery collection tasks.
9.28 Why Is the User-defined Device Name Reported by
the Monitoring System in a Language Different from
That of the NMS When a New Device Is Connected to
the NMS Through the Monitoring System?
Question
The language environment of the monitoring system is different from that of the
NMS.
Answer
The language environment of the monitoring system is different from that of the
NMS.
If the language environment of the monitoring system is Chinese and that of the
NMS is English, the user-defined device name is changed to a Chinese character
string in the monitoring system and reported to the NMS through the monitoring
system. The user-defined name on the NMS is also displayed as a Chinese

the same as that of the NMS. Otherwise, the language of the user-defined signals
on the device may be inconsistent.

10
Glossary
3
3GPP 3rd Generation Partnership Project.
A
AES
Advanced Encryption Standard
AIO
All In One.
Alarm Severity
Alarm severity identify fault effects on services. According to ITU-T's
suggestions, alarms are classified into four severity:
● Critical: indicates alarms that have affected services, and you need to
take corrective measures as soon as possible.
● Major: indicates alarms that have affected services, and you need to
take corrective measures in a timely manner.
● Minor: indicates alarms that have affected services, and you need to
take measures to eliminate potential risks.
● Warning: indicates alarms that may affect services, and you need to
take measures to diagnose and solve the problems if necessary.
Alarm Type Alarms are classified into various types based on causes, such as
communication alarms and device alarms.
Alarm (event) Filter
Alarm (event) filtering is a method used for managing alarms on the
server. By setting filter criteria, data that complies with the requirements is
displayed. Filter criteria do not affect the saving of alarms.
AMOS
Alarm monitor of outsourcing system.
Analog Input Remotely monitored equipment analog signal, such as air-conditioning,
temperature, and humidity.
Analog Output Analog signal for remotely controlling equipment, such as air temperature

ATS
Automatic Transfer Switch.
ATS-STS
Automatic Transfer Switch-static transfer switch.
ABB Asea Brown Boveri.
ACB Air Circuit Breaker.
B
BBOM Base bill of material.
BOM Bill of materials.
BT Beta test.
C
CA Certification Authority.
CBC DES Cipher Block Chaining- Data Encryption Standard.
CDMA A communication scheme that uses frequency expansion technology to
form different code sequences. When the CDMA scheme is used, subscribers
with different addresses can use different code sequences for multi-address
connection.
CIM Collection Integration Management System.
CLEI Common language equipment identification.
CPU Central processing unit.
D
DCiE
Data center infrastructure efficiency, DCiE equal to the energy consumption
of IT equipment energy consumption divided by the total data center
equipment multiplied by one hundred percent, DCiE is a percentage value,
the greater the value, the better.
Digital Input
Remotely monitored equipment switch signal, such as equipment status
and alarms.
Digital Output
Switch signal for remotely controlling equipment, such as signals for
powering on and powering off equipment.
E
ECC
Energy control center.
ESN
Equipment Serial Number.

Event Alarm
An event alarm presents a transient status during device operating. It
indicates that a pre-defined event occurs on the system, such as channel
congestion, and does not necessarily indicate a faulty status. Certain event
alarms are sent on a scheduled basis.
F
Fault Alarm
Fault alarms refer to alarms generated for hardware and software faults,
such as board faults, and abnormal main functions. After an alarm is
handled, the device will report a cleared alarm. The fault alarm severity is
higher than the event alarm severity.
FTP
File Transfer Protocol.
FTPS
File Transfer Protocol over SSL.
FM
Fault management.
G
GSM Global system for mobile communications.
I
IVS
Intelligent Video Surveillance.
IPv4 Internet Protocol version 4 (IPv4) . The current version of the Internet
Protocol (IP). IPv4 utilizes a 32bit address which is assigned to hosts. An
address belongs to one of five classes (A, B, C, D, or E) and is written as 4
octets separated by periods and may range from 0.0.0.0 through to
255.255.255.255. Each IPv4 address consists of a network number, an
optional subnetwork number, and a host number. The network and
subnetwork numbers together are used for routing, and the host number is
used to address an individual host within the network or subnetwork.
Intelligent Facility Intelligent facility operating system is a software system that centrally
Operating System manages basic hardware devices in the equipment room.
IVS Intelligent video surveillance system.
IMI Infrastructure Maintenance Index.
K
KPI Key performance indicator (KPI) indicates a monitoring indicator that
reflects key device operating status. KPI is an important component of
monitoring and measuring indicators. Users can monitor these indicators in

LDAP A network protocol based on TCP/IP, which allows access to a directory
system agent (DSA). It involves some reduced functionality from X.500
Directory Access Protocol (DAP) specifications.
LPT Link-state pass through.
LTE Long Term Evolution.
M
MAC Media Access Control.
Measurement Unit Measurement indicators are divided into several measurement units. Each
measurement unit has several measurement indicators related to system
functions.
Measurement A measurement object is a physical or logic entity, or a collection of
Object measured entities, such as a cabinet, module, or port.
MD5 Message digest algorithm.
MDF Model-Driven Framework.
Med Mediation.
Monitoring Defines monitoring data collection parameters for devices of various types,
Statistics Template which reduces visit times on the human-machine interface. You can use
values in the template directly when creating a collection task. Monitoring
data collection parameters include collection period and alarm threshold.
Management You can divide a large network into smaller networks to facilitate network
Domain management based on a certain principle. In the equipment room layout,
these small networks are called management domains.
Modbus A high-level protocol for industrial networks.
MCCB Molded Case Circuit Breaker.
O
OCX
Object Linking and Embedding Control eXtension.
OPEX
Operating expense.
Object Type
Monitored objects are classified into various object types, such as the whole
network, the network in a specific area, or devices of a certain type, in the
physical or logical dimension.
Object Instance
An object instance is a concrete monitored object, such as equipment room,
a cabinet, or a device.

PDM
Product Data Management.
PDU
Power Distribution Unit.
PE
Protective Earthing.
PUE
Power usage effectiveness (PUE) is an indicator that evaluates
infrastructure power efficiency, and is the ratio between the power
consumed by the infrastructure and the power consumed by IT loads. The
reference value is 2. If PUE is close to 1, it indicates high power efficiency.
PuTTy
PuTTY is a free and open source terminal emulator application which can
act as a client for the SSH, Telnet, rlogin, and raw TCP computing protocols.
R
RPDU Rack Power Distribution Unit.
RUL Remaining Useful Life
S
SEE
Site energy efficiency is the effective utilization ratio of the input energy such as
electricity, fuel, solar and others in telecom site or data center. It is the proportion
of the main equipment energy consumption in total site or data center input
energy.
SMTP
Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (SMTP) is a kind of Transmission Control
Protocol/Internet Protocol (TCP/IP) and is used for sending emails. It
stipulates interaction methods of two systems and the message format for
sending emails.
SNMP Simple Network Management Protocol(SNMP). An IETF protocol for
monitoring and managing systems and devices in a network. The data
being monitored and managed is defined by a MIB. The functions
supported by the protocol are the request and retrieval of data, the setting
or writing of data, and traps that signal the occurrence of events.
SOC State of charge.
SOH State of health.
SHA Secure hash algorithm.
SLA Service Level Agreement.

SPD Surge Protective Device.
SSL Secure Sockets Layer.
SSO Single Sign-On.
STS Static Transfer Switch.
T
TCP Transmission Control Protocol.
Threshold Alarm If an alarm threshold has been set on a device, the device generates an
alarm when the monitored value exceeds the threshold.
TLSv1 Transport Layer Security.
U
UPS Uninterruptible Power System.
V
VCN Video cloud node.
VPN Virtual Private Network.
